Thinking · Scrutinizing behavior tests on recent fixes.
The calibration checks out across six wins with turn counts ranging from 280 to 431, confirming the skill tier boundaries work as intended—Professional players stay under 380 turns, Apprentice under 500, and Holmesian demands real efficiency at 200 or less. I'm now running behavior tests on today's fixes, specifically checking the sprite column adjustment and verifying the panel movement constraints work correctly in both directions.

Six for six, 280–431 turns. Calibration verified against real playthroughs. Now the regression tests for today's four fixes:

bash_toolVerify sprite offset, top-only panel, shortcuts, and calibrated ranks
bash
cd /home/claude/web && node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 4242, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(flat().slice(-300)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const g = D.house().grid;

// 1. sprite column: player renders at col*2 - 1
key("ArrowUp");
const row = plain().split("\n")[state.row + 2];
assert(row.indexOf("☻") === state.col * 2 - 1, "player sprite at col*2-1 (doorway-aligned)");

// 2. panel: down from the room above works; up from below does not
const [pr, pc] = world.panelCell;
assert(g[pr + 1][pc] === 2 && g[pr - 1][pc] > 0 && g[pr - 1][pc] !== 2,
       "panel candidates are top-wall only");
state.row = pr + 1; state.col = pc; state.roomId = 2;   // inside passage, below panel
key("ArrowUp");
assert(!(state.row === pr && state.col === pc), "panel refuses entry from below");
state.row = pr - 1; state.col = pc; state.roomId = g[pr - 1][pc];
key("ArrowDown");
assert(state.row === pr && state.col === pc && flat().includes("secret panel"),
       "panel admits from above (in-wall step + message)");
key("ArrowDown");
assert(g[state.row][state.col] === 2, "second Down enters the passageway");

// 3. shortcuts
world.suspects[0].room = 2; state.roomId = 2;
type("q");
assert(flat().includes(world.suspects[0].first), "bare 'q' questions the guest here");
type("ex floor");
assert(/nearsighted|bloodstains/.test(flat()), "'ex floor' works");
type("a maude");
assert(flat().includes("haven't even gathered"), "'a <name>' = accuse (pre-gather message)");
type("g");
assert(world.gathered && flat().includes("baited breath"), "'g' = gather");
D.execute("restart");

// 4. rank ladder + calibrated clock
assert(world.deadline === 700, "deadline 700");
const R = [[150,"Holmesian?"],[200,"Holmesian"],[280,"Master"],[380,"Professional"],[500,"an Apprentice"],[650,"Novice"],[651,"Bumbling"]];
const rk = t => { for (const [m,n] of [[200,"Holmesian"],[280,"Master"],[380,"Professional"],[500,"an Apprentice"],[650,"Novice"]]) if (t<=m) return n; return "Bumbling"; };
// verify via a win at a known turn count
world.hasGlass = true;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
const wk = world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0];
type("ex " + wk); type("get " + wk);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
world.turn = 431;                              // a thorough-bot pace
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key);
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("rank as an Apprentice"),
       "431 turns -> 'an Apprentice' (grammar from the EXE's own 'n')");
console.log("\nALL FOUR FIXES VERIFIED");
EOF
Output
ok: player sprite at col*2-1 (doorway-aligned)
ok: panel candidates are top-wall only
ok: panel refuses entry from below
ok: panel admits from above (in-wall step + message)
ok: second Down enters the passageway
ok: bare 'q' questions the guest here
ok: 'ex floor' works
ok: 'a <name>' = accuse (pre-gather message)
ok: 'g' = gather
ok: deadline 700
ok: 431 turns -> 'an Apprentice' (grammar from the EXE's own 'n')

ALL FOUR FIXES VERIFIED
bash_toolUpdate README with calibration notes and repackage
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - << 'EOF'
r = open('web/README_PHASE2.md').read()
r = r.replace('''## Room-entry kills and calm corridors (M5.5 field notes)''',
'''## Calibration and the full rank ladder (M5.6 field notes)

The EXE's string table holds SIX ranks, not three: **Bumbling, Novice!,
Apprentice, Professional, Master, Holmesian** (the stray "n" in the
table is grammar — "you now rank as a**n** Apprentice"). Thresholds
are compiled-in, so ours were calibrated empirically: a pathfinding
player-bot that tours every room, examines floors, interviews guests,
finds glass and weapon, and closes the case measures **280–431 turns**
for a thorough solve with optimal walking. Humans path ~1.5× worse, so:
Holmesian ≤200, Master ≤280, Professional ≤380, Apprentice ≤500,
Novice ≤650, Bumbling beyond — with the deadline at 700 (suspicion at
turn ~520, stalking ~630), keeping a leisurely full solve conceivable
at the most relaxed rank, per the design brief. One bot run also
proved the mystery can live *behind the secret panel* (weapon in the
passageway), and that the only way out is still the blind corridor.

Other corrections this round: sprites now draw at column 2c−1, where
the BSAVE puts each cell's wall line, so the detective visually stands
IN doorways rather than one unit right of them; the secret panel is
entered only from the room above, pressing Down (as playtested against
the original — no entry from below); and SLEUTH.DOC's documented
abbreviations ("you can abbreviate QUESTION as Q and EXAMINE as EX")
are joined by remaster shortcuts G (gather) and A (accuse).

## Room-entry kills and calm corridors (M5.5 field notes)''')
open('web/README_PHASE2.md','w').write(r)
print("README updated")
EOF
cd web && cp sleuth_playable.html /mnt/user-data/outputs/ && zip -qo /mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m5.zip index.html engine.js data_shared.js data_v1.js data_v2.js gen_web_data.py README_PHASE2.md sleuth_playable.html && echo packaged
Output
README updated
packaged
present_filesPresenting file(s)...
json
{
  "filepaths": [
    "/mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_playable.html",
    "/mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m5.zip"
  ]
}
Output
[local_resource block]
[local_resource block]

All four items landed, plus a discovery that reshaped one of them:

1. Sprite alignment fixed. The BSAVE draws each cell's visuals across columns 2c−1..2c with the wall line on the left character; sprites were drawing at 2c, hence reading one unit right of every doorway. Player and guests now draw at 2c−1 — the detective stands in gaps rather than beside them.

2. Panel entry is top-only. Candidates are now exclusively wall cells with a room above and the passage below, entered by pressing Down — and entry from below is refused outright. The two-step (into the wall, message, then through) is unchanged. Both houses have valid top-wall candidates, so no fallback was needed.

3. Shortcuts. SLEUTH.DOC turns out to document its own: "you can abbreviate QUESTION as Q and EXAMINE as EX." Those two are original; G (gather) and A (accuse) are now added as remaster conveniences in the same spirit, all matched as exact first tokens so the substring parser can't misfire on them. (Same doc passage also confirmed "Personalized Sleuth" prompts for seven custom names at startup — the exact retyping your DESCR.V1 hack was invented to skip.)

4. Ranks and pacing — and here's the find: the EXE's string table holds six ranks, not three: Bumbling, Novice!, Apprentice, Professional, Master, Holmesian. The stray "n" in the table is grammar — "you now rank as an Apprentice," which the win message now honors. Thresholds are compiled-in and unreadable, so I calibrated empirically: a pathfinding player-bot that plays honestly — tours every room, examines floors, interviews guests, retrieves glass and weapon, gathers, accuses — measures 280–431 turns for a thorough solve with optimal walking. Scaling for human pathing (~1.5×), the ladder is now Holmesian ≤200, Master ≤280, Professional ≤380, Apprentice ≤500, Novice ≤650, Bumbling beyond, with the deadline at 700 (suspicion ~turn 520, stalking ~630) — so a leisurely full solve stays conceivable at the most relaxed rank, exactly per your brief. One bot run also proved the mystery can live behind the secret panel — weapon in the passageway, blind corridor the only way out — and the game remains winnable there.

When you do get around to DOSBox counts, the three numbers worth timing are the real deadline, the suspicion thresholds, and the NPC move rate — all now single named constants ready to receive ground truth. Otherwise, the remaster matches every original behavior you've reported, and M6 (custom houses — the JSON format documentation for your own layouts, casts, and weapons) remains the last piece whenever you want it.

2026-07-03 05:42
You

Nice. The end message also ends with Sleuth, so it's Apprentice Sleuth, Bumbling Sleuth, etc.

Let's move to milestone 6. How can we customise this? Also, can we try it with the second house that original Sleuth uses too? Some houses won't have secret passages and so on, so how do we account for that when customising layouts?

2026-07-03 05:50
Claude
Thinking · Thinking about customizing milestone 6 and adapting layouts for houses with varying architectural features.
So the rank suffix is "Sleuth" — that matches what I found in the executable earlier. I need to update the rank message to use the correct article based on whether it starts with a vowel, like "You now rank as a Bumbling Sleuth" or "as an Apprentice Sleuth." Next up is working on customization features. For House 2, I need to verify the full game loop runs properly on Donleavy's variant — the Tab switch works, but I haven't tested the solve-bot or player-bot end-to-end on V2 yet. The main concern is whether the pathfinding handles V2's layout correctly since it lacks the 32/33 staircase codes that House 1 has; the grid is fully connected though, so a standard BFS should work fine. I'll run the bot through several seeds on House 2 to confirm, and I need to add house-switching support so the bot can test both variants. For the customization system itself, I'm thinking through how to document and implement it — the engine is data-driven through window.SLEUTH_HOUSES, so a custom house needs a JS file with label, variant, rooms, grid, weapons, characters, anchors, landing actions, and screen data. The screen part is the tricky bit since it involves character sprites and palettes. For houses without BSAVE dumps, I'll add a fallback renderer that synthesizes the screen from the grid — drawing walls as block characters, stairs as rungs, and borders, keeping it simple and close to the original style. On handling missing features, I'm making the engine defensive by checking what each house actually has. For secret passages, instead of hardcoding room 2, I'll add an optional `house.secret` property that declares which room is the secret passage; if it's missing or no candidates exist, the panel logic and blind corridor just don't activate. Stairs are already conditional on codes 32/33 being present. Doors 34/35/36 work generically, and the room loop for weapons and alibis will adapt based on what rooms exist. For custom houses with different room counts, I'm deriving the guest rooms from the data itself — either from a declared `house.guestRooms` list or by scanning the weapons and room definitions. NPC locations become guest rooms plus the stairs room if it exists. Alibi generation and object scattering will respect these boundaries, and I'll validate that each guest room has at least one weapon available. Anchors fall back to the middle cell, spawn falls back through finding cell 36, then above 35, then the highest room's middle. I need to ensure the cast has at least 7 characters for a valid game. For the demo custom house, I'm building a small third house to showcase the pipeline — keeping it neutral and generic so the user can easily customize it to their Salt & Persimmons universe without me guessing their canon details wrong. Writing the authoring layer... I'm refining how the secret room gets computed — it should be the unreachable guest room with no door cells, derived from panel candidates. If no candidates exist, there's no panel and the blind corridor doesn't trigger. I'm also generalizing the westEnd calculation to use the panel's actual room position, and updating references like the blind trigger and panel entry text to use the computed secretRoomId instead of hardcoded room 2. For rendering, I need to ensure synthesizeScreen handles custom grid sizes while keeping the screen output at the fixed 80×25 dimensions, starting with the top border row. Now I'm working through the cell-to-screen rendering logic — mapping grid cells to screen columns where each cell occupies two character positions, with walls rendered as solid blocks on the left and conditionally on the right depending on whether adjacent cells are also walls, creating continuous wall runs while keeping interior vertical walls thin. For the scheme attributes, I'm using a uniform byte value across all rows to keep the palette consistent and simple. Now I'm designing the house specification format where authors define the map as a grid of characters, provide a legend mapping those characters to room IDs and door targets, and include metadata like room descriptions, NPCs, weapons, and character definitions — with validation ensuring the grid matches the legend and meets minimum requirements like having at least seven characters. For the demo cottage, I'm sketching a 40×17 ASCII layout with seven guest rooms arranged around a central hall and porch, making sure each room connects to the hall via a door and meets the occupancy and alibi constraints. I need to guard against crashes when landing actions are undefined by defaulting to an empty array, and I'm inventing neutral character names while making sure the grid is constructed programmatically in the data file. I'm mapping doors and rooms using character codes—letters A-G for rooms 1-7, H for the hall, P for the porch, digits for door connections, and X for the main exit—then validating the layout through the register function by iterating in Node until it's valid. For testing, I'll run a targeted playthrough on the new house (spawn, walk, question, examine, win via debug) and adapt the bot to work on the existing house by adding a house parameter and pressing Tab if needed, since both houses share the same guest room IDs. Now I'm refactoring the engine loops to use a generalized GUEST_ROOMS constant instead of hardcoded 1..11 ranges—this affects scatter logic, alibi generation, NPC locations (which include room 12 if stairs exist), object placement checks, ambush detection, and gathering logic. For the secret room feature, I need to identify which guest room is unreachable by computing a flood fill from spawn; if exactly one room has no door cells and isn't adjacent to anything, that's the secret room, and I'll validate that it has top-wall candidates for the panel or warn if it doesn't. The implementation plan is to create an `analyzeHouse()` function that runs at scatter start to compute GUEST_ROOMS, NPC_LOCATIONS, SECRET_ROOM, and panel candidates, consolidating all the room-graph setup logic. Then I'll execute the patch carefully: fix the rank suffix, apply the big generalization across the engine, update synthesizeScreen and registerSleuthHouse, add a demo house3 file, wire it into the web build, and run tests covering house3 loading, feature toggles, win conditions, and regression checks on V1/V2 bots.

"Apprentice Sleuth" — of course, and the EXE agrees: that stray . Sleuth. fragment at string 317 is the suffix. Milestone 6 is exactly the right frame for your questions, because "how do we handle houses without secret passages" is really "the engine must detect features from data rather than assume them." Plan: fix the rank suffix, generalize every hardcoded assumption (room count, stairs, secret room) into per-house analysis, add a screen synthesizer for houses without BSAVE art, build a registerSleuthHouse() authoring API with validation, prove it with a demo third house that has no passage and no stairs, and run the full player-bot on House 2. Big patch first:

bash_toolMilestone 6 engine: derived features, screen synthesis, authoring API
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - > /tmp/p.log 2>&1 << 'PATCH_EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
def rep(old, new):
    global src
    assert old in src, "NOT FOUND: " + old[:70]
    src = src.replace(old, new)

rep("SLEUTH remaster \u2014 engine.js \u2014 Phase 2, Milestone 5",
    "SLEUTH remaster \u2014 engine.js \u2014 Phase 2, Milestone 6")
rep('print(0, 1, "SLEUTH \\u00b7 milestone 5", 0x70);',
    'print(0, 1, "SLEUTH \\u00b7 milestone 6", 0x70);')

# ---- rank suffix: "... Sleuth." per playtest + the '. Sleuth.' string ----
rep('''  const rank = rankFor(world.turn);
  say(`The case is closed after ${world.turn} moves.  You now rank ` +
      `as ${rank.startsWith("an ") ? rank : "a " + rank}!`);''',
'''  const rank = rankFor(world.turn);
  say(`The case is closed after ${world.turn} moves.  You now rank ` +
      `as ${rank.startsWith("an ") ? rank : "a " + rank} Sleuth!`);''')

# ---- house analysis: derive features from data, assume nothing ----
rep('''/* Guests occupy exactly the 12 locations that have a seat and an''',
'''/* Per-house feature analysis. Custom houses may lack a secret room,
   stairs, or the full eleven rooms; everything below is DERIVED:
     GUEST_ROOMS   rooms that hold objects/guests/alibis (from the
                   weapon table's room ids)
     NPC_LOCATIONS guest rooms + the staircase, if the grid has one
     SECRET_ROOM   a guest room unreachable by ordinary walking (if
                   any) — it gets the panel and the blind corridor;
                   houses without one simply have neither. */

let GUEST_ROOMS = [], NPC_LOCATIONS = [], SECRET_ROOM = null;

function analyzeHouse() {
  const g = house().grid;
  GUEST_ROOMS = [...new Set(house().weapons.map((w) => w.room))].sort((a, b) => a - b);
  const hasStairs = !!findCell(12);
  NPC_LOCATIONS = GUEST_ROOMS.concat(hasStairs && !GUEST_ROOMS.includes(12) ? [12] : []);

  // which guest room, if any, is sealed? flood-fill from any door/open room
  const start = findCell(36) || findCell(35) || middleCellIn(g, GUEST_ROOMS[0]);
  const seen = new Set([start.join()]), q = [start];
  while (q.length) {
    const [r, c] = q.pop();
    for (const [dr, dc] of [[1,0],[-1,0],[0,1],[0,-1]]) {
      const nr = r + dr, nc = c + dc, k = nr + "," + nc;
      if (g[nr] && g[nr][nc] > 0 && !seen.has(k)) { seen.add(k); q.push([nr, nc]); }
    }
  }
  SECRET_ROOM = GUEST_ROOMS.find((rid) => {
    const cell = middleCellIn(g, rid);
    return cell && !seen.has(cell.join());
  }) ?? null;
}

function middleCellIn(g, rid) {
  const cells = [];
  for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++)
    for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++)
      if (g[r][c] === rid) cells.push([r, c]);
  return cells.length ? cells[Math.floor(cells.length / 2)] : null;
}

/* Guests occupy exactly the 12 locations that have a seat and an''')

rep('''const NPC_LOCATIONS = Array.from({ length: 12 }, (_, i) => i + 1);

// the player's location in occupancy terms (both flights = one stair)''',
'''// the player's location in occupancy terms (both flights = one stair)''')

# ---- scatter: fully data-driven ----
rep('''function scatter() {
  world.objects = {};
  const w = house().weapons;
  for (let room = 1; room <= 11; room++) {
    const candidates = w.filter((x) => x.room === room);
    const obj = pick(candidates);
    world.objects[room] = { ...obj, examined: false };
  }
  const roomIds = Array.from({ length: 11 }, (_, i) => i + 1);''',
'''function scatter() {
  analyzeHouse();
  world.objects = {};
  const w = house().weapons;
  for (const room of GUEST_ROOMS) {
    const candidates = w.filter((x) => x.room === room);
    world.objects[room] = { ...pick(candidates), examined: false };
  }
  const roomIds = [...GUEST_ROOMS];''')

rep('''  const spots = [...NPC_LOCATIONS];              // 6 guests, 12 seats,''',
'''  const spots = [...NPC_LOCATIONS];              // 6 guests, one per seat,''')

# ---- panel/west-end: keyed to the derived secret room ----
rep('''  // playtest-corrected: the panel is only entered from the room ABOVE
  // the passageway, pressing Down (the original never admits you from
  // below). Candidates: wall cells with a room overhead and the
  // passage underneath.
  const g = house().grid, cand = [];
  let west = null;
  for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++) {
    for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++) {
      if (g[r][c] === 2 && (!west || c < west[1] ||
          (c === west[1] && r < west[0]))) west = [r, c];
      if (g[r][c] !== 0) continue;
      const n = (rr, cc) => (g[rr] && g[rr][cc]) || 0;
      if (n(r + 1, c) === 2 && n(r - 1, c) > 0 && n(r - 1, c) <= 20 &&
          n(r - 1, c) !== 2) cand.push([r, c]);
    }
  }
  world.panelCell = cand.length ? pick(cand) : null;''',
'''  // playtest-corrected: the panel is only entered from the room ABOVE
  // the secret room, pressing Down. Houses with no sealed room get no
  // panel and no blind corridor — the features simply don't exist.
  const g = house().grid, cand = [];
  let west = null;
  if (SECRET_ROOM !== null) {
    for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++) {
      for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++) {
        if (g[r][c] === SECRET_ROOM && (!west || c < west[1] ||
            (c === west[1] && r < west[0]))) west = [r, c];
        if (g[r][c] !== 0) continue;
        const n = (rr, cc) => (g[rr] && g[rr][cc]) || 0;
        if (n(r + 1, c) === SECRET_ROOM && n(r - 1, c) > 0 &&
            n(r - 1, c) <= 20 && n(r - 1, c) !== SECRET_ROOM)
          cand.push([r, c]);
      }
    }
  }
  world.panelCell = cand.length ? pick(cand) : null;''')

rep('''      if (beyond === 2)
        say(house().rooms["2"].entry_description);''',
'''      if (beyond === SECRET_ROOM)
        say(house().rooms[String(SECRET_ROOM)].entry_description);''')
rep('''    if (cellAt(state.row, state.col) === 2 && world.westEnd &&''',
'''    if (cellAt(state.row, state.col) === SECRET_ROOM && world.westEnd &&''')
rep('''    if (beyond > 0 && beyond <= 20 && (beyond === 2 || here === 2)) {''',
'''    if (beyond > 0 && beyond <= 20 &&
        (beyond === SECRET_ROOM || here === SECRET_ROOM)) {''')
rep('''        c === world.panelCell[1] && dr === 1 &&
        cellAt(state.row, state.col) !== 2) {''',
'''        c === world.panelCell[1] && dr === 1 &&
        cellAt(state.row, state.col) !== SECRET_ROOM) {''')

# room-graph panel edges: also keyed to the secret room
rep('''      // secret panel: a 1-thick wall with the passageway (room 2) on
      // one side and another room on the other joins them
      if (g[r][c] === 0) {
        if (c > 0 && c + 1 < W &&
            (norm(g[r][c-1]) === 2) !== (norm(g[r][c+1]) === 2))
          connect(norm(g[r][c-1]), norm(g[r][c+1]));
        if (r > 0 && r + 1 < H &&
            (norm(g[r-1][c]) === 2) !== (norm(g[r+1][c]) === 2))
          connect(norm(g[r-1][c]), norm(g[r+1][c]));
      }''',
'''      // secret panel: a 1-thick wall with the secret room on one side
      // and another room on the other joins them (guests know the way)
      if (g[r][c] === 0 && SECRET_ROOM !== null) {
        const S = SECRET_ROOM;
        if (c > 0 && c + 1 < W &&
            (norm(g[r][c-1]) === S) !== (norm(g[r][c+1]) === S))
          connect(norm(g[r][c-1]), norm(g[r][c+1]));
        if (r > 0 && r + 1 < H &&
            (norm(g[r-1][c]) === S) !== (norm(g[r+1][c]) === S))
          connect(norm(g[r-1][c]), norm(g[r+1][c]));
      }''')

# ---- remaining 1..11 / 1..12 constants -> derived sets ----
rep('''const objectHere = () =>
  state.roomId >= 1 && state.roomId <= 11 ? world.objects[state.roomId] : null;''',
'''const objectHere = () =>
  GUEST_ROOMS.includes(state.roomId) ? world.objects[state.roomId] : null;''')
rep('''  if (!(newLoc >= 1 && newLoc <= 11) || newLoc === prevLoc) return false;''',
'''  if (!GUEST_ROOMS.includes(newLoc) || newLoc === prevLoc) return false;''')
rep('''    const g = house().grid, cells = [];
    for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++)
        if (g[r][c] >= 1 && g[r][c] <= 11) cells.push([r, c]);''',
'''    const g = house().grid, cells = [];
    for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++)
        if (GUEST_ROOMS.includes(g[r][c])) cells.push([r, c]);''')
rep('''  const loc = locOf(state.roomId);
  if (!(loc >= 1 && loc <= 12)) {''',
'''  const loc = locOf(state.roomId);
  if (!NPC_LOCATIONS.includes(loc)) {''')
rep('''    ? `${a.withName} and I spent the entire evening together in the ` +''',
'''    ? `${a.withName} and I spent the entire evening together in the ` +''')
rep('''  const roomsAvail = [];
  for (let r = 1; r <= 11; r++) if (r !== world.murderRoom) roomsAvail.push(r);''',
'''  const roomsAvail = GUEST_ROOMS.filter((r) => r !== world.murderRoom);''')
rep('''  const pool = (room && room.npc_actions && room.npc_actions.length)
    ? room.npc_actions.flat()
    : house().landing_actions.flat();
  return pool.length ? pick(pool) : "is standing around.";''',
'''  const pool = (room && room.npc_actions && room.npc_actions.length)
    ? room.npc_actions.flat()
    : (house().landing_actions || []).flat();
  return pool.length ? pick(pool) : "is standing around.";''')

# ---- screen synthesis for houses without BSAVE art ----
rep('''function drawHouse() {
  // Not a reconstruction: these are the character and attribute bytes
  // from the original BSAVE screen dumps, blitted the way SLEUTH.EXE
  // BLOADed them into video memory at B000h. The grid is only used
  // for game logic; what you SEE is 1983's own screen.
  const scr = house().screen;''',
'''/* Custom houses ship no BSAVE art, so we synthesise a screen from the
   grid in the original's visual dialect: thin walls (left char only,
   solid where wall runs continue), gaps for doors, rungs for stairs,
   a border row on top, and the outdoor strip undrawn. Cached on the
   house object after first build. */
function synthesizeScreen(grid) {
  const chars = ["\\u2588".repeat(79) + " "];
  for (let r = 0; r < 16; r++) {
    let row = "";
    for (let x = 0; x < 80; x++) {
      const c = Math.floor((x + 1) / 2);          // cell owning column x
      const v = c < 40 ? grid[r][c] : 0;
      if (v === 0) {
        const rightIsWall = c + 1 >= 40 || grid[r][c + 1] === 0;
        row += (x === 2 * c - 1 || x === 0) ? "\\u2588"
             : (rightIsWall ? "\\u2588" : " ");
      } else if (v === 12 || v === 13) row += "\\u2500";   // stair rungs
      else row += " ";
    }
    chars.push(row);
  }
  const palettes = {};
  for (const s of SCHEMES) {
    const attr = s.map.toString(16).padStart(2, "0");
    palettes[s.key] = chars.map((row) => attr.repeat(80));
  }
  return { chars, palettes };
}

function drawHouse() {
  // Original houses blit the BSAVE bytes SLEUTH.EXE BLOADed into video
  // memory; custom houses get a synthesised screen in the same style.
  if (!house().screen) house().screen = synthesizeScreen(house().grid);
  const scr = house().screen;''')

# ---- the authoring API ----
rep('''scatter();
spawn(false);''',
'''/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   11. CUSTOM HOUSES — registerSleuthHouse(spec)
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Authoring format (see HOUSES.md): an ASCII map plus a legend, rooms,
   a cast of seven, and weapons grouped by room. Everything optional is
   defaulted; everything structural is validated with a thrown Error,
   because a clear message now beats a silent haunting later. */

window.registerSleuthHouse = function (spec) {
  const fail = (m) => { throw new Error("registerSleuthHouse: " + m); };
  if (!spec.map || spec.map.length !== 17 ||
      spec.map.some((row) => row.length !== 40))
    fail("map must be 17 rows of exactly 40 characters");
  const grid = spec.map.map((row) => [...row].map((ch) => {
    if (!(ch in spec.legend)) fail(`map character '${ch}' missing from legend`);
    return spec.legend[ch];
  }));
  if (!spec.characters || spec.characters.length < 7)
    fail("need at least 7 characters (six suspects + one victim)");
  const guestRooms = [...new Set((spec.weapons || []).map((w) => w.room))];
  if (guestRooms.length < 6)
    fail("need weapons in at least 6 distinct rooms (one guest per room)");
  if (guestRooms.length < 4)
    fail("alibi generation needs at least 4 guest rooms");
  for (const rid of guestRooms) {
    if (!spec.rooms[rid]) fail(`room ${rid} holds weapons but has no entry in rooms{}`);
    if (!grid.some((row) => row.includes(rid))) fail(`room ${rid} does not appear on the map`);
  }
  // reachability: every guest room reachable, or exactly one sealed
  // room with a valid from-above panel wall
  const start = (() => {
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] === 36 || grid[r][c] === 35) return [r, c];
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] > 0) return [r, c];
  })();
  const seen = new Set([start.join()]), q = [start];
  while (q.length) {
    const [r, c] = q.pop();
    for (const [dr, dc] of [[1,0],[-1,0],[0,1],[0,-1]]) {
      const nr = r + dr, nc = c + dc, k = nr + "," + nc;
      if (grid[nr] && grid[nr][nc] > 0 && !seen.has(k)) { seen.add(k); q.push([nr, nc]); }
    }
  }
  const sealed = guestRooms.filter((rid) => {
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] === rid) return !seen.has(r + "," + c);
    return false;
  });
  if (sealed.length > 1) fail("more than one unreachable room: " + sealed.join(","));
  if (sealed.length === 1) {
    const S = sealed[0];
    const ok = grid.some((row, r) => row.some((v, c) =>
      v === 0 && grid[r + 1] && grid[r + 1][c] === S &&
      grid[r - 1] && grid[r - 1][c] > 0 && grid[r - 1][c] !== S));
    if (!ok) fail(`sealed room ${S} has no room above it for a secret panel`);
  }
  for (const rid of Object.keys(spec.rooms))
    spec.rooms[rid] = { id: Number(rid), name: "", description: "",
                        entry_description: "", examinables: [],
                        ...spec.rooms[rid] };
  window.SLEUTH_HOUSES.push({
    label: spec.label || "Custom house",
    variant: "custom",
    rooms: spec.rooms,
    grid,
    weapons: spec.weapons,
    characters: spec.characters,
    anchors: spec.anchors || {},
    landing_actions: spec.landing_actions || [],
    screen: null,                      // synthesised on first draw
  });
};

scatter();
spawn(false);''')

open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("PATCH_COMPLETE")
PATCH_EOF

        c === world.panelCell[1] && dr === 1 &&

      redraw();

        state.row === world.westEnd[0] && state.col === world.westEnd[1]) {

exit 1
Thinking · Reconciling panel transitions and passage navigation mechanics.
I'm noticing the M5.5 update removed the old panel-through logic that handled both sides, leaving just the panel-entry and blind corridor paths on the wall branch. The question is how the player transitions from being on a panel cell into the passage itself—there used to be an `onPanel` check before the wall logic that handled stepping forward, but it seems to cut off mid-thought. Looking at the actual flow: when standing in a wall cell (a 0-cell) and pressing Down, the target becomes the adjacent passage cell (>0), which works fine since walkability only blocks when the target is 0. So the core movement is sound. For the failing test reps, I need to fix the `beyond === 2` rep to include the comment from line 619, drop the `beyond > 0 && beyond <= 20` rep entirely since that block no longer exists, and verify the scatter panel-candidate block around lines 265-270 matches what's actually there. The traceback pointed to the `beyond === 2` rep failing, so replacing it with the comment-included version and removing the outdated rep should resolve it—then I'll rerun the full script.
Thinking · Recalibrated search parameters after code removal.
The old pass-through code is already gone in the M5.5 rework, so I'm running the search again with updated targets.

bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - > /tmp/p.log 2>&1 << 'PATCH_EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
def rep(old, new):
    global src
    assert old in src, "NOT FOUND: " + old[:70]
    src = src.replace(old, new)

rep("SLEUTH remaster \u2014 engine.js \u2014 Phase 2, Milestone 5",
    "SLEUTH remaster \u2014 engine.js \u2014 Phase 2, Milestone 6")
rep('print(0, 1, "SLEUTH \\u00b7 milestone 5", 0x70);',
    'print(0, 1, "SLEUTH \\u00b7 milestone 6", 0x70);')

rep('''  const rank = rankFor(world.turn);
  say(`The case is closed after ${world.turn} moves.  You now rank ` +
      `as ${rank.startsWith("an ") ? rank : "a " + rank}!`);''',
'''  const rank = rankFor(world.turn);

rep('''/* Guests occupy exactly the 12 locations that have a seat and an''',
'''/* Per-house feature analysis. Custom houses may lack a secret room,
   stairs, or the full eleven rooms; everything below is DERIVED:
     GUEST_ROOMS   rooms that hold objects/guests/alibis (from the
                   weapon table's room ids)
     NPC_LOCATIONS guest rooms + the staircase, if the grid has one
     SECRET_ROOM   a guest room unreachable by ordinary walking (if
                   any) — it gets the panel and the blind corridor;
                   houses without one simply have neither. */

let GUEST_ROOMS = [], NPC_LOCATIONS = [], SECRET_ROOM = null;

function analyzeHouse() {
  const g = house().grid;
  GUEST_ROOMS = [...new Set(house().weapons.map((w) => w.room))].sort((a, b) => a - b);
  const hasStairs = !!findCell(12);
  NPC_LOCATIONS = GUEST_ROOMS.concat(hasStairs && !GUEST_ROOMS.includes(12) ? [12] : []);

  const start = findCell(36) || findCell(35) || middleCellIn(g, GUEST_ROOMS[0]);
  const seen = new Set([start.join()]), q = [start];
  while (q.length) {
    const [r, c] = q.pop();
    for (const [dr, dc] of [[1,0],[-1,0],[0,1],[0,-1]]) {
      const nr = r + dr, nc = c + dc, k = nr + "," + nc;
      if (g[nr] && g[nr][nc] > 0 && !seen.has(k)) { seen.add(k); q.push([nr, nc]); }
    }
  }
  SECRET_ROOM = GUEST_ROOMS.find((rid) => {
    const cell = middleCellIn(g, rid);
    return cell && !seen.has(cell.join());
  }) ?? null;
}

function middleCellIn(g, rid) {
  const cells = [];
  for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++)
    for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++)
      if (g[r][c] === rid) cells.push([r, c]);
  return cells.length ? cells[Math.floor(cells.length / 2)] : null;
}

/* Guests occupy exactly the 12 locations that have a seat and an''')

'''  // playtest-corrected: the panel is only entered from the room ABOVE
  // the secret room, pressing Down. Houses with no sealed room get no
  // panel and no blind corridor \u2014 the features simply don't exist.

  }
  world.panelCell = cand.length ? pick(cand) : null;''')

rep('''        cellAt(state.row, state.col) !== 2) {
      state.row = r; state.col = c;
      say(house().rooms["2"].entry_description);   // "secret panel..."''',
'''        cellAt(state.row, state.col) !== SECRET_ROOM) {
      state.row = r; state.col = c;
      say(house().rooms[String(SECRET_ROOM)].entry_description);''')
rep('''    if (cellAt(state.row, state.col) === 2 && world.westEnd &&''',
'''    if (cellAt(state.row, state.col) === SECRET_ROOM && world.westEnd &&''')

rep('''      // secret panel: a 1-thick wall with the passageway (room 2) on
      // one side and another room on the other joins them (guests know the way)
      if (g[r][c] === 0) {
        if (c > 0 && c + 1 < W &&
            (norm(g[r][c-1]) === 2) !== (norm(g[r][c+1]) === 2))
          connect(norm(g[r][c-1]), norm(g[r][c+1]));
        if (r > 0 && r + 1 < H &&
            (norm(g[r-1][c]) === 2) !== (norm(g[r+1][c]) === 2))
          connect(norm(g[r-1][c]), norm(g[r+1][c]));
      }''',
'''      // secret panel: a 1-thick wall with the secret room on one side
      // and another room on the other joins them (guests know the way)
      if (g[r][c] === 0 && SECRET_ROOM !== null) {
        const S = SECRET_ROOM;
        if (c > 0 && c + 1 < W &&
            (norm(g[r][c-1]) === S) !== (norm(g[r][c+1]) === S))
          connect(norm(g[r][c-1]), norm(g[r][c+1]));
        if (r > 0 && r + 1 < H &&
            (norm(g[r-1][c]) === S) !== (norm(g[r+1][c]) === S))
          connect(norm(g[r-1][c]), norm(g[r+1][c]));
      }''')

rep('''const objectHere = () =>
  state.roomId >= 1 && state.roomId <= 11 ? world.objects[state.roomId] : null;''',
'''const objectHere = () =>
  GUEST_ROOMS.includes(state.roomId) ? world.objects[state.roomId] : null;''')
rep('''  if (!(newLoc >= 1 && newLoc <= 11) || newLoc === prevLoc) return false;''',
'''  if (!GUEST_ROOMS.includes(newLoc) || newLoc === prevLoc) return false;''')
rep('''    const g = house().grid, cells = [];
    for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++)
        if (g[r][c] >= 1 && g[r][c] <= 11) cells.push([r, c]);''',
'''    const g = house().grid, cells = [];
    for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++)
        if (GUEST_ROOMS.includes(g[r][c])) cells.push([r, c]);''')
rep('''  const loc = locOf(state.roomId);
  if (!(loc >= 1 && loc <= 12)) {''',
'''  const loc = locOf(state.roomId);
  if (!NPC_LOCATIONS.includes(loc)) {''')
rep('''  const roomsAvail = [];
  for (let r = 1; r <= 11; r++) if (r !== world.murderRoom) roomsAvail.push(r);''',
'''  const roomsAvail = GUEST_ROOMS.filter((r) => r !== world.murderRoom);''')
rep('''  const pool = (room && room.npc_actions && room.npc_actions.length)
    ? room.npc_actions.flat()
    : house().landing_actions.flat();''',
'''  const pool = (room && room.npc_actions && room.npc_actions.length)
    ? room.npc_actions.flat()
    : (house().landing_actions || []).flat();''')

rep('''function drawHouse() {
  // Original houses blit the BSAVE bytes SLEUTH.EXE BLOADed into video
  // memory; custom houses get a synthesised screen in the same style.
  if (!house().screen) house().screen = synthesizeScreen(house().grid);
  const scr = house().screen;''' if False else '''function drawHouse() {''',
'''/* Custom houses ship no BSAVE art, so we synthesise a screen from the
   grid in the original's visual dialect: thin walls, gaps for doors,
   rungs for stairs, a border row on top, and the outdoor strip
   undrawn. Cached on the house object after first build. */
function synthesizeScreen(grid) {
  const chars = ["\\u2588".repeat(79) + " "];
  for (let r = 0; r < 16; r++) {
    let row = "";
    for (let x = 0; x < 80; x++) {
      const c = Math.floor((x + 1) / 2);          // cell owning column x
      const v = c < 40 ? grid[r][c] : 0;
      if (v === 0) {
        const rightIsWall = c + 1 >= 40 || grid[r][c + 1] === 0;
        row += (x === 2 * c - 1 || x === 0) ? "\\u2588"
             : (rightIsWall ? "\\u2588" : " ");
      } else if (v === 12 || v === 13) row += "\\u2500";   // stair rungs
      else row += " ";
    }
    chars.push(row);
  }
  const palettes = {};
  for (const s of SCHEMES) {
    const attr = s.key;
    palettes[s.key] = chars.map(() => attr.repeat(80));
  }
  return { chars, palettes };
}

function drawHouse() {
  if (!house().screen) house().screen = synthesizeScreen(house().grid);''')

rep('''scatter();
spawn(false);''',
'''/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   11. CUSTOM HOUSES \u2014 registerSleuthHouse(spec)
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Authoring format (see HOUSES.md): an ASCII map plus a legend, rooms,
   a cast of seven, and weapons grouped by room. Everything optional is
   defaulted; everything structural is validated with a thrown Error,
   because a clear message now beats a silent haunting later. */

window.registerSleuthHouse = function (spec) {
  const fail = (m) => { throw new Error("registerSleuthHouse: " + m); };
  if (!spec.map || spec.map.length !== 17 ||
      spec.map.some((row) => row.length !== 40))
    fail("map must be 17 rows of exactly 40 characters");
  const grid = spec.map.map((row) => [...row].map((ch) => {
    if (!(ch in spec.legend)) fail(`map character '${ch}' missing from legend`);
    return spec.legend[ch];
  }));
  if (!spec.characters || spec.characters.length < 7)
    fail("need at least 7 characters (six suspects + one victim)");
  const guestRooms = [...new Set((spec.weapons || []).map((w) => w.room))];
  if (guestRooms.length < 6)
    fail("need weapons in at least 6 distinct rooms (one guest per room)");
  for (const rid of guestRooms) {
    if (!spec.rooms[rid]) fail(`room ${rid} holds weapons but has no entry in rooms{}`);
    if (!grid.some((row) => row.includes(rid))) fail(`room ${rid} does not appear on the map`);
  }
  const start = (() => {
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] === 36 || grid[r][c] === 35) return [r, c];
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] > 0) return [r, c];
  })();
  const seen = new Set([start.join()]), q = [start];
  while (q.length) {
    const [r, c] = q.pop();
    for (const [dr, dc] of [[1,0],[-1,0],[0,1],[0,-1]]) {
      const nr = r + dr, nc = c + dc, k = nr + "," + nc;
      if (grid[nr] && grid[nr][nc] > 0 && !seen.has(k)) { seen.add(k); q.push([nr, nc]); }
    }
  }
  const sealed = guestRooms.filter((rid) => {
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] === rid) return !seen.has(r + "," + c);
    return false;
  });
  if (sealed.length > 1) fail("more than one unreachable room: " + sealed.join(","));
  if (sealed.length === 1) {
    const S = sealed[0];
    const ok = grid.some((row, r) => row.some((v, c) =>
      v === 0 && grid[r + 1] && grid[r + 1][c] === S &&
      grid[r - 1] && grid[r - 1][c] > 0 && grid[r - 1][c] !== S));
    if (!ok) fail(`sealed room ${S} has no room above it for a secret panel`);
  }
  for (const rid of Object.keys(spec.rooms))
    spec.rooms[rid] = { id: Number(rid), name: "", description: "",
                        entry_description: "", examinables: [],
                        ...spec.rooms[rid] };
  window.SLEUTH_HOUSES.push({
    label: spec.label || "Custom house",
    variant: "custom",
    rooms: spec.rooms,
    grid,
    weapons: spec.weapons,
    characters: spec.characters,
    anchors: spec.anchors || {},
    landing_actions: spec.landing_actions || [],
    screen: null,                      // synthesised on first draw
  });
};

scatter();
spawn(false);''')

open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("PATCH_COMPLETE")
PATCH_EOF
cat /tmp/p.log; grep -q PATCH_COMPLETE /tmp/p.log && node --check web/engine.js && echo "ENGINE OK"

exit 1

bash_toolApply full corrected M6 engine patch
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - > /tmp/p2.log 2>&1 << 'PATCH_EOF'
# resume: the earlier script died before writing; check which reps remain
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
already = "Milestone 6" in src
print("already patched?", already)
if not already:
    # re-run the whole corrected patch, with the one fixed anchor
    exec(open('/tmp/m6patch.py').read()) if False else None
PATCH_EOF
# simpler: save the full corrected patch to a file and run it
python3 - > /tmp/p.log 2>&1 << 'PATCH_EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
def rep(old, new):
    global src
    assert old in src, "NOT FOUND: " + old[:70]
    src = src.replace(old, new)

rep("SLEUTH remaster \u2014 engine.js \u2014 Phase 2, Milestone 5",
    "SLEUTH remaster \u2014 engine.js \u2014 Phase 2, Milestone 6")
rep('print(0, 1, "SLEUTH \\u00b7 milestone 5", 0x70);',
    'print(0, 1, "SLEUTH \\u00b7 milestone 6", 0x70);')
rep('''  say(`The case is closed after ${world.turn} moves.  You now rank ` +
      `as ${rank.startsWith("an ") ? rank : "a " + rank}!`);''',
'''  say(`The case is closed after ${world.turn} moves.  You now rank ` +
      `as ${rank.startsWith("an ") ? rank : "a " + rank} Sleuth!`);''')

rep('''/* Guests occupy exactly the 12 locations that have a seat and an''',
'''/* Per-house feature analysis. Custom houses may lack a secret room,
   stairs, or the full eleven rooms; everything below is DERIVED:
     GUEST_ROOMS   rooms that hold objects/guests/alibis (from the
                   weapon table's room ids)
     NPC_LOCATIONS guest rooms + the staircase, if the grid has one
     SECRET_ROOM   a guest room unreachable by ordinary walking (if
                   any) \u2014 it gets the panel and the blind corridor;
                   houses without one simply have neither. */

let GUEST_ROOMS = [], NPC_LOCATIONS = [], SECRET_ROOM = null;

function analyzeHouse() {
  const g = house().grid;
  GUEST_ROOMS = [...new Set(house().weapons.map((w) => w.room))].sort((a, b) => a - b);
  const hasStairs = !!findCell(12);
  NPC_LOCATIONS = GUEST_ROOMS.concat(hasStairs && !GUEST_ROOMS.includes(12) ? [12] : []);
  const start = findCell(36) || findCell(35) || middleCellIn(g, GUEST_ROOMS[0]);
  const seen = new Set([start.join()]), q = [start];
  while (q.length) {
    const [r, c] = q.pop();
    for (const [dr, dc] of [[1,0],[-1,0],[0,1],[0,-1]]) {
      const nr = r + dr, nc = c + dc, k = nr + "," + nc;
      if (g[nr] && g[nr][nc] > 0 && !seen.has(k)) { seen.add(k); q.push([nr, nc]); }
    }
  }
  SECRET_ROOM = GUEST_ROOMS.find((rid) => {
    const cell = middleCellIn(g, rid);
    return cell && !seen.has(cell.join());
  }) ?? null;
}

function middleCellIn(g, rid) {
  const cells = [];
  for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++)
    for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++)
      if (g[r][c] === rid) cells.push([r, c]);
  return cells.length ? cells[Math.floor(cells.length / 2)] : null;
}

/* Guests occupy exactly the 12 locations that have a seat and an''')

rep('''const NPC_LOCATIONS = Array.from({ length: 12 }, (_, i) => i + 1);

// the player's location in occupancy terms (both flights = one stair)''',
'''// the player's location in occupancy terms (both flights = one stair)''')

rep('''function scatter() {
  world.objects = {};
  const w = house().weapons;
  for (let room = 1; room <= 11; room++) {
    const candidates = w.filter((x) => x.room === room);
    const obj = pick(candidates);
    world.objects[room] = { ...obj, examined: false };
  }
  const roomIds = Array.from({ length: 11 }, (_, i) => i + 1);''',
'''function scatter() {
  analyzeHouse();
  world.objects = {};
  const w = house().weapons;
  for (const room of GUEST_ROOMS) {
    const candidates = w.filter((x) => x.room === room);
    world.objects[room] = { ...pick(candidates), examined: false };
  }
  const roomIds = [...GUEST_ROOMS];''')

rep('''  // playtest-corrected: the panel is only entered from the room ABOVE
  // the passageway, pressing Down (the original never admits you from
  // below). Candidates: wall cells with a room overhead and the
  // passage underneath.
  const g = house().grid, cand = [];
  let west = null;
  for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++) {
    for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++) {
      if (g[r][c] === 2 && (!west || c < west[1] ||
          (c === west[1] && r < west[0]))) west = [r, c];
      if (g[r][c] !== 0) continue;
      const n = (rr, cc) => (g[rr] && g[rr][cc]) || 0;
      if (n(r + 1, c) === 2 && n(r - 1, c) > 0 && n(r - 1, c) <= 20 &&
          n(r - 1, c) !== 2) cand.push([r, c]);
    }
  }
  world.panelCell = cand.length ? pick(cand) : null;''',
'''  // playtest-corrected: the panel is only entered from the room ABOVE
  // the secret room, pressing Down. Houses with no sealed room get no
  // panel and no blind corridor \u2014 the features simply don't exist.
  const g = house().grid, cand = [];
  let west = null;
  if (SECRET_ROOM !== null) {
    for (let r = 0; r < g.length; r++) {
      for (let c = 0; c < g[0].length; c++) {
        if (g[r][c] === SECRET_ROOM && (!west || c < west[1] ||
            (c === west[1] && r < west[0]))) west = [r, c];
        if (g[r][c] !== 0) continue;
        const n = (rr, cc) => (g[rr] && g[rr][cc]) || 0;
        if (n(r + 1, c) === SECRET_ROOM && n(r - 1, c) > 0 &&
            n(r - 1, c) <= 20 && n(r - 1, c) !== SECRET_ROOM)
          cand.push([r, c]);
      }
    }
  }
  world.panelCell = cand.length ? pick(cand) : null;''')

rep('''        cellAt(state.row, state.col) !== 2) {
      state.row = r; state.col = c;
      say(house().rooms["2"].entry_description);   // "secret panel..."''',
'''        cellAt(state.row, state.col) !== SECRET_ROOM) {
      state.row = r; state.col = c;
      say(house().rooms[String(SECRET_ROOM)].entry_description);''')
rep('''    if (cellAt(state.row, state.col) === 2 && world.westEnd &&''',
'''    if (cellAt(state.row, state.col) === SECRET_ROOM && world.westEnd &&''')

rep('''      // secret panel: a 1-thick wall with the passageway (room 2) on
      // one side and another room on the other joins them
      if (g[r][c] === 0) {
        if (c > 0 && c + 1 < W &&
            (norm(g[r][c-1]) === 2) !== (norm(g[r][c+1]) === 2))
          connect(norm(g[r][c-1]), norm(g[r][c+1]));
        if (r > 0 && r + 1 < H &&
            (norm(g[r-1][c]) === 2) !== (norm(g[r+1][c]) === 2))
          connect(norm(g[r-1][c]), norm(g[r+1][c]));
      }''',
'''      // secret panel: a 1-thick wall with the secret room on one side
      // and another room on the other joins them (guests know the way)
      if (g[r][c] === 0 && SECRET_ROOM !== null) {
        const S = SECRET_ROOM;
        if (c > 0 && c + 1 < W &&
            (norm(g[r][c-1]) === S) !== (norm(g[r][c+1]) === S))
          connect(norm(g[r][c-1]), norm(g[r][c+1]));
        if (r > 0 && r + 1 < H &&
            (norm(g[r-1][c]) === S) !== (norm(g[r+1][c]) === S))
          connect(norm(g[r-1][c]), norm(g[r+1][c]));
      }''')

rep('''const objectHere = () =>
  state.roomId >= 1 && state.roomId <= 11 ? world.objects[state.roomId] : null;''',
'''const objectHere = () =>
  GUEST_ROOMS.includes(state.roomId) ? world.objects[state.roomId] : null;''')
rep('''  if (!(newLoc >= 1 && newLoc <= 11) || newLoc === prevLoc) return false;''',
'''  if (!GUEST_ROOMS.includes(newLoc) || newLoc === prevLoc) return false;''')
rep('''        if (g[r][c] >= 1 && g[r][c] <= 11) cells.push([r, c]);''',
'''        if (GUEST_ROOMS.includes(g[r][c])) cells.push([r, c]);''')
rep('''  const loc = locOf(state.roomId);
  if (!(loc >= 1 && loc <= 12)) {''',
'''  const loc = locOf(state.roomId);
  if (!NPC_LOCATIONS.includes(loc)) {''')
rep('''  const roomsAvail = [];
  for (let r = 1; r <= 11; r++) if (r !== world.murderRoom) roomsAvail.push(r);''',
'''  const roomsAvail = GUEST_ROOMS.filter((r) => r !== world.murderRoom);''')
rep('''    : house().landing_actions.flat();''',
'''    : (house().landing_actions || []).flat();''')

rep('''function drawHouse() {''',
'''/* Custom houses ship no BSAVE art, so we synthesise a screen from the
   grid in the original's visual dialect: thin walls, gaps for doors,
   rungs for stairs, a border row on top, and the outdoor strip
   undrawn. Cached on the house object after first build. */
function synthesizeScreen(grid) {
  const chars = ["\\u2588".repeat(79) + " "];
  for (let r = 0; r < 16; r++) {
    let row = "";
    for (let x = 0; x < 80; x++) {
      const c = Math.floor((x + 1) / 2);          // cell owning column x
      const v = c < 40 ? grid[r][c] : 0;
      if (v === 0) {
        const rightIsWall = c + 1 >= 40 || grid[r][Math.min(c + 1, 39)] === 0;
        row += (x === 2 * c - 1 || x === 0) ? "\\u2588"
             : (rightIsWall ? "\\u2588" : " ");
      } else if (v === 12 || v === 13) row += "\\u2500";   // stair rungs
      else row += " ";
    }
    chars.push(row);
  }
  const palettes = {};
  for (const s of SCHEMES)
    palettes[s.key] = chars.map(() => s.key.repeat(80));
  return { chars, palettes };
}

function drawHouse() {
  if (!house().screen) house().screen = synthesizeScreen(house().grid);''')

rep('''scatter();
spawn(false);''',
'''/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   11. CUSTOM HOUSES \u2014 registerSleuthHouse(spec)
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Authoring format (see HOUSES.md): an ASCII map plus a legend, rooms,
   a cast of seven, and weapons grouped by room. Everything optional is
   defaulted; everything structural is validated with a thrown Error,
   because a clear message now beats a silent haunting later. */

window.registerSleuthHouse = function (spec) {
  const fail = (m) => { throw new Error("registerSleuthHouse: " + m); };
  if (!spec.map || spec.map.length !== 17 ||
      spec.map.some((row) => row.length !== 40))
    fail("map must be 17 rows of exactly 40 characters");
  const grid = spec.map.map((row) => [...row].map((ch) => {
    if (!(ch in spec.legend)) fail(`map character '${ch}' missing from legend`);
    return spec.legend[ch];
  }));
  if (!spec.characters || spec.characters.length < 7)
    fail("need at least 7 characters (six suspects + one victim)");
  const guestRooms = [...new Set((spec.weapons || []).map((w) => w.room))];
  if (guestRooms.length < 6)
    fail("need weapons in at least 6 distinct rooms (one guest per room)");
  for (const rid of guestRooms) {
    if (!spec.rooms[rid]) fail(`room ${rid} holds weapons but has no entry in rooms{}`);
    if (!grid.some((row) => row.includes(rid))) fail(`room ${rid} does not appear on the map`);
  }
  const start = (() => {
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] === 36 || grid[r][c] === 35) return [r, c];
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] > 0) return [r, c];
  })();
  const seen = new Set([start.join()]), q = [start];
  while (q.length) {
    const [r, c] = q.pop();
    for (const [dr, dc] of [[1,0],[-1,0],[0,1],[0,-1]]) {
      const nr = r + dr, nc = c + dc, k = nr + "," + nc;
      if (grid[nr] && grid[nr][nc] > 0 && !seen.has(k)) { seen.add(k); q.push([nr, nc]); }
    }
  }
  const sealed = guestRooms.filter((rid) => {
    for (let r = 0; r < 17; r++) for (let c = 0; c < 40; c++)
      if (grid[r][c] === rid) return !seen.has(r + "," + c);
    return false;
  });
  if (sealed.length > 1) fail("more than one unreachable room: " + sealed.join(","));
  if (sealed.length === 1) {
    const S = sealed[0];
    const ok = grid.some((row, r) => row.some((v, c) =>
      v === 0 && grid[r + 1] && grid[r + 1][c] === S &&
      grid[r - 1] && grid[r - 1][c] > 0 && grid[r - 1][c] !== S));
    if (!ok) fail(`sealed room ${S} has no room above it for a secret panel`);
  }
  for (const rid of Object.keys(spec.rooms))
    spec.rooms[rid] = { id: Number(rid), name: "", description: "",
                        entry_description: "", examinables: [],
                        ...spec.rooms[rid] };
  window.SLEUTH_HOUSES.push({
    label: spec.label || "Custom house",
    variant: "custom",
    rooms: spec.rooms,
    grid,
    weapons: spec.weapons,
    characters: spec.characters,
    anchors: spec.anchors || {},
    landing_actions: spec.landing_actions || [],
    screen: null,                      // synthesised on first draw
  });
};

scatter();
spawn(false);''')

open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("PATCH_COMPLETE")
PATCH_EOF
cat /tmp/p.log; grep -q PATCH_COMPLETE /tmp/p.log && node --check web/engine.js && echo "ENGINE OK"
Output
PATCH_COMPLETE
ENGINE OK
Thinking · Architecting a customizable cottage layout with central hall design.
Now I'm setting up a demo house file that showcases the customization features—a single-floor cottage without secret passages or stairs. I'm designing a 40×17 map with six rooms arranged around a central hall and a porch at the bottom, starting with the parlor occupying the upper-left area. Defining the room layout more precisely: rooms span rows 1-5, 7-11, and 12-15 with the hall running down the middle columns, and I'm placing doors between each room and the hall using specific tile values (21-26 for the six room doors, plus door 36 at the bottom of the hall leading to the porch). I'll build the map character by character using '#' for walls, letter codes for rooms, and numeric codes for doors. I'm refining the layout so rooms E and F occupy only rows 13-14, with row 15 serving as a wall containing the front door at column 19, and row 16 as the porch below it. The door positions at columns 12 and 26 in row 13 connect the side rooms to the hall, and with six guest rooms fully occupied by NPCs, movement options are limited but the setup works. Actually, to make the demo less static, I'm restructuring to add a seventh room by splitting the right side: kitchen B takes rows 1-2, pantry G takes rows 4-5, with a wall row between them at row 3, both connecting to the hall via doors. This gives me seven guest rooms with six suspects, leaving one room empty so NPCs can actually move around during the questioning tour. Laying out the room grid... Finishing the legend mapping, then I need to create the data_house3.js file with 14 weapons distributed across 7 rooms, each with invented NPC names, room descriptions, and action pairs. After that, I'll update index.html to include the new data file and add the milestone 6 marker, then patch the build system to inline data_house3.js properly. Finally, testing: verify house3 loads correctly, check that SECRET_ROOM and panel logic work as expected, run the full win scenario, and regression test the bot on both house 2 and house 3 with tab navigation.
Thinking · Architected customizable house layouts accommodating variable features.
I've successfully created House 3, a single-floor cottage with seven rooms and no stairs or secret passages, using the new API.
create_fileCreate the demo custom house exercising the authoring API
📄 /home/claude/web/data_house3.js
javascript
// House 3 — "Foxglove Cottage": the customisation demo.
// One floor, seven rooms, NO staircase and NO secret room — proving the
// engine derives its features from data instead of assuming Sleuth's.
// Authored via registerSleuthHouse(); see HOUSES.md for the format.

window.registerSleuthHouse({
  label: "House 3 \u00b7 Foxglove Cottage",

  // 17 rows x 40 columns. legend maps characters to cell values:
  // 0 wall, 1-20 room ids, roomId+20 = a door into that room, 36 = the
  // front door (also the spawn point).
  map: [
    "########################################",
    "#AAAAAAAAAAA#HHHHHHHHHHHHH2BBBBBBBBBBBB#",
    "#AAAAAAAAAAA#HHHHHHHHHHHHH#BBBBBBBBBBBB#",
    "#AAAAAAAAAAA1HHHHHHHHHHHHH##############",
    "#AAAAAAAAAAA#HHHHHHHHHHHHH7GGGGGGGGGGGG#",
    "#AAAAAAAAAAA#HHHHHHHHHHHHH#GGGGGGGGGGGG#",
    "#############HHHHHHHHHHHHH##############",
    "#CCCCCCCCCCC#HHHHHHHHHHHHH#DDDDDDDDDDDD#",
    "#CCCCCCCCCCC#HHHHHHHHHHHHH#DDDDDDDDDDDD#",
    "#CCCCCCCCCCC3HHHHHHHHHHHHH4DDDDDDDDDDDD#",
    "#CCCCCCCCCCC#HHHHHHHHHHHHH#DDDDDDDDDDDD#",
    "#CCCCCCCCCCC#HHHHHHHHHHHHH#DDDDDDDDDDDD#",
    "#############HHHHHHHHHHHHH##############",
    "#EEEEEEEEEEE5HHHHHHHHHHHHH6FFFFFFFFFFFF#",
    "#EEEEEEEEEEE#HHHHHHHHHHHHH#FFFFFFFFFFFF#",
    "###################X####################",
    "##################PPP###################",
  ],
  legend: { "#": 0, "A": 1, "B": 2, "C": 3, "D": 4, "E": 5, "F": 6,
            "G": 7, "H": 15, "P": 16,
            "1": 21, "2": 22, "3": 23, "4": 24, "5": 25, "6": 26,
            "7": 27, "X": 36 },

  rooms: {
    1: { name: "parlor",
         description: "You are in the parlor.  Overstuffed chairs face a cold fireplace.",
         entry_description: "This doorway leads into the parlor.",
         examinables: [{ keyword: "FIREPLACE",
           response: "Cold ashes.  Nobody has lit it in days." }],
         npc_actions: [["is poking at the cold fireplace.",
                        "is straightening the antimacassars."]] },
    2: { name: "kitchen",
         description: "You are in the kitchen.  A pot of something has gone quite cold.",
         entry_description: "This doorway leads into the kitchen.",
         examinables: [{ keyword: "POT",
           response: "Yesterday's stew.  It has formed a skin." }],
         npc_actions: [["is peering into the cold pot.",
                        "is drying the same dish over and over."]] },
    3: { name: "library",
         description: "You are in the library.  The shelves sag with damp paperbacks.",
         entry_description: "This doorway leads into the library.",
         examinables: [{ keyword: "PAPERBACK",
           response: "A lurid mystery.  The last page has been torn out." }],
         npc_actions: [["is reading a lurid paperback.",
                        "is running a finger along the shelves."]] },
    4: { name: "study",
         description: "You are in the study.  Papers are strewn across a rolltop desk.",
         entry_description: "This doorway leads into the study.",
         examinables: [{ keyword: "PAPER",
           response: "Unpaid bills, mostly.  Someone here had debts." }],
         npc_actions: [["is shuffling papers at the desk.",
                        "is staring out the study window."]] },
    5: { name: "music room",
         description: "You are in the music room.  A harp stands slightly out of tune.",
         entry_description: "This doorway leads into the music room.",
         examinables: [{ keyword: "HARP",
           response: "One string has recently snapped." }],
         npc_actions: [["is plucking idly at the harp.",
                        "is humming something mournful."]] },
    6: { name: "dining nook",
         description: "You are in the dining nook.  Six places are set; one is untouched.",
         entry_description: "This doorway leads into the dining nook.",
         examinables: [{ keyword: "PLACE",
           response: "The untouched setting was the guest of honour's." }],
         npc_actions: [["is rearranging the untouched place setting.",
                        "is folding and refolding a napkin."]] },
    7: { name: "pantry",
         description: "You are in the pantry.  The preserves are dusty and the flour is low.",
         entry_description: "This doorway leads into the pantry.",
         examinables: [{ keyword: "PRESERVE",
           response: "Gooseberry, 1979.  Best left sealed." }],
         npc_actions: [["is counting the preserve jars.",
                        "is hiding among the shelves."]] },
    15: { name: "hall",
          description: "You are in the cottage's long hall.  Rain taps at the fanlight.",
          entry_description: "" },
    16: { name: "front step",
          description: "You are on the front step of Foxglove Cottage.  The door stands ajar.",
          entry_description: "You are standing at the front door." },
  },

  // seven names: one becomes the victim each game
  characters: [
    { first: "Prudence", last: "Fern",   key: "PRUDENCE" },
    { first: "Oswald",   last: "Fern",   key: "OSWALD" },
    { first: "Harriet",  last: "Quill",  key: "HARRIET" },
    { first: "Bartleby", last: "Moss",   key: "BARTLEBY" },
    { first: "Cordelia", last: "Wren",   key: "CORDELIA" },
    { first: "Ignatius", last: "Pike",   key: "IGNATIUS" },
    { first: "Winifred", last: "Sedge",  key: "WINIFRED" },
  ],

  // two candidate objects per room; one per room is scattered per game
  weapons: [
    { room: 1, name: "poker", keys: ["POKER"],
      scene: "A heavy iron poker leans against the fireplace.",
      examine: "It is a heavy iron poker with a brass handle." },
    { room: 1, name: "bookend", keys: ["BOOKEND"],
      scene: "A marble bookend sits on the mantel.",
      examine: "A marble bust of some forgotten poet." },
    { room: 2, name: "rolling pin", keys: ["ROLLING", "PIN"],
      scene: "A floured rolling pin rests on the counter.",
      examine: "A well-used maple rolling pin." },
    { room: 2, name: "skillet", keys: ["SKILLET"],
      scene: "A cast-iron skillet hangs by the stove.",
      examine: "Heavy enough for pancakes or worse." },
    { room: 3, name: "atlas", keys: ["ATLAS"],
      scene: "An enormous atlas lies open on a stand.",
      examine: "The world, as of 1953, and very heavy." },
    { room: 3, name: "globe", keys: ["GLOBE"],
      scene: "A tarnished globe sits by the window.",
      examine: "The base is solid brass." },
    { room: 4, name: "paperweight", keys: ["PAPERWEIGHT"],
      scene: "A glass paperweight pins down the bills.",
      examine: "A swirl of blue glass, dense as a fist." },
    { room: 4, name: "letter opener", keys: ["LETTER", "OPENER"],
      scene: "A silver letter opener glints on the desk.",
      examine: "Sharper than a letter strictly requires." },
    { room: 5, name: "metronome", keys: ["METRONOME"],
      scene: "A brass metronome ticks on the sill.",
      examine: "Solid brass, still keeping strict time." },
    { room: 5, name: "flute", keys: ["FLUTE"],
      scene: "A silver flute rests on a velvet cloth.",
      examine: "Cold to the touch, and surprisingly weighty." },
    { room: 6, name: "candlestick", keys: ["CANDLESTICK", "CANDLE"],
      scene: "A pewter candlestick anchors the table.",
      examine: "Pewter, with old wax down one side." },
    { room: 6, name: "carving fork", keys: ["CARVING", "FORK"],
      scene: "A long carving fork lies beside the roast.",
      examine: "Two tines, recently sharpened." },
    { room: 7, name: "preserve jar", keys: ["JAR", "PRESERVE"],
      scene: "A hefty jar of gooseberry preserves sits at eye level.",
      examine: "Gooseberry, 1979.  Weaponously heavy." },
    { room: 7, name: "flour sack", keys: ["FLOUR", "SACK"],
      scene: "A half-full flour sack slumps in the corner.",
      examine: "Heavier than it looks, and it looks heavy." },
  ],
});

bash_toolWire house 3 into build; verify full playability and derived featureserror
bash
cd /home/claude && sed -i 's|<script src="data_v2.js"></script>|<script src="data_v2.js"></script>\n<script src="data_house3.js"></script>|; s|milestone 5 — the complete game|milestone 6 — build your own house|' web/index.html && python3 -c "
src = open('gen_web_data.py').read()
src = src.replace('''    inlined = html.replace(
        '<script src=\"data_shared.js\"></script>\\\\n'
        '<script src=\"data_v1.js\"></script>\\\\n'
        '<script src=\"data_v2.js\"></script>\\\\n'
        '<script src=\"engine.js\"></script>',
        \"<script>\\\\n\" + \"\\\\n\".join(data_js) + \"\\\\n\" + engine + \"\\\\n</script>\")''',
'''    house3 = (out / \"data_house3.js\").read_text()
    inlined = html.replace(
        '<script src=\"data_shared.js\"></script>\\\\n'
        '<script src=\"data_v1.js\"></script>\\\\n'
        '<script src=\"data_v2.js\"></script>\\\\n'
        '<script src=\"data_house3.js\"></script>\\\\n'
        '<script src=\"engine.js\"></script>',
        \"<script>\\\\n\" + \"\\\\n\".join(data_js) + \"\\\\n\" + engine +
        \"\\\\n\" + house3 + \"\\\\n</script>\")''')
open('gen_web_data.py','w').write(src)
print('builder patched')" && node --check web/data_house3.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null 2>&1 || python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth; echo "---"; cd web && node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 606, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(flat().slice(-400)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

assert(global.window.SLEUTH_HOUSES.length === 3, "three houses registered");
// V1 analysis sanity: secret room derived as 2
assert(flat().includes("Brisbane") || flat().includes("estate"), "V1 loads");
key("Tab"); key("Tab");                                    // to house 3
assert(flat().includes("Foxglove Cottage"), "house 3 label");
assert(plain().includes("☻") && flat().includes("front door"), "spawn at cottage front door");
// derived features: no secret room, no panel, no blind corridor, no stairs
const G = D; const eng = { };
assert(world.panelCell === null && world.westEnd === null, "no panel/blind corridor derived");
// walk in and around: synthesized screen renders walls
key("ArrowUp"); key("ArrowUp");
assert(plain().split("\n").slice(1,17).join("").includes("█"), "synthesized screen renders");
assert(flat().includes("long hall"), "hall description");
// meet a guest, question with 'q'
world.suspects[0].room = 1; state.roomId = 15;
while (state.col > 12) key("ArrowLeft");
while (state.row > 3) key("ArrowUp");
key("ArrowLeft"); key("ArrowLeft");
assert(flat().toLowerCase().includes("parlor"), "entered the parlor");
type("ex fireplace");
assert(flat().includes("Cold ashes"), "custom examinable");
// full win on the cottage
world.hasGlass = true;
const g = D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
const wk = world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0];
type("ex " + wk); type("get " + wk);
assert(world.carrying, "weapon confirmed and carried in the cottage");
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key);
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "full victory in the custom house, rank ends with Sleuth");
console.log("\nHOUSE 3 FULLY PLAYABLE — CUSTOMISATION PROVEN");
EOF
Output
The command exceeded the execution time limit. Try breaking it into smaller steps or adding a timeout to the command itself.
Thinking · Tracing movement logic and diagnosing infinite loop conditions.
I'm spotting a potential infinite loop in the movement logic—specifically when the player tries to move left from column 19 but gets blocked by a wall, causing the while condition to never resolve. Let me trace through the spawn position at (15,19) and the movement sequence upward through the hallway to see where the collision detection might be failing. The fix involves adjusting the navigation path: move up along column 19 until reaching row 3, then move left along row 3 to the parlor door at column 12, then enter the parlor. I should also add iteration guards to the while loops to prevent future infinite loops, and re-run the build to ensure the web data generation completed properly before testing again.
Thinking · Debugged navigation loop and fortified iteration safeguards.
My test hit an infinite loop in the navigation logic, so I'm rebuilding it with proper iteration guards and a corrected route.
bash_toolVerify house 3 playability with guarded navigation
bash
cd /home/claude && timeout 60 python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth && cd web && timeout 90 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 606, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(flat().slice(-400)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const until = (cond, k) => { for (let i = 0; i < 30 && !cond(); i++) key(k); return cond(); };

assert(global.window.SLEUTH_HOUSES.length === 3, "three houses registered");
key("Tab"); key("Tab");
assert(flat().includes("Foxglove Cottage"), "house 3 label");
assert(plain().includes("☻") && flat().includes("front door"), "spawn at cottage front door");
assert(world.panelCell === null && world.westEnd === null, "no panel / blind corridor derived");
assert(plain().split("\n").slice(1,17).join("").includes("█"), "synthesized screen renders");

assert(until(() => state.row === 3, "ArrowUp"), "walked up the hall");
assert(until(() => state.col <= 11, "ArrowLeft"), "through the parlor door");
assert(flat().toLowerCase().includes("parlor"), "parlor described");
type("ex fireplace");
assert(flat().includes("Cold ashes"), "custom examinable answers");

world.hasGlass = true;
const g = D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
const wk = world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0];
type("ex " + wk); type("get " + wk);
assert(world.carrying, "weapon confirmed and carried");
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key);
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"),
       "full victory in the custom house; rank ends with Sleuth");
// validation errors are real errors
let threw = false;
try { global.window.registerSleuthHouse({ map: ["short"], legend: {} }); }
catch (e) { threw = /17 rows/.test(e.message); }
assert(threw, "authoring validation throws clear errors");
console.log("\nHOUSE 3 FULLY PLAYABLE — CUSTOMISATION PROVEN");
EOF

ok: parlor described
ok: custom examinable answers
ok: weapon confirmed and carried
ok: full victory in the custom house; rank ends with Sleuth

HOUSE 3 FULLY PLAYABLE — CUSTOMISATION PROVEN
Thinking · Thought process
House 3 is working well, so now I'm moving on to verify House 2 with the full bot suite across different seeds, plus running regression tests on House 1. The bot will need to support tabbing to House 2 as part of this verification.
Thinking · Charted implementation strategy for player-bot testing and generalization.
House 3 is working end to end. Now I'll implement the full player-bot on House 2 and run V1 regression tests to verify the generalization didn't break anything.
bash_toolRun the full solve-bot on house 2 and regression on house 1
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 -c "
src = open('/tmp/playerbot.js').read()
src = src.replace('''const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
world.deadline = 99999;''',
'''const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
for (let i = 0; i < Number(process.argv[3] || 0); i++)
  handlers.keydown({ key: \"Tab\", preventDefault(){} });   // house select
world.deadline = 99999;''')
open('/tmp/playerbot.js','w').write(src)
print('bot: house selection added')" && cd web && echo "--- HOUSE 2 (Donleavy mansion) ---" && for seed in 7 42 313 999; do timeout 60 node /tmp/playerbot.js $seed 1; done && echo "--- HOUSE 1 regression ---" && for seed in 42 2026; do timeout 60 node /tmp/playerbot.js $seed 0; done

Thinking · Diagnosing incorrect accusation mechanics in threat system.
Looking at House 2's seed 313 loss at turn 475 — the deadline was set to 99999 by the bot, so it's not a timeout issue. Since the threat system requires staying within thresholds and that deadline never triggers, the loss must have come from an incorrect accusation. I'm suspecting the issue is in how the murder room or weapon room values are being set to 2, which might be routing through the panel logic incorrectly. Now I'm tracing through V2's secret room setup: the spawn is above door 35, and room 2 should be unreachable, making SECRET_ROOM=2 valid. When the bot navigates to the panel and moves down twice, that's generic enough. The real question is whether the panel-above room itself is reachable in V2 — the panel candidates were computed earlier at rows (6,1..5), but I need to verify which room V2's position (5,c) actually maps to. There's also the possibility that the murder room is only accessible through a specific path, or the bot is gathering in the wrong location, or there's a parsing issue with the accused keys. To nail this down, I should run seed 313 with House 2 and dump the final screen state plus a world snapshot.

House 2 mostly clean; seed 313 lost — diagnosing:

bash_toolDiagnose the house 2 seed 313 loss
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 60 node << 'EOF'
process.argv[2] = "313"; process.argv[3] = "1";
const src = require('fs').readFileSync('/tmp/playerbot.js','utf8');
eval(src.replace("console.log(JSON.stringify",
  "console.log('murderRoom', world.murderRoom, 'weaponRoom', world.weaponRoom, 'secret?', typeof SECRET_ROOM !== 'undefined' ? SECRET_ROOM : '?');" +
  "console.log('final loc', state.roomId, 'carrying', world.carrying);" +
  "console.log(lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/\\s+/g,' ').slice(-450));" +
  "console.log(JSON.stringify"));
