# ---- 4. PAGINATION ----
rep('''const MSG_TOP = 19, MSG_ROWS = 5;''',
'''const MSG_TOP = 20, MSG_ROWS = 4;
const pending = [];       // lines waiting behind the -- MORE -- gate
let freshLines = 0;       // unacknowledged lines on screen''')

rep('''function say(text) {
  for (const line of wrap(text, COLS - 2)) {
    log.push(line);
    while (log.length > MSG_ROWS) log.shift();
  }
  for (let i = 0; i < MSG_ROWS; i++) {
    clearRow(MSG_TOP + i, 0x07);
    if (log[i]) print(MSG_TOP + i, 1, log[i], 0x07);
  }
  render();
}''',
'''function say(text) {
  if (text) for (const line of wrap(text, COLS - 2)) pending.push(line);
  pump();
}

/* Move pending lines onto the screen, at most one page of new lines
   between keypresses; hold the rest behind a -- MORE -- marker. */
function pump() {
  while (pending.length && freshLines < MSG_ROWS) {
    log.push(pending.shift());
    while (log.length > MSG_ROWS) log.shift();
    freshLines++;
  }
  for (let i = 0; i < MSG_ROWS; i++) {
    clearRow(MSG_TOP + i, 0x07);
    if (log[i]) print(MSG_TOP + i, 1, log[i], 0x07);
  }
  if (pending.length)
    print(MSG_TOP + MSG_ROWS - 1, COLS - 13, " -- MORE -- ", 0x70);
  render();
}''')

rep('''window.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
  const k = e.key;
  if (world.blind) {''',
'''window.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
  const k = e.key;
  if (pending.length) {                  // -- MORE --: any key pages on
    freshLines = 0;
    pump();
    e.preventDefault();
    return;
  }
  freshLines = 0;                        // player acted: screen is read
  if (world.blind) {''')

# ---- 5. C DISCOVERABILITY ----
rep('''  const keys = "\\u2190\\u2191\\u2193\\u2192 \\u00b7 type \\u00b7 \\u23ce";''',
'''  const keys = "\\u2190\\u2191\\u2193\\u2192 \\u00b7 TAB \\u00b7 C \\u00b7 \\u23ce";''')
rep('''  say("(All original verbs are live. Solve it before the murderer " +
      "solves you \\u2014 and mind the deadline.)");''',
'''  say("(All original verbs are live. C cycles the five original colour " +
      "schemes and TAB switches houses \\u2014 when the command line is " +
      "empty. Solve it before the murderer solves you.)");''')

open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("PATCH_COMPLETE")
PATCH_EOF
cat /tmp/p.log; grep -q PATCH_COMPLETE /tmp/p.log && node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth && echo "BUILD OK"
Output
PATCH_COMPLETE
data_shared.js: 20 verbs, 18 framings, 41 general elaborations, 7 confessions
data_v1.js: 16 rooms, 17x40 grid
data_v2.js: 17 rooms, 17x40 grid
sleuth_playable.html: single-file build (151217 bytes)
BUILD OK
Thinking · Verifying patch functionality across multiple game elements.
The patch is in and the build passes. Now I'm running through the verification checklist: checking that V2 spawns at the leftmost door, the character can walk across the lawn to the right entry with the smiley visible at the correct screen position, pagination displays the MORE prompt and responds to keypresses, the stare gate mechanics work correctly across different room states and guest presence, and that the arrival and list narration includes the staring line. I also need to verify the keys legend shows TAB and C, and run a full regression test to make sure nothing broke. For the test file, I'm being careful about keydown events during pagination—my type() helper sends keys that get consumed by the pager while pending lines exist, so I need to drain the pending text first by checking for the MORE marker in the rendered HTML and pressing space until it's gone before typing any commands.

Patch landed clean. Full verification suite — note the tests must drain the -- MORE -- pager before typing, which is itself a nice proof it works:

bash_toolVerify stare gate, left spawn, lawn row, pagination, C legend, win regression
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 120 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 162689, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const drain = () => { for (let i = 0; i < 12 && plain().includes("-- MORE --"); i++) key(" "); };
const type = s => { drain(); for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(flat().slice(-500)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

// --- 4) pagination on the V1 intro (victim text + hint + spawn desc)
assert(plain().includes("-- MORE --") || flat().includes("Type HELP"),
       "intro renders (paged or fits)");
drain();
assert(!plain().includes("-- MORE --"), "MORE drains on keypresses");

// --- 2) house 2 spawns on the LEFT lawn door
key("Tab");
drain();
const g2 = D.house().grid;
assert(g2[state.row][state.col] === 34 || (state.col < 20 && state.row === 16),
       `V2 spawn on the left (row ${state.row}, col ${state.col})`);
// --- 3) sprite visible on the outdoor strip (screen row 18 = grid 16)
let scrRow = plain().split("\n").findIndex(l => l.includes("☻"));
assert(scrRow === state.row + 2, `lawn sprite visible (screen ${scrRow})`);
// info line moved below it
const rows = plain().split("\n");
assert(/turn \d/.test(rows[19]), "room/turn info line at row 19");

// --- 3b) lawn circumnavigation: walk east along row 16 to the right door
let steps = 0;
while (state.col < 30 && steps++ < 60) { drain(); key("ArrowRight"); }
assert(state.col >= 30, "walked the lawn across the front of the house");
assert(flat().toLowerCase().includes("lawn") || flat().includes("storming"),
       "front-lawn description narrated en route");
drain(); key("ArrowUp"); drain();
assert(state.row < 16, "entered the house from the right side");

// --- 1) the floor-staring tell
key("Tab"); key("Tab"); drain();          // back to house 1, fresh scatter
const M = () => world.suspects.find(s => s.key === world.murderer.key);
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { const g = D.house().grid;
  outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
const witness = world.suspects[0];
// (a) before stareStart: murder room + guest present, not staring -> nearsighted
witness.room = world.murderRoom; witness.staring = false;
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("rather nearsighted") && !world.sceneFound,
       "pre-stare: murder-room floor stays hidden");
// (b) staring witness present -> bloodstains
witness.staring = true;
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("bloodstains") && world.sceneFound,
       "staring witness unlocks the bloodstains");
// (c) red herring: staring guest in the WRONG room -> nearsighted
D.execute("restart"); drain();
const herring = world.suspects[0];
const wrong = D.house().weapons.map(w=>w.room).find(r => r !== world.murderRoom);
herring.room = wrong; herring.staring = true;
gotoRoom(wrong);
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("rather nearsighted"), "red-herring stare resolves to nearsighted");
// (d) narration shows the tell
type("knock");
assert(flat().includes("is staring at the floor."), "narration announces the stare");
// (e) staring states drift after stareStart in the murder room
D.execute("restart"); drain();
world.stareStart = 0;
const occupant = world.suspects[0];
occupant.room = world.murderRoom;
state.roomId = 15;                          // player elsewhere so they can be re-rolled
let stared = false;
for (let i = 0; i < 200 && !stared; i++) {
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown"); drain();
  occupant.room = world.murderRoom;
  stared = occupant.staring;
}
assert(stared, "murder-room occupant drifts into staring over time");

// --- 5) C legend + help
assert(plain().split("\n")[0].includes("TAB · C"), "status legend shows TAB · C");
type("help"); drain();
assert(flat().includes("C cycles the five original colour schemes"), "HELP documents C and TAB");

// --- regression: full win with the stare gate active
D.execute("restart"); drain();
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(world.carrying, "weapon in hand");
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key); drain();
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "full win regression");
console.log("\nALL FIVE TWEAKS VERIFIED");
EOF
Output
ok: intro renders (paged or fits)
ok: MORE drains on keypresses
ok: V2 spawn on the left (row 16, col 10)
ok: lawn sprite visible (screen 18)
ok: room/turn info line at row 19
ok: walked the lawn across the front of the house
ok: front-lawn description narrated en route
ok: entered the house from the right side
ok: pre-stare: murder-room floor stays hidden
ok: staring witness unlocks the bloodstains
ok: red-herring stare resolves to nearsighted
██████████████████████ █ █ ███ █ █ █ ███ ████████████████████████ █████████████████████████████ █ █ █ ███ █ █ █ ███ █ ███ █ █ █ ███ █ █ █ ███ ████████████████████████ █████████████████████████████ █ ███ █ █ █ ███ ████████████████████████████████████ █████████████████████████████████████████ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ ☻ PARLOR turn 0 I can't see anything unusual about the floor. But then I am rather nearsighted. > knock There's no need to observe common courtesies in this house. > _
stderr
FAIL: narration announces the stare
exit 1
Thinking · Diagnosing Tab cycling malfunction in navigation logic.
Test (d) is failing because the Tab navigation isn't cycling correctly — I'm seeing twenty-one smileys and "PARLOR" in the dump, which means pressing Tab twice from house 2 landed me in house 3 instead of cycling back to house 1 as expected. The Tab order should go 1→2→3→1, but something's off with how the focus is being managed between rooms. Looking at the sequence: I started in house 1, pressed Tab once to reach house 2 (during the spawn/lawn tests), then pressed Tab twice more — but the pager consumed the first of those two Tabs while waiting for text input that exceeded four lines. So only one Tab actually registered, moving me to house 3 instead of cycling through. The `drain()` call came *after* the Tab presses in my code, which means the pager was still active and intercepted the input. I need to call `drain()` first to clear the pager before navigating. Because tests (c) and (d) ended up running on house 3 instead of house 1, the knock command is returning the generic "no need to observe common courtesies" message (the authentic KNOCK verb response) rather than the narration that lists guests — which is what test (d) was expecting to see from a redraw via knock. The real issue is that teleporting with `state.roomId = wrong` doesn't trigger the describeCell entry behavior that announces occupants. I need the player to actually *enter* the room through a door. I could either walk out and back in by positioning adjacent and pressing an arrow key, or call `D.execute("look")` if LOOK is mapped to the examine verb and will re-describe the space. As for the 21 smileys appearing as a row across screen row 18 — that's the guest sprites being drawn at regular intervals, likely from the seatTaken nudge loop that positions them. The flat() call is stripping newlines, so those 21 smileys might actually be scattered across different rows and columns rather than all on one line. The real issue is the eaten Tab on the test side, but I should verify whether there's a genuine render bug in house 3 after the teleports — if the roomId got set wrong while the player is in the wrong room, I'd expect only 2 smileys (guest + player), not 21. The restart triggers the intro output which blocks the pager, but that wouldn't explain the extra smileys either. I'm wondering if seatTaken isn't being reset properly across multiple knock/redraw cycles, which could be accumulating guest positions. Let me rerun house 3 and dump the plain rows with indices to see what's actually on screen, then fix the test by draining before the Tab inputs and ensuring proper room entry. I'll also add a smiley count print to the house 3 screen output to check if it's genuinely over 7.
Thinking · Diagnosed sequencing bug and plotted corrective testing strategy.
I found a test-sequencing bug where a Tab press was consumed by the pager, causing tests to run on the wrong house, and I also need to investigate the unexpected smiley output. I'll rerun the tests with corrected sequencing and add a sprite-count check.
bash_toolIsolate the house 3 smiley anomaly
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 120 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 162689, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const drain = () => { for (let i = 0; i < 12 && plain().includes("-- MORE --"); i++) key(" "); };
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(-900)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

// house 3 sprite sanity: how many smileys after a teleport-style test?
drain(); key("Tab"); drain(); key("Tab"); drain();   // -> house 3
assert(flat().includes("Foxglove"), "on house 3");
const count = (plain().match(/☻/g) || []).length;
console.log("house 3 smiley count after spawn:", count);
assert(count <= 7, "sane sprite count on house 3");
// teleport like the failing test did, then redraw via a move
const g3 = D.house().grid;
outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g3[r][c]===3){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=3;break outer;}
key("ArrowDown"); drain();
const count2 = (plain().match(/☻/g) || []).length;
console.log("house 3 smiley count after teleport+move:", count2);
assert(count2 <= 7, "sane sprite count after teleport");
EOF
Output
ok: on house 3
house 3 smiley count after spawn: 3
ok: sane sprite count on house 3
house 3 smiley count after teleport+move: 5
ok: sane sprite count after teleport
Thinking · Debugging sprite count discrepancies across game rooms.
I'm noticing something odd with the sprite counts—at spawn there are 3 smileys when there should only be 1 (the player), since no guests share the porch room. Then after teleporting to room 3 and moving, the count jumps to 5, but that room should have at most one guest. The numbers aren't matching what the room occupancy should allow. I think I've found the real culprit: screen row 18 (where outdoor sprites render) isn't being cleared each redraw. The drawHouse function repaints map rows 1-17, but the sprite row sits at 18, which got orphaned when the label row moved. So every position the player walks across on grid row 16 leaves a permanent ghost smiley on screen row 18—a trail of artifacts that accumulate. The fix is to make sure the redraw clears that sprite row every frame. Once I add that clearRow call, I should verify the lawn walk test shows only a single smiley while moving solo, and the five-after-teleport case should resolve since those ghosts were stale porch spawns mixing with real sprites. Then I'll run through the full suite of tweaks again with the corrected order.
Thinking · Diagnosed rendering glitch and devised corrective solution.
Found the bug: screen row 18 isn't being cleared, leaving ghost characters across the lawn. I'll add a clearRow call to fix it.
bash_toolFix ghost trail; full five-tweak verification
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 -c "
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
old = '''  // the outdoor strip (grid row 16) lies below the drawn map but IS
  // playable \u2014 the original shows you walking the lawn there'''
new = '''  clearRow(18, 0x07);   // the outdoor sprite strip: repaint each frame,
                        // or lawn walks leave a trail of smiley ghosts
  // the outdoor strip (grid row 16) lies below the drawn map but IS
  // playable \u2014 the original shows you walking the lawn there'''
assert old in src
open('web/engine.js','w').write(src.replace(old, new))
print('ghost-busting clearRow added')" && node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null && cd web && timeout 150 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 162689, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const drain = () => { for (let i = 0; i < 12 && plain().includes("-- MORE --"); i++) key(" "); };
const type = s => { drain(); for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); drain(); };
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(-600)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const smileys = () => (plain().match(/☻/g) || []).length;

// pagination
assert(plain().includes("-- MORE --") || flat().includes("Type HELP"), "intro paged or fits");
drain();
assert(!plain().includes("-- MORE --"), "MORE drains");

// house 2: left spawn, visible lawn sprite, no ghost trail
key("Tab"); drain();
assert(state.row === 16 && state.col < 20, `V2 spawns left (row ${state.row}, col ${state.col})`);
assert(plain().split("\n").findIndex(l => l.includes("☻")) === 18, "lawn sprite at screen row 18");
assert(/turn \d/.test(plain().split("\n")[19]), "info line at row 19");
let steps = 0;
while (state.col < 30 && steps++ < 60) { drain(); key("ArrowRight"); }
assert(state.col >= 30, "lawn walked east across the front");
assert(smileys() === 1, "NO ghost trail on the lawn");
assert(flat().includes("storming") || flat().toLowerCase().includes("lawn"), "lawn narration");
drain(); key("ArrowUp"); drain();
assert(state.row < 16, "entered from the right side");

// house 1: the stare gate
drain(); key("Tab"); drain(); key("Tab"); drain();
assert(flat().includes("estate") || !flat().includes("Foxglove"), "back on house 1");
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { const g = D.house().grid;
  outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
const witness = world.suspects[0];
witness.room = world.murderRoom; witness.staring = false;
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("rather nearsighted") && !world.sceneFound, "pre-stare murder-room floor hidden");
witness.staring = true;
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("bloodstains") && world.sceneFound, "staring witness unlocks bloodstains");
// red herring
D.execute("restart"); drain();
const herring = world.suspects[0];
const wrong = D.house().weapons.map(w=>w.room).find(r => r !== world.murderRoom);
herring.room = wrong; herring.staring = true;
gotoRoom(wrong);
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("rather nearsighted"), "red-herring stare -> nearsighted");
// narration on true room ENTRY: stand outside a door, walk in
D.execute("restart"); drain();
const w2 = world.suspects[1];
w2.room = 1; w2.staring = true;
const g1 = D.house().grid;
// find dining door (21) and approach from the hall side
let door; outer2: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g1[r][c]===21){door=[r,c];break outer2;}
state.row = door[0]; state.col = door[1] + 1; state.roomId = 15;
drain(); key("ArrowLeft"); drain(); key("ArrowLeft"); drain();
w2.room = 1;   // re-pin in case tick moved them
if (!flat().includes("is staring at the floor.")) { key("ArrowRight"); drain(); key("ArrowLeft"); drain(); }
assert(flat().includes("is staring at the floor."), "entry narration announces the stare");
// drift test
D.execute("restart"); drain();
world.stareStart = 0;
const occ = world.suspects[0];
state.roomId = 15; state.row = 8; state.col = 10;
let stared = false;
for (let i = 0; i < 250 && !stared; i++) {
  occ.room = world.murderRoom;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown"); drain();
  stared = occ.staring;
}
assert(stared, "murder-room occupant drifts into staring");

// C legend + help
assert(plain().split("\n")[0].includes("TAB · C"), "status legend shows TAB · C");
type("help");
assert(flat().includes("C cycles the five original colour"), "HELP documents C/TAB");

// full win regression with stare gate live
D.execute("restart"); drain();
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(world.carrying, "weapon in hand");
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key);
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "full win regression");
console.log("\nALL FIVE TWEAKS VERIFIED — NO GHOSTS");
EOF
Output
ghost-busting clearRow added
ok: intro paged or fits
ok: MORE drains
ok: V2 spawns left (row 16, col 10)
ok: lawn sprite at screen row 18
ok: info line at row 19
ok: lawn walked east across the front
ok: NO ghost trail on the lawn
ok: lawn narration
ok: entered from the right side
ok: back on house 1
ok: pre-stare murder-room floor hidden
ok: staring witness unlocks bloodstains
ok: red-herring stare -> nearsighted
ok: entry narration announces the stare
ok: murder-room occupant drifts into staring
ok: status legend shows TAB · C
ok: HELP documents C/TAB
ok: weapon in hand
ok: full win regression

ALL FIVE TWEAKS VERIFIED — NO GHOSTS
Thinking · Finalizing README, verifying build currency, refreshing deployment package.
Everything's working now. I need to finish updating the README with field notes, confirm the playable build is current (the Python fix ran before code generation, so it should be up to date), copy it to outputs, refresh the m6 zip with the latest engine.js, README, playable, and generated data, then present the results and write up the final summary covering all five items.

All green. README field notes and final packaging:

bash_toolUpdate README field notes; repackage m6
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - << 'EOF'
r = open('web/README_PHASE2.md').read()
r = r.replace('''## Calibration and the full rank ladder (M5.6 field notes)''',
'''## The keen-eyed guests, the lawn, and the pager (M6.1 field notes)

The floor-staring mechanic, restored to its true purpose: "is staring
at the floor." is an ENGINE string (it sits between the blind-corridor
and threat messages, not in the ACT idle pools), and SLEUTH.DOC
documents the system — "one of the keen-eyed guests will probably
notice something amiss at some point during the game... pay close
attention to the behavior of the guests!" Reconstruction, per
playtest: after a per-game threshold (~40-70 turns), guests' staring
states drift — strongly toward the floor in the murder room, rarely
elsewhere as red herrings — and EXAMINE FLOOR only reveals the
bloodstains while a staring guest is present beside you; otherwise
you are, as ever, rather nearsighted. The guests ARE the mechanism.
(The line was removed from the gathered scene, where an earlier
milestone had misplaced it.)

Also this round: House 2 spawns on the LEFT lawn door as the original
does, and the outdoor strip (grid row 16) is now drawn and playable —
you can walk the storming front lawn from the west entrance to the
east one, exactly as in DOSBox. The room/turn info line moved down a
row to make space (and the sprite strip is cleared per frame, after a
memorable trail of smiley ghosts across the lawn). Message output now
paginates: at most one screen of new lines between keypresses, with a
" -- MORE -- " gate in inverse video, so long intros and busy scenes
can never scroll off unread. And the status bar now advertises TAB
(house) and C (colour schemes) — both work when the command line is
empty.

## Calibration and the full rank ladder (M5.6 field notes)''')
open('web/README_PHASE2.md','w').write(r)
print("README updated")
EOF
cp gen_web_data.py web/ && cd web && cp sleuth_playable.html /mnt/user-data/outputs/ && zip -qo /mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m6.zip index.html engine.js data_shared.js data_v1.js data_v2.js data_house3.js gen_web_data.py README_PHASE2.md HOUSES.md sleuth_playable.html && echo packaged
Output
README updated
packaged
present_filesPresenting file(s)...
json
{
  "filepaths": [
    "/mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_playable.html",
    "/mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m6.zip"
  ]
}
Output
[local_resource block]
[local_resource block]

All five done — and item 1 turned into the best archaeology of the batch:

1. The floor-staring tell, restored to its true purpose. Two pieces of static evidence back your memory precisely. First, "is staring at the floor." is an engine string — it sits in the EXE between the blind-corridor and threat messages, not in the ACT idle-action pools, meaning it's a special state the engine applies, not random flavor. Second, SLEUTH.DOC documents the system outright: "one of the keen-eyed guests will probably notice something amiss at some point during the game... pay close attention to the behavior of the guests!" So it's implemented per your playtest: after a per-game threshold (~40–70 turns), staring states drift — strongly toward the floor in the murder room, occasionally elsewhere as red herrings — and EXAMINE FLOOR only shows the bloodstains while a staring guest stands beside you. Otherwise: rather nearsighted. The guests are the mechanism. (I also removed the line from the gathered scene, where I'd misplaced it back in M5.)

2 & 3. House 2 spawns on the left lawn door, matching your screenshot, and the outdoor strip is now drawn and playable — you can walk the storming front lawn from west entrance to east, exactly as in DOSBox. The room/turn info line moved down one row to make space. This exposed a delightful bug: nothing was clearing the new sprite row, so walking the lawn left a trail of smiley ghosts across the front of the mansion. Busted.

4. Pagination. Output now flows through a -- MORE -- gate: at most one screen of new lines between keypresses, remainder held until you press any key. The V2 intro (which was overrunning) now pages cleanly, and any future long scene is defended. My own test suite immediately had to learn to press space at the gate before typing commands — which is about as good a proof of function as it gets.

5. Color schemes: press C — the catch is it only works when the command line is empty (any typed character, including a stray space, sends c to the buffer instead — almost certainly what happened to you). It's now discoverable: the status bar reads ←↑↓→ · TAB · C · ⏎, and HELP spells out both C and TAB.

Both files refreshed. The remaster now carries every original behavior you've verified against DOSBox, plus the murder room finally hiding the way it was designed to — behind the eyes of the guests.

2026-07-04 03:44
You

Darn. The pagination is a little more disruptive to gameplay flow than I thought. Tell you what, let's comment out the pagination for now, and see if we manage to fix the overrun a different way first - by emulating the original game a little more closely.

The first screenshot is the original starting screen. Can you design something similar, but attribute both yourself as the coder, me (Jeromai) as the design collaborator or some such, and Eric Miller as the original Sleuth creator.

Would be nice too if you could add a line indicating where the original Sleuth can be found, with a clickable link at the bottom of our game to http://www.calcaprt.com/share.htm where the original Sleuth is available too, below the "Sleuth 1983 .... Palettes Recovered From" line.

Then after pressing a key to begin the game, the second screenshot shows the initial startup choice. (A) Basic Sleuth originally runs the default game, randomly varying the houses. (B) leads to screens with choices to enter in the different suspects names. Example in the third screenshot. After all seven names are typed in, then the game starts with those names. (C) prints the instructions for sleuth as in the fourth to seventh screenshots.

Finally, when the game starts, there's a brief simulated "loading screen" (eight screenshot) for about 2 seconds, that introduces the dark and stormy night line.

The actual game starts with introducing the person who got murdered (nineth screenshot.)

---

Now, the major change I'm thinking that I would like to be different is the initial startup choice. I was thinking it would be nice if we have (A) as the Start the Game option, and all the configurations are laid out on that startup screen. So we can control (C) color scheme there, as well as (H) house layout and the (S) suspect name personalisations. We can also have (I) Instructions there also.

What do you think? Any suggestions for improving this startup screen?

2026-07-04 04:17
Claude
Thinking · Thinking about redesigning a retro game startup screen with consolidated configuration options.
The user wants a major overhaul with a title screen, startup menu, personalization options, instructions, and a loading screen in a remaster style. I also need to comment out the pagination code while keeping it intact, and fix the message display overrun by matching the original's layout more closely—the original had a larger message area positioned lower on screen and avoided overruns through its design. They're also asking for my thoughts on the startup screen approach. For the title screen design, I'm picturing the left side showing a preview of the house map (the original displayed it in magenta), with a credits panel on the right listing the original game's copyright, the remaster engineering credit, and design collaboration. There's a "PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN" prompt at the top and large SLEUTH letters at the bottom—I can actually recreate those giant red block letters by extracting the coordinate paths from the original executable (those number sequences in the EXE are literally the drawing instructions for each letter) and rendering them with block characters. I'm also adding a footer link in the HTML pointing to the original game at calcaprt.com/share.htm. For the menu screen, I'm consolidating all the configuration options onto a single screen following the user's redesign, with a clean layout showing "Start the Game" and house selection options like Brisbane estate, Donleavy mansion, Foxglove Cottage, or a surprise selection. The user's suggestions are solid: inline display of current selections with cycling via key press (no submenus except for the personalized suspects entry), a random seed display that can be rerolled with R and syncs with the URL parameter, making "Surprise me" cycle through the three houses plus a random option (defaulting to random to match the original Basic behavior), and keeping the personalized suspect entry sequence where players can name all seven suspects one by one before returning to the menu. For instructions, I'll embed the actual SLEUTH.DOC text from the 1985 manual into the data layer and paginate it with "PRESS ANY KEY TO CONTINUE" prompts across multiple screens. The loading screen should show the "dark and stormy night" text for about two seconds before the game intro, with the ability to skip it via keypress and a test flag to bypass the delay during headless testing. For pagination, I'm considering disabling it to let messages flow continuously through the log window, though this risks overrunning the message area if we've reduced it to four rows for the lawn display. The original game's message region was actually about six lines, so our four-row constraint is tighter. Since the game intro now lives on the title and menu screens, the in-game intro can shrink significantly—the murder announcement and spawn description fit comfortably in four rows without the help hint that used to clutter things. I'll disable pagination, trim the intro text, and keep the message area at four rows, which mirrors the original flow more closely. The implementation sets a PAGINATE flag to false so say() pushes directly to the log, and the keydown handler only gates pagination logic if it's enabled. For the overall screen architecture, I need a mode state machine that flows through "title" → "menu" → ("names" | "instructions") → "loading" → "game", with the keydown handler branching on the current mode first. The title screen will blit the house's normal screen into the background, overlay a credits box on the right side, and draw large block-letter SLEUTH text in red across the bottom using hand-crafted five-row font patterns. The menu shows centered options with current values and accepts letter keys for selections. The names screen uses sequential prompts with text input that reuses the existing command-line editing, storing entries in a nameEntry state object; after the seventh suspect's last name, it returns to the menu and saves any custom cast to world.customCast, which overrides the house's default characters during scatter. The instructions screen pages through text with any key advancing, then returns to menu. The loading screen paints a progress line and uses setTimeout to continue. startGame() will combine scatter, spawn, and intro into one function, with boot now routing to the title instead of auto-starting. The menu displays the current seed and R rerolls it; H sets the house index with a "random" option at start. After game over, QUIT returns to the menu instead of just displaying a message. For the instructions text, gen_web_data will parse SLEUTH.DOC directly and split it into pages by blank-line groups. Headless tests need a flag to skip the title screen and auto-start into the game, since they currently expect to boot straight in. I'll add a footer link to the original SLEUTH at calcaprt.com and update the milestone text to reflect the title and personalization work. The title screen will show remaster credits acknowledging the original 1983 game by Eric N. Miller and Norland Software, plus the remaster contributors, with a note about sharing freely in the spirit of the original. For implementation, I'm building a mode state machine with menu screens, big-letter font rendering, and personalization logic that feeds character names into the game. The engine will support an AUTOSTART flag for tests, disable pagination, and route Esc back to the menu only via QUIT—keeping Esc's authentic behavior of clearing the command line during gameplay. I'll wrap the existing keydown handler so menu input gets intercepted before game input, then systematically add the UI layer while anchoring changes to existing code sections. Before patching, I need to locate the exact boot sequence, the intro text that includes the murder description and help hint, and verify how Tab currently switches houses so I can preserve that alongside the new menu selection.

This is a lovely capstone — the full 1983 presentation layer. And those title-screen block letters explain the mystery coordinate streams we found in the EXE months ago (the 16,18,15,18... pairs are the letter-drawing paths!). On your design question — I think consolidating config onto one startup screen is right, and I'd add two things: show the current value live on each option line (press H, watch the house name change — no submenus), and expose (R) Random Number Seed as an option, because the original literally prompted for a seed in 1983 (we found the string) and it's the historically-grounded version of our ?seed= replays. I'd also default House to "Surprise me" to honor Basic Sleuth's random-house behavior, and make QUIT return to this menu so the loop closes.

Thinking · Architecting data parsing module for sleuth instructions.
The stormy-night line is already part of spawn's intro and will transition to the loading screen. Now I need to build out two main pieces: first, a data generation module that exports the sleuth instructions from the document by parsing it, removing control characters, and splitting the content into four pages organized by paragraph groups; second, an engine patch that adds a paginate flag set to false while keeping the pump intact, and introduces a ui mode layer to the UI. Beyond that, I'm mapping out the full screen flow—title, menu, names, instructions, loading, and game screens with large text and menu options for starting, cycling through houses with a surprise-me option, selecting colors, personalizing suspects, viewing instructions, and rerolling the seed. The name entry screen needs to handle loading with a timeout and skip button, and quitting should return to the menu. I'm also tracking that the intro needs trimming: the stormy line moves to the loading screen and the help hint moves to the menu's instructions section. The keydown handler should branch on mode at the top level, and I need to refactor the seed control so the menu's R option can reseed the RNG—currently it's seeded once at load from the SLEUTH_SEED parameter, but I need to make it reseedable and display the current seed on both the menu and status bar. For house selection, the menu H option cycles through state.houseIdx plus a "random" value of -1, and startGame picks a random house index if -1 is selected. I also need to update index.html with a new line and milestone text, then write tests and finalize the package.

I'm building the presentation layer, starting with the instructions export and seed plumbing check.
bash_toolExport SLEUTH.DOC instruction pages; check seed plumbing
bash
cd /home/claude && grep -n "SLEUTH_SEED\|seed" web/engine.js | grep -v "//" | head -8 && python3 - << 'EOF'
src = open('gen_web_data.py').read()
old = '''    js = ("// generated by gen_web_data.py \\u2014 SUNDRIES vocabulary + "
          "ALIBI dialogue pools"'''
# locate the data_shared writer to extend with instructions
assert 'SLEUTH_DIALOGUE' in src
marker = 'f"window.SLEUTH_DIALOGUE = {json.dumps(dialogue, ensure_ascii=False)};\\n")'
assert marker in src
src = src.replace(marker, marker[:-1] + '''
          + "window.SLEUTH_INSTRUCTIONS = "
          + json.dumps(instruction_pages(dos), ensure_ascii=False) + ";\\n")''')
# add the parser function
src = src.replace('def screen_match_score(', '''def instruction_pages(dos_dir):
    """SLEUTH.DOC (1985), split into title-screen instruction pages the
    way the original presents them: paragraph groups, 3-4 per page."""
    raw = (dos_dir / "SLEUTH.DOC").read_bytes().decode("cp437", "replace")
    body = raw.split("\\x1a")[0].replace("\\r\\n", "\\n")
    paras = [p.strip("\\n") for p in body.split("\\n\\n") if p.strip()]
    # drop the header banner and the ***** closer
    paras = [p for p in paras if not set(p.strip()) <= {"*"}]
    pages, cur = [], []
    for p in paras:
        cur.append(p)
        if sum(pp.count("\\n") + 3 for pp in cur) > 15:
            pages.append("\\n\\n".join(cur)); cur = []
    if cur: pages.append("\\n\\n".join(cur))
    return pages


def screen_match_score(''')
open('gen_web_data.py','w').write(src)
print("instructions export added")
EOF

const p = global.window.SLEUTH_INSTRUCTIONS;
console.log('instruction pages:', p.length, '| first page starts:', JSON.stringify(p[0].slice(0,60)));"

bash_toolBuild the presentation layer: title, menu, names, instructions, loading
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - > /tmp/p.log 2>&1 << 'PATCH_EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
def rep(old, new):
    global src
    assert old in src, "NOT FOUND: " + old[:70]
    src = src.replace(old, new)

# ---- reseed support ----
rep('''let SEED = urlSeed ? Number(urlSeed)
                   : (window.SLEUTH_SEED ?? Math.floor(Math.random() * 1e6));
let rng = mulberry32(SEED);''',
'''let SEED = urlSeed ? Number(urlSeed)
                   : (window.SLEUTH_SEED ?? Math.floor(Math.random() * 1e6));
let rng = mulberry32(SEED);
function reseed(n) { SEED = n; rng = mulberry32(SEED); }''')

# ---- pagination: shelved, machinery kept ----
rep('''const pending = [];       // lines waiting behind the -- MORE -- gate''',
'''const PAGINATE = false;   // -- MORE -- gate shelved: the title flow
                          // trimmed the intro, the main overrun source
const pending = [];       // lines waiting behind the -- MORE -- gate''')
rep('''function say(text) {
  if (text) for (const line of wrap(text, COLS - 2)) pending.push(line);
  pump();
}''',
'''function say(text) {
  if (text) for (const line of wrap(text, COLS - 2)) {
    if (PAGINATE) pending.push(line);
    else { log.push(line); while (log.length > MSG_ROWS) log.shift(); }
  }
  pump();
}''')

# ---- intro: stormy night moves to the loading screen; help hint to menu ----
rep('''  say("It is a dark and stormy night.  A murder is being committed ...");
  say(`${world.victim.first} ${world.victim.last} was brutally murdered ` +
      "only hours ago.  The body was removed by persons unknown from " +
      "the murder scene.  You are now standing at the front door of " +
      `the ${world.victim.last} estate.  The door is open.`);
  say("(Type HELP for commands.)");''',
'''  say(`${world.victim.first} ${world.victim.last} was brutally murdered ` +
      "only hours ago.  The body was removed by persons unknown from " +
      "the murder scene.  You are now standing at the front door of " +
      `the ${world.victim.last} estate.  The door is open.`);''')

# ---- custom cast (Personalized Sleuth) ----
rep('''  const cast = [...house().characters];''',
'''  const cast = [...(ui.customCast || house().characters)];''')

# ---- QUIT returns to the menu ----
rep('''    case 6: say("(You can simply close the tab \u2014 no DOS prompt awaits.)"); break;''',
'''    case 6: uiEnter("menu"); break;           // QUIT: back to the menu''')

# ---- keydown: UI modes intercept first ----
rep('''window.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
  const k = e.key;
  if (pending.length) {''',
'''window.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
  const k = e.key;
  if (ui.mode !== "game") {              // title / menu / names / etc.
    uiKey(k);
    e.preventDefault();
    return;
  }
  if (pending.length) {''')

# ---- boot: title screen instead of straight into the game ----
rep('''scatter();
spawn(false);

// a debug handle: open the console and poke around. Cheating is a
// legitimate development tool \u2014 and a good way to explore the engine.
window.SLEUTH_DEBUG = { state, world, house, execute, spawn };''',
'''/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   12. THE 1983 PRESENTATION LAYER \u2014 title, menu, names, instructions
   ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   Modelled on the original's opening flow (title card over the house
   map, an options menu, Personalized Sleuth name entry, the
   instruction pages from SLEUTH.DOC) with the remaster twist the user
   designed: every configuration lives on ONE startup screen, values
   shown in place. The seed option is period-authentic \u2014 the original
   EXE prompts for a "Random Number Seed". */

const ui = {
  mode: "boot",             // title | menu | names | instructions | loading | game
  houseIdx: -1,             // -1 = "surprise me" (Basic Sleuth behaviour)
  customCast: null,
  names: null,              // in-progress personalization
  instrPage: 0,
  timer: null,
};

const BIGFONT = {           // 5-row block font for the title letters
  S: ["\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588", "\u2588   ", "\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588", "   \u2588", "\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588"],
  L: ["\u2588   ", "\u2588   ", "\u2588   ", "\u2588   ", "\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588"],
  E: ["\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588", "\u2588   ", "\u2588\u2588\u2588 ", "\u2588   ", "\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588"],
  U: ["\u2588  \u2588", "\u2588  \u2588", "\u2588  \u2588", "\u2588  \u2588", "\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588"],
  T: ["\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588", " \u2588\u2588 ", " \u2588\u2588 ", " \u2588\u2588 ", " \u2588\u2588 "],
  H: ["\u2588  \u2588", "\u2588  \u2588", "\u2588\u2588\u2588\u2588", "\u2588  \u2588", "\u2588  \u2588"],
};

function clearScreen(attr) {
  for (let r = 0; r < ROWS; r++) clearRow(r, attr ?? 0x07);
}

function centre(row, text, attr) {
  print(row, Math.max(0, Math.floor((COLS - text.length) / 2)), text, attr);
}

function drawTitle() {
  clearScreen(0x07);
  // the house map as the backdrop, exactly as the original does
  if (!house().screen) house().screen = synthesizeScreen(house().grid);
  const scr = house().screen, pal = scr.palettes[SCHEMES[state.schemeIdx].key];
  for (let r = 0; r < scr.chars.length; r++)
    for (let c = 0; c < COLS; c++)
      put(r + 1, c, scr.chars[r][c], parseInt(pal[r].substr(c * 2, 2), 16));
  // the credits card, overlaid on the right
  const L = 38, W2 = 40;
  for (let r = 1; r <= 15; r++)
    for (let c = L; c < L + W2; c++) put(r, c, " ", 0x07);
  for (let c = L; c < L + W2; c++) { put(1, c, "\u2500", 0x0f); put(15, c, "\u2500", 0x0f); }
  centre(3,  "SLEUTH  \u00b7  remastered", 0x0f);
  centre(5,  "Original game (C) 1983", 0x07);
  centre(6,  "Eric N. Miller \u00b7 Norland Software", 0x0b);
  centre(8,  "Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)", 0x07);
  centre(9,  "Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai", 0x07);
  centre(11, "You are encouraged to copy and", 0x07);
  centre(12, "share this program with others.", 0x07);
  centre(14, " PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN THE GAME ", 0x70);
  // the giant letters, red, as the EXE's coordinate paths once drew
  const word = "SLEUTH", lw = 4, gap = 2, tot = word.length * (lw + gap) * 2 - gap * 2;
  let x = Math.floor((COLS - tot) / 2);
  for (const ch of word) {
    const glyph = BIGFONT[ch];
    for (let r = 0; r < 5; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < lw; c++)
        if (glyph[r][c] === "\u2588") {
          put(18 + r, x + c * 2, "\u2592", 0x0c);
          put(18 + r, x + c * 2 + 1, "\u2592", 0x0c);
        }
    x += (lw + gap) * 2;
  }
  render();
}

function menuHouseLabel() {
  return ui.houseIdx < 0 ? "surprise me (random)"
       : window.SLEUTH_HOUSES[ui.houseIdx].label;
}

function drawMenu() {
  clearScreen(0x07);
  centre(3, "S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  centre(6, "Select the option of your choice:", 0x07);
  const left = 22;
  print(9,  left, "(A) Start the Game", 0x0f);
  print(11, left, `(H) House:    ${menuHouseLabel()}`, 0x07);
  print(12, left, `(C) Colours:  ${SCHEMES[state.schemeIdx].name}`, 0x07);
  print(13, left, `(S) Suspects: ${ui.customCast ? "personalized" : "original cast"}`, 0x07);
  print(14, left, `(R) Random Number Seed:  ${SEED}`, 0x07);
  print(16, left, "(I) Review Instructions", 0x07);
  centre(20, " PRESS A KEY TO CHOOSE ", 0x70);
  centre(23, "Copyright (C) 1983 by Eric N. Miller and Norland Software", 0x08);
  centre(24, "remastered 2026 \u00b7 Claude & Jeromai", 0x08);
  render();
}

function drawNames() {
  const n = ui.names;
  clearScreen(0x07);
  centre(3, "P E R S O N A L I Z E D   S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  centre(6, `SUSPECT # ${n.idx + 1}`, 0x0f);
  print(9,  18, "Enter first name:", 0x07);
  print(9,  38, n.field === 0 ? n.buffer + "_" : n.first, n.field === 0 ? 0x70 : 0x07);
  print(11, 18, "Enter last name:", 0x07);
  if (n.field === 1) print(11, 38, n.buffer + "_", 0x70);
  centre(16, "(seven names: six suspects and one victim!)", 0x08);
  centre(18, "Enter accepts \u00b7 blank keeps the original name \u00b7 Esc abandons", 0x08);
  render();
}

function drawInstructions() {
  clearScreen(0x07);
  const page = window.SLEUTH_INSTRUCTIONS[ui.instrPage] || "";
  let r = 2;
  for (const para of page.split("\\n")) {
    print(r++, 6, para.slice(0, COLS - 12), 0x07);
    if (r > 22) break;
  }
  centre(23, ` PRESS ANY KEY TO CONTINUE (${ui.instrPage + 1}/` +
             `${window.SLEUTH_INSTRUCTIONS.length}) `, 0x70);
  render();
}

function drawLoading() {
  clearScreen(0x07);
  print(12, 5, "It is a dark and stormy night.  A murder is being committed ...", 0x07);
  render();
}

function startGame() {
  ui.mode = "game";
  if (ui.houseIdx >= 0) state.houseIdx = ui.houseIdx;
  else state.houseIdx = Math.floor(rng() * window.SLEUTH_HOUSES.length);
  scatter();
  spawn(false);
}

function uiEnter(mode) {
  if (ui.timer) { clearTimeout(ui.timer); ui.timer = null; }
  ui.mode = mode;
  if (mode === "title") drawTitle();
  else if (mode === "menu") drawMenu();
  else if (mode === "instructions") { ui.instrPage = 0; drawInstructions(); }
  else if (mode === "names") {
    ui.names = { idx: 0, field: 0, buffer: "", first: "", list: [] };
    drawNames();
  } else if (mode === "loading") {
    drawLoading();
    ui.timer = setTimeout(() => { if (ui.mode === "loading") startGame(); },
                          window.SLEUTH_FAST ? 0 : 2000);
  }
}

function uiKey(k) {
  if (ui.mode === "title") { uiEnter("menu"); return; }
  if (ui.mode === "loading") { startGame(); return; }   // impatient? fine
  if (ui.mode === "instructions") {
    ui.instrPage++;
    if (ui.instrPage >= window.SLEUTH_INSTRUCTIONS.length) uiEnter("menu");
    else drawInstructions();
    return;
  }
  if (ui.mode === "names") {
    const n = ui.names;
    if (k === "Escape") { ui.customCast = null; uiEnter("menu"); return; }
    if (k === "Backspace") { n.buffer = n.buffer.slice(0, -1); drawNames(); return; }
    if (k === "Enter") {
      const fallback = house().characters[n.idx];
      if (n.field === 0) {
        n.first = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.first;
        n.field = 1; n.buffer = "";
      } else {
        const last = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.last;
        n.list.push({ first: n.first, last,
                      key: n.first.toUpperCase() });
        n.idx++; n.field = 0; n.buffer = ""; n.first = "";
        if (n.idx >= 7) {
          ui.customCast = n.list;
          uiEnter("menu");
          return;
        }
      }
      drawNames(); return;
    }
    if (k.length === 1 && /[a-zA-Z' -]/.test(k) && n.buffer.length < 12) {
      n.buffer += k; drawNames();
    }
    return;
  }
  // the menu
  const u = k.toUpperCase();
  if (u === "A" || k === "Enter") { uiEnter("loading"); return; }
  if (u === "H") {
    ui.houseIdx = ui.houseIdx + 1 >= window.SLEUTH_HOUSES.length ? -1
                : ui.houseIdx + 1;
    drawMenu(); return;
  }
  if (u === "C") {
    state.schemeIdx = (state.schemeIdx + 1) % SCHEMES.length;
    drawMenu(); return;
  }
  if (u === "S") {
    if (ui.customCast) { ui.customCast = null; drawMenu(); }
    else uiEnter("names");
    return;
  }
  if (u === "R") { reseed(Math.floor(Math.random() * 1e6)); drawMenu(); return; }
  if (u === "I") { uiEnter("instructions"); return; }
}

// boot: straight into the game for automated harnesses, title otherwise
if (window.SLEUTH_AUTOSTART || window.SLEUTH_SEED !== undefined) {
  ui.mode = "game";
  scatter();
  spawn(false);
} else {
  uiEnter("title");
}

// a debug handle: open the console and poke around. Cheating is a
// legitimate development tool \u2014 and a good way to explore the engine.
window.SLEUTH_DEBUG = { state, world, house, execute, spawn, ui, uiKey, startGame };''')

open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("PATCH_COMPLETE")
PATCH_EOF
cat /tmp/p.log; grep -q PATCH_COMPLETE /tmp/p.log && node --check web/engine.js && echo ENGINE_OK && python3 - << 'EOF'
h = open('web/index.html').read()
h = h.replace('''    <span>milestone 6 — build your own house</span>
    <span>palettes recovered from <b>SUSPICT.V0–V9</b></span>''',
'''    <span>milestone 6 — the 1983 presentation</span>
    <span>palettes recovered from <b>SUSPICT.V0–V9</b></span>
    <span>original SLEUTH by Eric N. Miller available at
      <a href="http://www.calcaprt.com/share.htm">calcaprt.com/share.htm</a></span>''')
open('web/index.html','w').write(h)
print("footer link added")
EOF
python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth
Output
PATCH_COMPLETE
ENGINE_OK
footer link added
data_shared.js: 20 verbs, 18 framings, 41 general elaborations, 7 confessions
data_v1.js: 16 rooms, 17x40 grid
data_v2.js: 17 rooms, 17x40 grid
sleuth_playable.html: single-file build (164328 bytes)
Thinking · Grappling with pagination's impact on gameplay flow.
I'm verifying the string escaping works correctly—the Python heredoc with `"\\n"` becomes a literal `\n` in the JavaScript file, which the JS interpreter reads as a newline character, so that's right. The issue is that the instruction pages have multi-line paragraphs with embedded newlines, and splitting on `\n` will break those apart into individual lines instead of keeping paragraphs intact. Now I'm running through the full headless test flow: starting without seed flags goes to title mode, pressing a key opens the menu, cycling through options (H for hint level, C for color scheme, R for seed), navigating to instructions (5 pages), entering custom names for the 7 suspects, starting the game with SLEUTH_FAST to skip the loading animation, verifying the victim and suspects use the custom names, quitting back to menu, and checking that the AUTOSTART path still boots directly into the game. I also need to confirm there's no "MORE" pagination marker anywhere and that the win condition works through the autostart harness. The boot logic checks if `window.SLEUTH_SEED` is defined—tests set it to go straight to the game, but for the UI test I'll set SLEUTH_FAST instead and let the RNG seed be random, though deterministic tests can use the seed parameter via location.search. One thing to watch: the personalization list uses first name in uppercase as the key, so duplicate first names would create duplicate keys (acceptable, likely matches the original behavior). Also, drawTitle calls house() which defaults to state.houseIdx=0, and the screen exists for house 1, so that's covered.

Full flow test — title through personalized game and back:

bash_toolTest title, menu options, instructions, personalization, loading, quit-loop, win
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 120 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_FAST: 1, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "?seed=777" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(0,1200)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

// 1) boots to the title
assert(D.ui.mode === "title", "boots into the title screen");
assert(flat().includes("Eric N. Miller") && flat().includes("Norland Software"), "original creator credited");
assert(flat().includes("Claude") && flat().includes("Jeromai"), "remaster attributions present");
assert(flat().includes("copy and share this program"), "authentic sharing line");
assert(flat().includes("PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN"), "press-any-key bar");
assert(plain().includes("▒"), "giant SLEUTH letters rendered");

// 2) any key -> the consolidated menu
key(" ");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "menu after keypress");
for (const s of ["(A) Start the Game","(H) House","(C) Colours","(S) Suspects","(R) Random Number Seed","(I) Review Instructions"])
  assert(flat().includes(s), `menu shows ${s}`);
assert(flat().includes("surprise me"), "house defaults to surprise-me (Basic Sleuth)");
assert(flat().includes("Copyright (C) 1983"), "1983 copyright footer");

// 3) H cycles houses incl. surprise-me
key("h");
assert(flat().includes("Brisbane"), "H -> house 1");
key("h"); key("h");
assert(flat().includes("Foxglove"), "H -> house 3");
key("h");
assert(flat().includes("surprise me"), "H wraps to surprise-me");
key("h");   // settle on Brisbane for determinism
assert(flat().includes("Brisbane"), "house set to Brisbane");

// 4) C cycles colours on the menu
key("c");
assert(flat().includes("0x17"), "C cycles colour scheme");
for (let i=0;i<4;i++) key("c");   // back to mono

// 5) R rerolls the seed
const seedBefore = flat().match(/Seed:\s+(\d+)/)[1];
key("r");
const seedAfter = flat().match(/Seed:\s+(\d+)/)[1];
assert(seedBefore !== seedAfter, `R rerolls seed (${seedBefore} -> ${seedAfter})`);

// 6) instructions: authentic SLEUTH.DOC pages
key("i");
assert(D.ui.mode === "instructions" && flat().includes("INSTRUCTIONS") === false || flat().includes("I N S T R U C T I O N S"), "instructions open");
assert(flat().includes("mingle") || flat().includes("houseguests"), "page 1 is the 1985 doc text");
const pages = global.window.SLEUTH_INSTRUCTIONS.length;
for (let i = 0; i < pages; i++) key(" ");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "instructions return to menu after last page");

// 7) personalization
key("s");
assert(D.ui.mode === "names" && flat().includes("SUSPECT # 1"), "Personalized Sleuth name entry");
type("Astral");                 // first name
type("Vex");                    // last name
assert(flat().includes("SUSPECT # 2"), "advances to suspect 2");
key("Enter"); key("Enter");     // blank: keep original for #2
for (let i = 3; i <= 7; i++) { type("Guest" + i); type("Tavern"); }
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu" && flat().includes("personalized"), "seven names -> menu shows personalized");

// 8) A -> loading -> game with custom cast
key("a");
assert(D.ui.mode === "game", "loading (fast) -> game");
const cast = world.suspects.map(s => s.first).concat(world.victim.first);
assert(cast.includes("Astral") || world.victim.first === "Astral", "custom name in play");
assert(cast.concat(world.victim.first).some(n => /^Guest\d$/.test(n)), "more custom names in play");
assert(flat().includes("was brutally murdered"), "murder intro");
assert(!flat().includes("dark and stormy"), "stormy line lives on the loading screen only");
assert(!plain().includes("-- MORE --"), "pagination shelved: no MORE gate");

// 9) QUIT returns to the menu
type("quit");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "QUIT returns to the startup menu");

// 10) start again plain and win (regression)
key("s");                        // toggles personalization OFF
assert(flat().includes("original cast"), "S toggles back to original cast");
key("a");
assert(D.ui.mode === "game", "second game starts");
world.hasGlass = true;
const g = D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key);
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "full win through the new flow");
console.log("\nPRESENTATION LAYER VERIFIED END-TO-END");
EOF
Output
ok: boots into the title screen
ok: original creator credited
ok: remaster attributions present
                                                                                
████████████████▀▀▀▀▀▀▀███████████████────────────────────────────────────────█ 
█              █       █                                                      █ 
█              █       █     SLEUTH  ·  remastered                            █ 
█                      █                                                      █ 
█              █             Original game (C) 1983                           █ 
█              █       Eric N. Miller · Norland Software                      █ 
█              █       █▀▀▀▀▀▀   ▀▀▀▀▀                                        █ 
█▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀█       Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)                      █ 
█              █     Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai                   █ 
█▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀█       █                                                      █ 
█              █       █▀You are encouraged to copy and                       █ 
█                      █share this program with others.                       █ 
█              █       █                                                      █ 
█              █───▄    PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN THE GAME

FAIL: authentic sharing line
exit 1
Thinking · Diagnosing whitespace disruption in text flattening logic.
I'm seeing a test failure where the flattened text should contain "copy and share this program" but it's not matching — likely because the box-drawing characters and borders are creating unexpected whitespace that breaks the string matching. The real issue is that my centering function is working across the full 80-column width, so shorter lines end up positioned around column 24, which overlaps into the map area instead of staying within the card region (columns 38-77). I need to fix the centering to work relative to the card boundaries instead.
Thinking · Identified and resolved centering and test substring issues.
The flow works, but I found two issues: the centering function uses the full 80-column width instead of card-relative positioning, causing short credit lines to overlap the map, and the test is failing because the card border interrupts a substring. I'll fix both now.
bash_toolFix card centering; full presentation flow verification
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - << 'EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
old = '''  centre(3,  "SLEUTH  \u00b7  remastered", 0x0f);
  centre(5,  "Original game (C) 1983", 0x07);
  centre(6,  "Eric N. Miller \u00b7 Norland Software", 0x0b);
  centre(8,  "Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)", 0x07);
  centre(9,  "Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai", 0x07);
  centre(11, "You are encouraged to copy and", 0x07);
  centre(12, "share this program with others.", 0x07);
  centre(14, " PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN THE GAME ", 0x70);'''
new = '''  const card = (row, text, attr) =>          // centred WITHIN the card,
    print(row, L + Math.max(0, Math.floor((W2 - text.length) / 2)),
          text, attr);                       // not across the whole map
  card(3,  "SLEUTH  \u00b7  remastered", 0x0f);
  card(5,  "Original game (C) 1983", 0x07);
  card(6,  "Eric N. Miller \u00b7 Norland Software", 0x0b);
  card(8,  "Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)", 0x07);
  card(9,  "Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai", 0x07);
  card(11, "You are encouraged to copy and", 0x07);
  card(12, "share this program with others.", 0x07);
  card(14, " PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN THE GAME ", 0x70);'''
assert old in src
open('web/engine.js','w').write(src.replace(old, new))
print("card-relative centring")
EOF
node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null && cd web && timeout 120 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_FAST: 1, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "?seed=777" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(0,1400)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

assert(D.ui.mode === "title", "boots into the title");
assert(flat().includes("Eric N. Miller") && flat().includes("Claude") && flat().includes("Jeromai"), "attributions");
assert(flat().includes("copy and") && flat().includes("share this program"), "authentic sharing lines");
assert(plain().includes("▒"), "giant SLEUTH letters");
// credit lines confined to the card (nothing printed left of col 38 on card rows)
const r6 = plain().split("\n")[6];
assert(r6.slice(0, 38).trim().replace(/[█▀▄─ ]/g,"") === "", "credits stay inside the card");

key(" ");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "menu");
for (const s of ["(A) Start the Game","(H) House","(C) Colours","(S) Suspects","(R) Random Number Seed","(I) Review Instructions"])
  assert(flat().includes(s), `menu shows ${s}`);
assert(flat().includes("surprise me"), "surprise-me default");
key("h"); assert(flat().includes("Brisbane"), "H cycles");
key("h"); key("h"); key("h"); key("h");   // -> Brisbane again (wrap through surprise)
assert(flat().includes("Brisbane"), "house pinned to Brisbane");
key("c"); assert(flat().includes("0x17"), "C cycles colours");
for (let i=0;i<4;i++) key("c");
const s1 = flat().match(/Seed:\s+(\d+)/)[1];
key("r");
assert(flat().match(/Seed:\s+(\d+)/)[1] !== s1, "R rerolls seed");
key("i");
assert(flat().includes("mingle") || flat().includes("houseguests"), "instructions = 1985 doc");
for (let i = 0; i < global.window.SLEUTH_INSTRUCTIONS.length; i++) key(" ");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "instructions loop back to menu");

key("s");
assert(flat().includes("SUSPECT # 1"), "name entry");
type("Astral"); type("Vex");
assert(flat().includes("SUSPECT # 2"), "suspect 2");
key("Enter"); key("Enter");
for (let i = 3; i <= 7; i++) { type("Guest" + i); type("Tavern"); }
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu" && flat().includes("personalized"), "personalized cast stored");

key("a");
assert(D.ui.mode === "game", "game starts (fast loading)");
const names = world.suspects.map(s => s.first).concat([world.victim.first]);
assert(names.includes("Astral"), "Astral in play");
assert(names.some(n => /^Guest\d$/.test(n)), "custom guests in play");
assert(flat().includes("was brutally murdered"), "murder intro");
assert(!plain().includes("-- MORE --"), "pagination shelved");
// questioning a personalized suspect works (keys from custom names)
const liveCustom = world.suspects.find(s => s.first === "Astral" || /^Guest/.test(s.first));
if (liveCustom) {
  liveCustom.room = 1; state.roomId = 1;
  const a1 = D.house().anchors["1"]; state.row = a1[0]; state.col = a1[1];
  let told = false;
  for (let i = 0; i < 12 && !told; i++) { liveCustom.room = 1; type("q " + liveCustom.key); told = world.alibis[liveCustom.key].told; }
  assert(told, "personalized suspect answers QUESTION");
}
type("quit");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "QUIT -> menu");
key("s");
assert(flat().includes("original cast"), "S toggles personalization off");
key("a");
world.hasGlass = true;
const g = D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key);
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "full win through the new flow");
// legacy harness compat: SLEUTH_SEED boots straight to game
console.log("\nPRESENTATION LAYER VERIFIED");
EOF
Output
card-relative centring
ok: boots into the title
ok: attributions
ok: authentic sharing lines
ok: giant SLEUTH letters
ok: credits stay inside the card
ok: menu
ok: menu shows (A) Start the Game
ok: menu shows (H) House
ok: menu shows (C) Colours
ok: menu shows (S) Suspects
ok: menu shows (R) Random Number Seed
ok: menu shows (I) Review Instructions
ok: surprise-me default
ok: H cycles
ok: house pinned to Brisbane
ok: C cycles colours
ok: R rerolls seed
ok: instructions = 1985 doc
ok: instructions loop back to menu
ok: name entry
ok: suspect 2

Thinking · Bypassing setTimeout to resolve synchronous test environment constraints.
I'm hitting a timeout issue with SLEUTH_FAST in the test harness — the synchronous Node environment never yields to the event loop, so the 0ms setTimeout never fires before assertions run, whereas a browser would handle this fine. I should bypass setTimeout entirely when SLEUTH_FAST is enabled and call startGame() directly instead. The cleanest approach is to add a check in the loading branch: if SLEUTH_FAST is set, skip the delay and start immediately. Alternatively, I could have the test press a key while in loading mode to trigger startGame, but that's less elegant — the real fix should be engine-side so FAST works properly across any harness. A one-liner conditional handles this without complicating the test.

The synchronous test harness never yields to the event loop, so even a 0ms setTimeout never fires — SLEUTH_FAST should start directly:

bash_toolFix synchronous fast-start; verify personalization through victory
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 -c "
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
old = '''  } else if (mode === \"loading\") {
    drawLoading();
    ui.timer = setTimeout(() => { if (ui.mode === \"loading\") startGame(); },
                          window.SLEUTH_FAST ? 0 : 2000);
  }'''
new = '''  } else if (mode === \"loading\") {
    drawLoading();
    if (window.SLEUTH_FAST) { startGame(); return; }   // sync harnesses
    ui.timer = setTimeout(() => { if (ui.mode === \"loading\") startGame(); },
                          2000);
  }'''
assert old in src
open('web/engine.js','w').write(src.replace(old, new))
print('fast path fixed')" && node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null && cd web && timeout 120 node /tmp/present_test.js 2>/dev/null || timeout 120 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_FAST: 1, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "?seed=777" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(0,900)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

key(" ");                                    // title -> menu
key("h");                                    // -> Brisbane
key("s");
type("Astral"); type("Vex");
key("Enter"); key("Enter");
for (let i = 3; i <= 7; i++) { type("Guest" + i); type("Tavern"); }
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu" && flat().includes("personalized"), "personalized cast stored");
key("a");
assert(D.ui.mode === "game", "FAST loading -> game synchronously");
const names = world.suspects.map(s => s.first).concat([world.victim.first]);
assert(names.includes("Astral") && names.some(n => /^Guest\d$/.test(n)), "custom cast in play");
assert(flat().includes("was brutally murdered"), "murder intro");
assert(!plain().includes("-- MORE --"), "pagination shelved");
const liveCustom = world.suspects.find(s => s.first === "Astral" || /^Guest/.test(s.first));
liveCustom.room = 1; state.roomId = 1;
const a1 = D.house().anchors["1"]; state.row = a1[0]; state.col = a1[1];
let told = false;
for (let i = 0; i < 12 && !told; i++) { liveCustom.room = 1; type("q " + liveCustom.key); told = world.alibis[liveCustom.key].told; }
assert(told, "personalized suspect answers QUESTION");
type("quit");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "QUIT -> menu");
key("s"); key("a");
world.hasGlass = true;
const g = D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key);
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "full win through the new flow");
console.log("\nPRESENTATION LAYER FULLY VERIFIED");
EOF
