Node.js v22.22.2
exit 1
Thinking · Diagnosing undefined suspect filtering bug with duplicate keys.
There's a crash in generateAlibis where `innocents[3]` is undefined. The filter is supposed to remove the murderer from the suspects list, but with a custom cast where duplicate keys are possible, something's going wrong. I'm checking my test names — Astral, the original character at position 2, and Guest3 through Guest7 — they all have unique keys, so that shouldn't be the issue. Wait, I see it now. The murderer is picked from the raw cast before suspects are mapped and cloned, so the reference comparison `s !== world.murderer` should work, and filtering by key should leave 5 innocents. But the crash happens because innocents has fewer than 4 elements. The input filter rejects digits, so when "Guest3" through "Guest7" are typed, only "Guest" gets buffered for all five entries, creating five identical "GUEST" keys. When the filter removes the murderer by key, it's removing multiple suspects with that same key, leaving fewer innocents than expected. The fix is twofold: allow digits in the name input regex so "Guest3" stays intact, and add duplicate-key detection on first-name entry. If someone tries to enter a duplicate first name, reject it with a hint and keep them on that field until they pick a unique name. This prevents key collisions in the alibi lookup logic entirely. I should also add a defensive check in the alibi generation to assert that exactly five innocents are present before proceeding, throwing a clear error if validation somehow fails.
Thinking · Identified name filter bug causing duplicate suspect keys and devised validation fix.
The crash revealed that my name filter strips digits, creating five suspects with identical keys that breaks the alibi system. I'll fix this by allowing digits in names and requiring distinct first names at entry.
bash_toolAllow digits, reject duplicate names; full flow verification
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - << 'EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
old = '''    if (k === "Enter") {
      const fallback = house().characters[n.idx];
      if (n.field === 0) {
        n.first = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.first;
        n.field = 1; n.buffer = "";
      } else {'''
new = '''    if (k === "Enter") {
      const fallback = house().characters[n.idx];
      if (n.field === 0) {
        const cand = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.first;
        // first names are the parser's match keys: they must differ
        if (n.list.some((p) => p.key === cand.toUpperCase())) {
          n.dup = true; n.buffer = "";
          drawNames(); return;
        }
        n.dup = false;
        n.first = cand;
        n.field = 1; n.buffer = "";
      } else {'''
assert old in src
src = src.replace(old, new)
old2 = '''    if (k.length === 1 && /[a-zA-Z' -]/.test(k) && n.buffer.length < 12) {'''
new2 = '''    if (k.length === 1 && /[a-zA-Z0-9' -]/.test(k) && n.buffer.length < 12) {'''
assert old2 in src
src = src.replace(old2, new2)
old3 = '''  centre(18, "Enter accepts \u00b7 blank keeps the original name \u00b7 Esc abandons", 0x08);'''
new3 = '''  centre(18, "Enter accepts \u00b7 blank keeps the original name \u00b7 Esc abandons", 0x08);
  if (n.dup) centre(20, "(first names must be distinct \u2014 they are how the parser knows who's who)", 0x0c);'''
assert old3 in src
src = src.replace(old3, new3)
open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("digits allowed, duplicates rejected")
EOF
node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null && cd web && timeout 120 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_FAST: 1, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "?seed=777" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(0,900)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

key(" "); key("h");                          // menu, Brisbane
key("s");
type("Astral"); type("Vex");
type("Astral");                              // duplicate first name!
assert(flat().includes("must be distinct"), "duplicate first name rejected with hint");
assert(flat().includes("SUSPECT # 2"), "still on suspect 2");
type("Iguana"); type("Wyrm");
for (let i = 3; i <= 7; i++) { type("Guest" + i); type("Tavern"); }
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu" && flat().includes("personalized"), "seven distinct names accepted (digits ok)");
key("a");
assert(D.ui.mode === "game", "game starts");
const names = world.suspects.map(s => s.first).concat([world.victim.first]);
assert(names.includes("Astral") && names.includes("Iguana") && names.includes("Guest3"), "custom cast in play");
const live = world.suspects.find(s => /^Guest\d$/.test(s.first));
live.room = 1; state.roomId = 1;
const a1 = D.house().anchors["1"]; state.row = a1[0]; state.col = a1[1];
let told = false;
for (let i = 0; i < 12 && !told; i++) { live.room = 1; type("q " + live.key); told = world.alibis[live.key].told; }
assert(told, "digit-named suspect answers QUESTION");
type("quit");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "QUIT -> menu");
key("s"); key("a");
world.hasGlass = true;
const g = D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.key);
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "full win regression");
console.log("\nPRESENTATION LAYER FULLY VERIFIED");
EOF

Thinking · Orchestrating layout elements and positioning card text strategically.
The layout is coming together nicely — the map shows through on the left side, the credits card sits on the right, and those big dithered SLEUTH letters span the bottom in true original style. The card text positions itself right-of-center within the card bounds, creating that slightly offset look that mirrors how the card overlays the map in the original composition. Now I'm preparing the package with a README note and bundling everything together as a playable version plus a zip file with all the source files. For the wrap-up, I'll recap the design decisions I made, touch on how the seed option connects to the original's history, explain why pagination got shelved, detail the personalization approach with the distinct-names rule, and include the footer link.
Thinking · Synthesized packaging design with map, credits, and typographic elements.
I've created the final packaging design with a map element bleeding in from the left, a credits card, and dithered block letters.
bash_toolREADME notes; repackage m6 with presentation layer
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - << 'EOF'
r = open('web/README_PHASE2.md').read()
r = r.replace('''## The keen-eyed guests, the lawn, and the pager (M6.1 field notes)''',
'''## The 1983 presentation layer (M6.2 field notes)

The remaster now opens the way the original does: a title card over
the house map (credits: Eric N. Miller's 1983 original, Claude on
remaster code, Jeromai on design and playtest archaeology, plus the
original's own "You are encouraged to copy and share" line), giant
block letters, then a startup menu — with the remaster's twist that
every configuration lives on ONE screen, values shown in place:
(A) Start, (H) House incl. "surprise me" (Basic Sleuth's random-house
behaviour), (C) Colours, (S) Suspects with the full Personalized
Sleuth seven-name entry (first names must be distinct — they are the
parser's match keys), (R) Random Number Seed (period-authentic: the
original EXE prompts for one), and (I) the actual 1985 SLEUTH.DOC
instruction pages. Starting a game passes through the two-second
"dark and stormy night" card, verbatim from the EXE. QUIT returns to
the menu. The -- MORE -- pager is shelved (PAGINATE=false, machinery
kept): moving the intro text onto the title flow removed the main
overrun source. Headless harnesses set SLEUTH_AUTOSTART or
SLEUTH_SEED to skip the front door; SLEUTH_FAST skips the loading
delay synchronously.

## The keen-eyed guests, the lawn, and the pager (M6.1 field notes)''')
open('web/README_PHASE2.md','w').write(r)
print("README updated")
EOF
cp gen_web_data.py web/ && cd web && cp sleuth_playable.html /mnt/user-data/outputs/ && zip -qo /mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m6.zip index.html engine.js data_shared.js data_v1.js data_v2.js data_house3.js gen_web_data.py README_PHASE2.md HOUSES.md sleuth_playable.html && echo packaged

Output
[local_resource block]
[local_resource block]

The 1983 front door is installed. What you get on load now:

The title card — the house map as backdrop, credits box overlaid on the right, and giant dithered SLEUTH letters across the bottom (my homage to the coordinate-path letter-drawing we found fossilized in the EXE). Attribution reads: Original game (C) 1983, Eric N. Miller · Norland Software / Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic) / Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai — plus the original's own shareware blessing, "You are encouraged to copy and share this program with others," which felt right to keep verbatim. The footer plate below the game now links to calcaprt.com/share.htm where the original lives.

The consolidated startup menu, per your design with my two suggested additions: every option shows its current value in place and cycles on a keypress — (A) Start the Game, (H) House (defaulting to surprise me, honoring Basic Sleuth's random-house behavior), (C) Colours, (S) Suspects, (I) Instructions — plus (R) Random Number Seed, which turned out to be the most historically grounded option on the screen: the original EXE literally prompts for one. (I) shows the actual 1985 SLEUTH.DOC text, paginated with "PRESS ANY KEY TO CONTINUE" like the original's four screens. (S) runs the full Personalized Sleuth sequence — seven names, first/last, blank keeps the original name, Esc abandons — with one rule the testing forced: first names must be distinct, because they're the parser's match keys (my test entered five guests all named "Guest" and the alibi generator rightly had a breakdown; the menu now explains the rule politely). Starting a game passes through the two-second "It is a dark and stormy night. A murder is being committed ..." card — verbatim EXE string — and QUIT now returns to this menu, closing the loop.

Pagination is shelved, not deleted — PAGINATE = false with the machinery intact, exactly as you asked. And your instinct was right about the root cause: moving the stormy-night line and the HELP hint out of the in-game intro onto the title flow removed the main overrun source, so the message area breathes again without a gate.

One honest note for your DOSBox sessions: the title layout here is inspired-by rather than pixel-recovered — the original's magenta title map and letter placement come from code paths we haven't decoded, so I composed in its spirit instead. If it ever bothers you, screenshots like today's are exactly the calibration input that's fixed everything else. Enjoy the front door — it's been waiting since 1983 to ask for your seven names again.

2026-07-04 04:24
You

Very nice. A few more tweaks:

1) The title screen message is looking somewhat offset presently, per the first screenshot. Can we reshape it so that it fits over the right half of the house exactly?

2) The red Sleuth letters are pretty good, but very close to the house. Can we push it a row downwards so that there's some black space in between the red letters and the house? The base of the T also is a bit thick, can we half the width?

3) The original Sleuth by Eric N Miller message is making the whole sentence very long, let's start it on a new line.

4) For the startup screen and the suspects, I was thinking of offsetting it something like the second screenshot for the mockup. Push the options to the left. Align the choices so that it's easier to read. And include a suspect guest list that shows the current suspect variables, showing the default original cast at the start, and the personalized choices if personalisation is entered.

5) For the personalization of the suspects, let's add a third variable that is basically the nickname. If left blank, the default for it is the first name, otherwise, it's whatever's entered (to support names where the last name might be the commonly used form of address, or where a diminutive might be preferred, etc.) The nickname is used everywhere where just one name is used for the suspect, rather than the full name, and can also be used in the parser to accuse, besides the first name.

6) Let's also support one-word names by allowing the last name variable to be 'blank' if the user just types a space and presses enter.

7) Finally, there's an odd artifact I don't quite understand that's more strongly visible on the colored house schemes. Two thin black lines that run horizontally through the house. What's going on there? Is that fixable?

📎 1783140203074_image.png📎 1783141103312_image.png📎 1783141692700_image.png
2026-07-04 05:12
Claude
Thinking · Thinking about refining UI layout, text positioning, suspect customization system, and visual artifacts in a game interface.
I'm working through the positioning issues with the title card. The card region spans columns 38-77, but the text is centering too far to the right within it. Looking at the screenshot, the card doesn't align properly with the right half of the house—I need to check whether the house's right wing actually occupies columns 39-79 and adjust the card placement to sit exactly over that right half. I need to define the card region precisely to cover the right half of the screen without overlapping map fragments — setting it to columns 39-79 and rows 1-17, then drawing a clean single-line box border with the text positioned at specific rows (title at row 3, copyright info at rows 5-6, credits at rows 8-9 and 11-12, and the prompt at row 14). Now I'm adjusting the letter grid placement to rows 19-23 instead of 18-22 to create a proper gap, and refining the T stem width — the current four-character stem needs to be narrower to look more balanced, so I'm testing a two-character stem centered within the letter's grid width. For the footer, I need to restructure the HTML so the link appears on its own line, either by adding a second plate div or using flexbox properties to force a line break. The menu layout requires pushing options to the left side with values aligned at a fixed column around 34, and adding a bordered "Guest List" panel on the right showing the cast members from the selected house with their custom names. For the guest list, I'll display either the personalized cast if available, or the characters from the selected house—for surprise-me mode without personalization, I'll show house 1's cast with a note that it varies by house. Each entry formats as "number. First Last (nickname)" with the nickname defaulting to the first name for original characters. The menu positioning places the house/character/setting/role options starting at column 2 with labels padded to column 26 and values at column 28, while the guest list box occupies columns 46-78. For nicknames, I'm adding a third input field that stores the nickname alongside first and last names, defaulting to the first name if blank. Everywhere the engine displays a single name—in dialogue framings, seat announcements, refusals, accusations, and confessions—I'll use the nickname instead. The parser will match both the original first name key and a new uppercase nickname key so players can accuse or reference suspects by either name. For full name displays like "Maude Crompton is admiring," I'll keep the first and last name together, and the alibi statements will reference the partner by nickname for consistency. Now I'm doing a targeted pass through the engine to replace display-only `.first` references with a helper function that returns the nickname or falls back to the first name. I'll ensure every suspect and victim object has a nickname set at scatter time, then systematically update all the display sites—the intro text keeps the full name, the estate name handles blank last names, victim references in fillTokens and accusations switch to nicknames, and the full name helper trims extra spaces when the last name is blank. The suspects list and menu will also flow through the nickname field properly. For one-word names, I'm refining the logic so that if someone types just whitespace and hits Enter, it deliberately sets the last name to blank rather than falling back to the original—checking whether the buffer is empty versus whitespace-only to distinguish intentional blanks from no input. The two thin black horizontal lines across the map are likely a line-height rendering issue where certain rows have different span structures or character metrics that create gaps in the background, so I need to investigate which specific rows are causing the mismatch. ern background extends beyond the line box with vertical padding and negative margin tricks — that's the ticket for eliminating gaps without restructuring the DOM. Now I need to map out the full scope of changes: first, inspect the current CSS and span markup in index.html to apply the padding fix correctly. Then tackle the engine side — reshape the title card to full height, reposition the letters and adjust the font, redesign the menu layout with guest list support, implement the three-field name flow with nickname plumbing throughout the display logic, and scatter in the default nickname values. Finally, update index.html with the CSS padding fix and add the footer's second line, then rebuild and verify the title geometry, menu layout with both original and personalized guest lists, and the nickname parser for matching accusations and questions. For the parser, I'm building a helper that checks if the input includes either the suspect's key or their nickname key, then using that same logic in doQuestion and doAccuse lookups. I'll also need to grep through the codebase to find all the display site replacements before writing them out.

Seven items — and #7 is a genuinely interesting rendering puzzle (I have a strong suspect: fractional line-height rounding at non-integer display scaling, very common on Windows at 125%). Let me inspect the render markup and CSS first, plus map every .first display site for the nickname plumbing:

bash_toolInspect CSS, render markup, and all first-name display sites
bash
cd /home/claude 2>/dev/null && ls web/engine.js >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo INTACT || (mkdir -p /home/claude/sleuth /home/claude/web && cd /home/claude && unzip -qo /mnt/user-data/uploads/sleuth.zip -d sleuth && cp /mnt/user-data/outputs/extract_sleuth.py . && cd web && unzip -qo /mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m6.zip && cp gen_web_data.py .. && cd .. && python3 extract_sleuth.py sleuth extracted > /dev/null && echo RESTORED); cd /home/claude && echo "=== screen CSS ===" && grep -n -A6 "#screen" web/index.html | head -20 && echo "=== render() ===" && grep -n -A12 "function render" web/engine.js | head -16 && echo "=== .first display sites ===" && grep -n "\.first" web/engine.js | grep -v "n\.first\|firstLines\|freshLines" | head -30
Output
INTACT
=== screen CSS ===
39:  #screen {
40-    margin: 0;
41-    font-family: inherit;
42-    font-size: clamp(8px, 1.12vw, 15px);
43-    line-height: 1.18;
44-    letter-spacing: 0;
45-    background: #000;
=== render() ===
79:function render() {
80-  let html = "";
81-  for (let r = 0; r < ROWS; r++) {
82-    let runAttr = null, run = "";
83-    for (let c = 0; c < COLS; c++) {
84-      const { ch, attr } = screen[r][c];
85-      if (attr !== runAttr) {
86-        if (run) html += spanFor(run, runAttr);
87-        runAttr = attr; run = "";
88-      }
89-      run += ch;
90-    }
91-    html += spanFor(run, runAttr) + "\n";
=== .first display sites ===
361:  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.first;
363:    say(`In a flash, ${m.first} locks the door behind you, grabs the ` +
367:    say(`${m.first} quietly locks the door behind you as you enter the ` +
372:    say(`${m.first} locks the door and turns toward you.  "I'm sick and ` +
451:      withName: withS ? withS.first : null,
642:          say(`${s.first} ${actionOf(s)}`);
697:  say(`${world.victim.first} ${world.victim.last} was brutally murdered ` +
825:        say(`${s.first} ${s.last} ${actionOf(s)}`);
1042:  const scapegoat = pick(others).first;
1046:    .replace(/#\+{9}(?=[A-Za-z])/g, world.victim.first + " ")
1047:    .replace(/#\+{9}/g, world.victim.first)
1059:    say(`${named.first} is not in this room.`);
1068:    say(`${target.first} can't hear you from all the way over there.`);
1074:    say(`${target.first} ${pick(DIALOGUE.refusals)}`);
1082:    say(`${target.first} ${framing}${pick(DIALOGUE.repeats)} ` +
1086:    let line = `${target.first} ${framing}${alibiStatement(a)}`;
1143:  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.first;
1147:    say(`${accused.first} rises and declares angrily, "You're wrong!!"`);
1153:    say(`${m.first} stands up and exclaims, "You fool!!  Yes, I used ` +
1161:    say(`${m.first} rises and declares angrily, "You're wrong!!  ` +
1169:  say(`${m.first} ${fillTokens(pick(DIALOGUE.confessions), m)}`);
1207:      say(world.suspects.map((s) => `${s.first} ${s.last}`).join(", ") + ".");
1602:        const cand = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.first;
Thinking · Pinpointing line-height and fractional pixels causing rendering artifacts.
I've found the culprit: the line-height of 1.18 combined with a fractional font-size from the clamp function creates non-integer pixel values that, when multiplied by the device pixel ratio, cause some line boxes to round down by a pixel, leaving those black hairlines visible at irregular intervals. The fix is to add vertical padding to inline spans so their backgrounds extend beyond the line box without affecting the layout, or alternatively adjust the line-height value itself. Now I'm going through the display sites to replace all `.first` references with `.nick` for character names — checking death speeches, accusations, arrival messages, room listings, and the guest list. I'm also creating helper functions to handle name formatting: one that returns a nickname if available or falls back to the first name, and another that combines first and last names with proper trimming. Since victims and suspects will always have nicknames assigned during the scatter phase, I need to make sure those are set there, though raw house characters might not have them initially. For parsing player input, I'm matching against both the uppercase key and the uppercase nickname key to catch different ways players might reference suspects. The names entry form takes three fields (first, last, nick), handles blank last names by converting whitespace to empty strings, and checks for duplicate keys and nicknames across all entries. The menu layout is shifting to a left column at position 6 with labels padded to width 26 so all values align at column 30, and I'm adding a guest list box on the right side with a cyan border. Now I'm working on the guest list display — it shows numbered entries with full name and nickname, truncated to fit within 28 characters, and pulls the cast from either custom settings or the selected house's character list. If it's a surprise game without custom casting, I'll add a dimmed note at the bottom saying the cast follows the house. The card itself overlays the right half of the screen starting at column 40 with a width of 40 columns, drawing a box border with proper corners and vertical lines, centering the content rows between columns 41-78. The letter keys are moving down to rows 19-23 with a blank row 18 in between, and the footer is being restructured to put the plate link on its own line. For the tests, I need to verify the card box displays correctly over the right half with the map visible on the left, that the menu labels and values align properly, the guest list shows entries with personalization working, the three name fields handle blanks and duplicates correctly, the intro text uses the estate fallback when needed, and the full win scenario still works. Then I'll write the comprehensive patch with the exact line numbers and make the index.html edits for the padding fix and link restructuring.
Thinking · Identified line-height rendering issue and devised padding solution.
I found the issue: `line-height: 1.18` on a clamped font size causes line boxes to round short at non-integer display scaling, letting the black background peek through. I'll apply vertical padding to the inline spans to fix this across the full batch.
bash_toolApply all seven tweaks: title geometry, nicknames, menu redesign, CSS fix
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - > /tmp/p.log 2>&1 << 'PATCH_EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
def rep(old, new):
    global src
    assert old in src, "NOT FOUND: " + old[:70]
    src = src.replace(old, new)

# ============ 5+6. NICKNAMES + one-word names: data layer ============
rep('''  const cast = [...(ui.customCast || house().characters)];
  world.victim = cast.splice(Math.floor(rng() * cast.length), 1)[0];''',
'''  const cast = [...(ui.customCast || house().characters)]
    .map((p) => ({ ...p, nick: p.nick || p.first,
                   nickKey: (p.nick || p.first).toUpperCase() }));
  world.victim = cast.splice(Math.floor(rng() * cast.length), 1)[0];''')

rep('''// what a guest is visibly doing: the stare overrides idle flavour''',
'''// display helpers: the NICKNAME is used wherever one name appears;
// full names tolerate a blank last name (one-word characters)
const fullName = (p) => (p.first + " " + (p.last || "")).trim();
const matchSuspect = (input, s) =>
  input.includes(s.key) ||
  (s.nickKey && s.nickKey !== s.key && input.includes(s.nickKey));

// what a guest is visibly doing: the stare overrides idle flavour''')

# ---- display sites: first -> nick ----
rep('''  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.first;
  if (variant === "cosh") {
    say(`In a flash, ${m.first} locks the door behind you, grabs the ` +''',
'''  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.nick;
  if (variant === "cosh") {
    say(`In a flash, ${m.nick} locks the door behind you, grabs the ` +''')
rep('''    say(`${m.first} quietly locks the door behind you as you enter the ` +''',
'''    say(`${m.nick} quietly locks the door behind you as you enter the ` +''')
rep('''    say(`${m.first} locks the door and turns toward you.  "I'm sick and ` +''',
'''    say(`${m.nick} locks the door and turns toward you.  "I'm sick and ` +''')
rep('''      withName: withS ? withS.first : null,''',
'''      withName: withS ? withS.nick : null,''')
rep('''          say(`${s.first} ${actionOf(s)}`);''',
'''          say(`${s.nick} ${actionOf(s)}`);''')
rep('''  say(`${world.victim.first} ${world.victim.last} was brutally murdered ` +
      "only hours ago.  The body was removed by persons unknown from " +
      "the murder scene.  You are now standing at the front door of " +
      `the ${world.victim.last} estate.  The door is open.`);''',
'''  say(`${fullName(world.victim)} was brutally murdered ` +
      "only hours ago.  The body was removed by persons unknown from " +
      "the murder scene.  You are now standing at the front door of " +
      `the ${world.victim.last || world.victim.nick} estate.  ` +
      "The door is open.");''')
rep('''        say(`${s.first} ${s.last} ${actionOf(s)}`);''',
'''        say(`${fullName(s)} ${actionOf(s)}`);''')
rep('''  const scapegoat = pick(others).first;''',
'''  const scapegoat = pick(others).nick;''')
rep('''    .replace(/#\\+{9}(?=[A-Za-z])/g, world.victim.first + " ")
    .replace(/#\\+{9}/g, world.victim.first)''',
'''    .replace(/#\\+{9}(?=[A-Za-z])/g, world.victim.nick + " ")
    .replace(/#\\+{9}/g, world.victim.nick)''')
rep('''    say(`${named.first} is not in this room.`);''',
'''    say(`${named.nick} is not in this room.`);''')
rep('''    say(`${target.first} can't hear you from all the way over there.`);''',
'''    say(`${target.nick} can't hear you from all the way over there.`);''')
rep('''    say(`${target.first} ${pick(DIALOGUE.refusals)}`);''',
'''    say(`${target.nick} ${pick(DIALOGUE.refusals)}`);''')
rep('''    say(`${target.first} ${framing}${pick(DIALOGUE.repeats)} ` +''',
'''    say(`${target.nick} ${framing}${pick(DIALOGUE.repeats)} ` +''')
rep('''    let line = `${target.first} ${framing}${alibiStatement(a)}`;''',
'''    let line = `${target.nick} ${framing}${alibiStatement(a)}`;''')
rep('''  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.first;
  const here = locOf(state.roomId);''',
'''  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.nick;
  const here = locOf(state.roomId);''')
rep('''    say(`${accused.first} rises and declares angrily, "You're wrong!!"`);''',
'''    say(`${accused.nick} rises and declares angrily, "You're wrong!!"`);''')
rep('''    say(`${m.first} stands up and exclaims, "You fool!!  Yes, I used ` +''',
'''    say(`${m.nick} stands up and exclaims, "You fool!!  Yes, I used ` +''')
rep('''    say(`${m.first} rises and declares angrily, "You're wrong!!  ` +''',
'''    say(`${m.nick} rises and declares angrily, "You're wrong!!  ` +''')
rep('''  say(`${m.first} ${fillTokens(pick(DIALOGUE.confessions), m)}`);''',
'''  say(`${m.nick} ${fillTokens(pick(DIALOGUE.confessions), m)}`);''')
rep('''      say(world.suspects.map((s) => `${s.first} ${s.last}`).join(", ") + ".");''',
'''      say(world.suspects.map(fullName).join(", ") + ".");''')

# ---- parser: nickname also matches ----
rep('''  const here = world.suspects.filter((s) => s.room === locOf(state.roomId));
  if (!here.length) { say("But there's nobody here!"); return; }
  const named = world.suspects.find((s) => input.includes(s.key));''',
'''  const here = world.suspects.filter((s) => s.room === locOf(state.roomId));
  if (!here.length) { say("But there's nobody here!"); return; }
  const named = world.suspects.find((s) => matchSuspect(input, s));''')
rep('''  const accused = world.suspects.find((s) => input.includes(s.key));''',
'''  const accused = world.suspects.find((s) => matchSuspect(input, s));''')

# ============ 1+2+3. TITLE: card over the right half; letters ============
rep('''  // the credits card, overlaid on the right
  const L = 38, W2 = 40;
  for (let r = 1; r <= 15; r++)
    for (let c = L; c < L + W2; c++) put(r, c, " ", 0x07);
  for (let c = L; c < L + W2; c++) { put(1, c, "\\u2500", 0x0f); put(15, c, "\\u2500", 0x0f); }
  const card = (row, text, attr) =>          // centred WITHIN the card,
    print(row, L + Math.max(0, Math.floor((W2 - text.length) / 2)),
          text, attr);                       // not across the whole map
  card(3,  "SLEUTH  \\u00b7  remastered", 0x0f);
  card(5,  "Original game (C) 1983", 0x07);
  card(6,  "Eric N. Miller \\u00b7 Norland Software", 0x0b);
  card(8,  "Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)", 0x07);
  card(9,  "Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai", 0x07);
  card(11, "You are encouraged to copy and", 0x07);
  card(12, "share this program with others.", 0x07);
  card(14, " PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN THE GAME ", 0x70);
  // the giant letters, red, as the EXE's coordinate paths once drew
  const word = "SLEUTH", lw = 4, gap = 2, tot = word.length * (lw + gap) * 2 - gap * 2;
  let x = Math.floor((COLS - tot) / 2);
  for (const ch of word) {
    const glyph = BIGFONT[ch];
    for (let r = 0; r < 5; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < lw; c++)
        if (glyph[r][c] === "\\u2588") {
          put(18 + r, x + c * 2, "\\u2592", 0x0c);
          put(18 + r, x + c * 2 + 1, "\\u2592", 0x0c);
        }
    x += (lw + gap) * 2;
  }
  render();''',
'''  // the credits card: covers the RIGHT HALF of the house exactly,
  // rows 1-17 x cols 40-79, boxed
  const L = 40, R2 = 79, T = 1, B = 17;
  for (let r = T; r <= B; r++)
    for (let c = L; c <= R2; c++) put(r, c, " ", 0x07);
  for (let c = L + 1; c < R2; c++) { put(T, c, "\\u2500", 0x0f); put(B, c, "\\u2500", 0x0f); }
  for (let r = T + 1; r < B; r++) { put(r, L, "\\u2502", 0x0f); put(r, R2, "\\u2502", 0x0f); }
  put(T, L, "\\u250c", 0x0f); put(T, R2, "\\u2510", 0x0f);
  put(B, L, "\\u2514", 0x0f); put(B, R2, "\\u2518", 0x0f);
  const card = (row, text, attr) =>
    print(row, L + 1 + Math.max(0, Math.floor((R2 - L - 1 - text.length) / 2)),
          text, attr);
  card(3,  "SLEUTH  \\u00b7  remastered", 0x0f);
  card(6,  "Original game (C) 1983", 0x07);
  card(7,  "Eric N. Miller \\u00b7 Norland Software", 0x0b);
  card(9,  "Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)", 0x07);
  card(10, "Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai", 0x07);
  card(12, "You are encouraged to copy and", 0x07);
  card(13, "share this program with others.", 0x07);
  card(15, " PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN THE GAME ", 0x70);
  // the giant letters, red, one blank row below the house
  const word = "SLEUTH", lw = 4, gap = 2, tot = word.length * (lw + gap) * 2 - gap * 2;
  let x = Math.floor((COLS - tot) / 2);
  for (const ch of word) {
    const glyph = BIGFONT[ch];
    for (let r = 0; r < 5; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < lw; c++)
        if (glyph[r][c] === "\\u2588") {
          put(19 + r, x + c * 2, "\\u2592", 0x0c);
          put(19 + r, x + c * 2 + 1, "\\u2592", 0x0c);
        }
    x += (lw + gap) * 2;
  }
  render();''')

rep('''  T: ["\\u2588\\u2588\\u2588\\u2588", " \\u2588\\u2588 ", " \\u2588\\u2588 ", " \\u2588\\u2588 ", " \\u2588\\u2588 "],''',
'''  T: ["\\u2588\\u2588\\u2588\\u2588", " \\u2588  ", " \\u2588  ", " \\u2588  ", " \\u2588  "],''')

# ============ 4. MENU: left options, aligned values, guest list ============
rep('''function drawMenu() {
  clearScreen(0x07);
  centre(3, "S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  centre(6, "Select the option of your choice:", 0x07);
  const left = 22;
  print(9,  left, "(A) Start the Game", 0x0f);
  print(11, left, `(H) House:    ${menuHouseLabel()}`, 0x07);
  print(12, left, `(C) Colours:  ${SCHEMES[state.schemeIdx].name}`, 0x07);
  print(13, left, `(S) Suspects: ${ui.customCast ? "personalized" : "original cast"}`, 0x07);
  print(14, left, `(R) Random Number Seed:  ${SEED}`, 0x07);
  print(16, left, "(I) Review Instructions", 0x07);
  centre(20, " PRESS A KEY TO CHOOSE ", 0x70);
  centre(23, "Copyright (C) 1983 by Eric N. Miller and Norland Software", 0x08);
  centre(24, "remastered 2026 \\u00b7 Claude & Jeromai", 0x08);
  render();
}''',
'''function menuCast() {
  if (ui.customCast) return ui.customCast;
  const h = window.SLEUTH_HOUSES[ui.houseIdx < 0 ? 0 : ui.houseIdx];
  return h.characters.slice(0, 7).map((p) => ({ ...p, nick: p.first }));
}

function drawMenu() {
  clearScreen(0x07);
  print(3, 8, "S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  print(6, 4, "Select the option of your choice:", 0x07);
  const left = 4, valCol = 30;
  print(9,  left, "(A) Start the Game", 0x0f);
  print(11, left, "(H) House:", 0x07);
  print(11, valCol, menuHouseLabel(), 0x0f);
  print(12, left, "(C) Colours:", 0x07);
  print(12, valCol, SCHEMES[state.schemeIdx].name, 0x0f);
  print(13, left, "(S) Suspects:", 0x07);
  print(13, valCol, ui.customCast ? "personalized" : "original cast", 0x0f);
  print(14, left, "(R) Random Number Seed:", 0x07);
  print(14, valCol, String(SEED), 0x0f);
  print(16, left, "(I) Review Instructions", 0x07);
  // the guest list panel: whoever will attend the party
  const GL = 48, GR = 79, GT = 8, GB = 18;
  for (let c = GL + 1; c < GR; c++) { put(GT, c, "\\u2500", 0x0f); put(GB, c, "\\u2500", 0x0f); }
  for (let r = GT + 1; r < GB; r++) { put(r, GL, "\\u2502", 0x0f); put(r, GR, "\\u2502", 0x0f); }
  put(GT, GL, "\\u250c", 0x0f); put(GT, GR, "\\u2510", 0x0f);
  put(GB, GL, "\\u2514", 0x0f); put(GB, GR, "\\u2518", 0x0f);
  print(GT + 1, GL + 2, "Guest List", 0x0b);
  const cast = menuCast();
  for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(7, cast.length); i++) {
    const p = cast[i];
    const full = (p.first + " " + (p.last || "")).trim();
    const nick = p.nick || p.first;
    const line = `${i + 1}. ${full}` + (nick !== p.first ? ` (${nick})` : "");
    print(GT + 3 + i, GL + 2, line.slice(0, GR - GL - 3), 0x07);
  }
  if (ui.houseIdx < 0 && !ui.customCast)
    print(GB - 1, GL + 2, "(house 1 shown; cast follows house)", 0x08);
  centre(21, " PRESS A KEY TO CHOOSE ", 0x70);
  centre(23, "Copyright (C) 1983 by Eric N. Miller and Norland Software", 0x08);
  centre(24, "remastered 2026 \\u00b7 Claude & Jeromai", 0x08);
  render();
}''')

# ============ 5+6. NAME ENTRY: three fields, blank last, nick dup ============
rep('''function drawNames() {
  const n = ui.names;
  clearScreen(0x07);
  centre(3, "P E R S O N A L I Z E D   S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  centre(6, `SUSPECT # ${n.idx + 1}`, 0x0f);
  print(9,  18, "Enter first name:", 0x07);
  print(9,  38, n.field === 0 ? n.buffer + "_" : n.first, n.field === 0 ? 0x70 : 0x07);
  print(11, 18, "Enter last name:", 0x07);
  if (n.field === 1) print(11, 38, n.buffer + "_", 0x70);
  centre(16, "(seven names: six suspects and one victim!)", 0x08);
  centre(18, "Enter accepts \\u00b7 blank keeps the original name \\u00b7 Esc abandons", 0x08);
  if (n.dup) centre(20, "(first names must be distinct \\u2014 they are how the parser knows who's who)", 0x0c);
  render();
}''',
'''function drawNames() {
  const n = ui.names;
  clearScreen(0x07);
  centre(3, "P E R S O N A L I Z E D   S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  centre(6, `SUSPECT # ${n.idx + 1}`, 0x0f);
  print(9,  18, "Enter first name:", 0x07);
  print(9,  38, n.field === 0 ? n.buffer + "_" : n.first, n.field === 0 ? 0x70 : 0x07);
  print(11, 18, "Enter last name:", 0x07);
  print(11, 38, n.field === 1 ? n.buffer + "_" : (n.field > 1 ? (n.last || "\\u2014") : ""),
        n.field === 1 ? 0x70 : 0x07);
  print(13, 18, "Enter nickname:", 0x07);
  if (n.field === 2) print(13, 38, n.buffer + "_", 0x70);
  centre(16, "(seven names: six suspects and one victim!)", 0x08);
  centre(17, "Enter accepts \\u00b7 blank keeps the original \\u00b7 a lone space = no last name",
         0x08);
  centre(18, "blank nickname = first name \\u00b7 Esc abandons", 0x08);
  if (n.dup) centre(20, "(names must be distinct \\u2014 they are how the parser knows who's who)", 0x0c);
  render();
}''')

rep('''    if (k === "Enter") {
      const fallback = house().characters[n.idx];
      if (n.field === 0) {
        const cand = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.first;
        // first names are the parser's match keys: they must differ
        if (n.list.some((p) => p.key === cand.toUpperCase())) {
          n.dup = true; n.buffer = "";
          drawNames(); return;
        }
        n.dup = false;
        n.first = cand;
        n.field = 1; n.buffer = "";
      } else {
        const last = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.last;
        n.list.push({ first: n.first, last,
                      key: n.first.toUpperCase() });
        n.idx++; n.field = 0; n.buffer = ""; n.first = "";
        if (n.idx >= 7) {
          ui.customCast = n.list;
          uiEnter("menu");
          return;
        }
      }
      drawNames(); return;
    }''',
'''    if (k === "Enter") {
      const fallback = house().characters[n.idx];
      const taken = (name) => n.list.some((p) =>
        p.key === name.toUpperCase() || p.nickKey === name.toUpperCase());
      if (n.field === 0) {
        const cand = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.first;
        if (taken(cand)) { n.dup = true; n.buffer = ""; drawNames(); return; }
        n.dup = false;
        n.first = cand;
        n.field = 1; n.buffer = "";
      } else if (n.field === 1) {
        // a lone space means: this character has no last name
        n.last = n.buffer.length && !n.buffer.trim() ? ""
               : (n.buffer.trim() || fallback.last);
        n.field = 2; n.buffer = "";
      } else {
        const nick = n.buffer.trim() || n.first;
        if (nick !== n.first && taken(nick)) {
          n.dup = true; n.buffer = ""; drawNames(); return;
        }
        n.dup = false;
        n.list.push({ first: n.first, last: n.last, nick,
                      key: n.first.toUpperCase(),
                      nickKey: nick.toUpperCase() });
        n.idx++; n.field = 0; n.buffer = ""; n.first = ""; n.last = "";
        if (n.idx >= 7) {
          ui.customCast = n.list;
          uiEnter("menu");
          return;
        }
      }
      drawNames(); return;
    }''')

# name buffer: allow a bare space to register for the blank-last rule
rep('''    if (k.length === 1 && /[a-zA-Z0-9' -]/.test(k) && n.buffer.length < 12) {
      n.buffer += k; drawNames();
    }''',
'''    if (k.length === 1 && /[a-zA-Z0-9' -]/.test(k) && n.buffer.length < 14) {
      n.buffer += k; drawNames();
    }''')

open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("PATCH_COMPLETE")
PATCH_EOF
cat /tmp/p.log; grep -q PATCH_COMPLETE /tmp/p.log && node --check web/engine.js && python3 - << 'EOF'
h = open('web/index.html').read()
# 7) hairline fix: inline-span vertical padding overpaints line-box seams
h = h.replace('''    line-height: 1.18;''',
'''    line-height: 1.18;''')
h = h.replace('''  #screen {''',
'''  #screen span { padding: 2px 0; }   /* overpaint fractional-scaling
                                        hairlines between text rows */
  #screen {''')
# 3) the original-availability line on its own plate row
h = h.replace('''    <span>original SLEUTH by Eric N. Miller available at
      <a href="http://www.calcaprt.com/share.htm">calcaprt.com/share.htm</a></span>
  </div>''',
'''  </div>
  <div class="plate">
    <span>original SLEUTH by Eric N. Miller available at
      <a href="http://www.calcaprt.com/share.htm">calcaprt.com/share.htm</a></span>
  </div>''')
open('web/index.html','w').write(h)
print("index.html: hairline fix + footer line")
EOF

Thinking · Reconciling patch expectations with current implementation mismatch.
I'm running into a mismatch between what the patch expects and what's actually in the file — the title-card replacement is looking for the original `centre()` calls with specific dimensions, but the current version has the `card()` helper I implemented in the last session, so the grep pattern doesn't find anything to replace. The assertion prevents the write, but the index.html and generation steps still ran because they're in separate conditional blocks. Looking at the command chain more carefully: the grep for `PATCH_COMPLETE` failed (since the Python script printed a traceback instead), which should've stopped the `&&` chain, but the gen script output still appears in the results. That's because the final `python3 gen_web_data.py` command is on its own line after the heredoc — it runs unconditionally regardless of whether the earlier steps succeeded. So the patch didn't fully apply: the engine remains unpatched, the index.html block wasn't updated, and the playable was rebuilt from the original sources. I need to grab the exact current title-card block text from the last session and correct that one replacement, then rerun the entire patch. I should also verify the other anchors I'm searching for — drawMenu with the `centre()` version, drawNames with the duplicate hint, and the names Enter block with the duplicate check — all match what was applied in the previous session.

The title-card rep is stale from the last session, so I'm pulling the current text and rerunning it.

  // the credits card, overlaid on the right
  const L = 38, W2 = 40;
  for (let r = 1; r <= 15; r++)
    for (let c = L; c < L + W2; c++) put(r, c, " ", 0x07);
  for (let c = L; c < L + W2; c++) { put(1, c, "─", 0x0f); put(15, c, "─", 0x0f); }
  const card = (row, text, attr) =>          // centred WITHIN the card,
    print(row, L + Math.max(0, Math.floor((W2 - text.length) / 2)),
          text, attr);                       // not across the whole map
  card(3,  "SLEUTH  ·  remastered", 0x0f);
  card(5,  "Original game (C) 1983", 0x07);
  card(6,  "Eric N. Miller · Norland Software", 0x0b);
  card(8,  "Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)", 0x07);

  let x = Math.floor((COLS - tot) / 2);
  for (const ch of word) {
    const glyph = BIGFONT[ch];
    for (let r = 0; r < 5; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < lw; c++)
        if (glyph[r][c] === "█") {
          put(18 + r, x + c * 2, "▒", 0x0c);
          put(18 + r, x + c * 2 + 1, "▒", 0x0c);
        }
    x += (lw + gap) * 2;
  }
  render();
}
Thinking · Rewriting search pattern with actual unicode characters directly.
I see the issue — the search pattern was using escaped unicode notation when the actual file contains real unicode characters. I need to rewrite the title anchor to use the actual ─ · █ ▒ characters directly in the Python search string, then run the full patch again with the corrected pattern. Then I'll update index.html, rebuild, and run the tests.

Real characters in the file, escape sequences in my search — classic. Rerunning with real-char anchors:

bash_toolApply all seven tweaks with real-character anchors
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - > /tmp/p.log 2>&1 << 'PATCH_EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
def rep(old, new):
    global src
    assert old in src, "NOT FOUND: " + old[:70]
    src = src.replace(old, new)

# ============ 5+6. NICKNAMES + one-word names ============
rep('''  const cast = [...(ui.customCast || house().characters)];
  world.victim = cast.splice(Math.floor(rng() * cast.length), 1)[0];''',
'''  const cast = [...(ui.customCast || house().characters)]
    .map((p) => ({ ...p, nick: p.nick || p.first,
                   nickKey: (p.nick || p.first).toUpperCase() }));
  world.victim = cast.splice(Math.floor(rng() * cast.length), 1)[0];''')

rep('''// what a guest is visibly doing: the stare overrides idle flavour''',
'''// display helpers: the NICKNAME is used wherever one name appears;
// full names tolerate a blank last name (one-word characters)
const fullName = (p) => (p.first + " " + (p.last || "")).trim();
const matchSuspect = (input, s) =>
  input.includes(s.key) ||
  (s.nickKey && s.nickKey !== s.key && input.includes(s.nickKey));

// what a guest is visibly doing: the stare overrides idle flavour''')

rep('''  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.first;
  if (variant === "cosh") {
    say(`In a flash, ${m.first} locks the door behind you, grabs the ` +''',
'''  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.nick;
  if (variant === "cosh") {
    say(`In a flash, ${m.nick} locks the door behind you, grabs the ` +''')
rep('''    say(`${m.first} quietly locks the door behind you as you enter the ` +''',
'''    say(`${m.nick} quietly locks the door behind you as you enter the ` +''')
rep('''    say(`${m.first} locks the door and turns toward you.  "I'm sick and ` +''',
'''    say(`${m.nick} locks the door and turns toward you.  "I'm sick and ` +''')
rep('''      withName: withS ? withS.first : null,''',
'''      withName: withS ? withS.nick : null,''')
rep('''          say(`${s.first} ${actionOf(s)}`);''',
'''          say(`${s.nick} ${actionOf(s)}`);''')
rep('''  say(`${world.victim.first} ${world.victim.last} was brutally murdered ` +
      "only hours ago.  The body was removed by persons unknown from " +
      "the murder scene.  You are now standing at the front door of " +
      `the ${world.victim.last} estate.  The door is open.`);''',
'''  say(`${fullName(world.victim)} was brutally murdered ` +
      "only hours ago.  The body was removed by persons unknown from " +
      "the murder scene.  You are now standing at the front door of " +
      `the ${world.victim.last || world.victim.nick} estate.  ` +
      "The door is open.");''')
rep('''        say(`${s.first} ${s.last} ${actionOf(s)}`);''',
'''        say(`${fullName(s)} ${actionOf(s)}`);''')
rep('''  const scapegoat = pick(others).first;''',
'''  const scapegoat = pick(others).nick;''')
rep('''    .replace(/#\\+{9}(?=[A-Za-z])/g, world.victim.first + " ")
    .replace(/#\\+{9}/g, world.victim.first)''',
'''    .replace(/#\\+{9}(?=[A-Za-z])/g, world.victim.nick + " ")
    .replace(/#\\+{9}/g, world.victim.nick)''')
rep('''    say(`${named.first} is not in this room.`);''',
'''    say(`${named.nick} is not in this room.`);''')
rep('''    say(`${target.first} can't hear you from all the way over there.`);''',
'''    say(`${target.nick} can't hear you from all the way over there.`);''')
rep('''    say(`${target.first} ${pick(DIALOGUE.refusals)}`);''',
'''    say(`${target.nick} ${pick(DIALOGUE.refusals)}`);''')
rep('''    say(`${target.first} ${framing}${pick(DIALOGUE.repeats)} ` +''',
'''    say(`${target.nick} ${framing}${pick(DIALOGUE.repeats)} ` +''')
rep('''    let line = `${target.first} ${framing}${alibiStatement(a)}`;''',
'''    let line = `${target.nick} ${framing}${alibiStatement(a)}`;''')
rep('''  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.first;
  const here = locOf(state.roomId);''',
'''  const m = murdererS(), w = weaponName(), v = world.victim.nick;
  const here = locOf(state.roomId);''')
rep('''    say(`${accused.first} rises and declares angrily, "You're wrong!!"`);''',
'''    say(`${accused.nick} rises and declares angrily, "You're wrong!!"`);''')

'''    say(`${m.nick} stands up and exclaims, "You fool!!  Yes, I used ` +''')

rep('''  say(`${m.first} ${fillTokens(pick(DIALOGUE.confessions), m)}`);''',

rep('''      say(world.suspects.map((s) => `${s.first} ${s.last}`).join(", ") + ".");''',
'''      say(world.suspects.map(fullName).join(", ") + ".");''')
rep('''  const named = world.suspects.find((s) => input.includes(s.key));
  if (named && named.room !== locOf(state.roomId)) {''',

  if (named && named.room !== locOf(state.roomId)) {''')
rep('''  const accused = world.suspects.find((s) => input.includes(s.key));''',
'''  const accused = world.suspects.find((s) => matchSuspect(input, s));''')

# ============ 1+2. TITLE: right-half card; letters shifted; thin T ============
rep('''  // the credits card, overlaid on the right
  const L = 38, W2 = 40;
  for (let r = 1; r <= 15; r++)
    for (let c = L; c < L + W2; c++) put(r, c, " ", 0x07);
  for (let c = L; c < L + W2; c++) { put(1, c, "─", 0x0f); put(15, c, "─", 0x0f); }
  const card = (row, text, attr) =>          // centred WITHIN the card,
    print(row, L + Math.max(0, Math.floor((W2 - text.length) / 2)),
          text, attr);                       // not across the whole map
  card(3,  "SLEUTH  ·  remastered", 0x0f);
  card(5,  "Original game (C) 1983", 0x07);
  card(6,  "Eric N. Miller · Norland Software", 0x0b);
  card(8,  "Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)", 0x07);
  card(9,  "Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai", 0x07);
  card(11, "You are encouraged to copy and", 0x07);
  card(12, "share this program with others.", 0x07);
  card(14, " PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN THE GAME ", 0x70);
  // the giant letters, red, as the EXE's coordinate paths once drew
  const word = "SLEUTH", lw = 4, gap = 2, tot = word.length * (lw + gap) * 2 - gap * 2;
  let x = Math.floor((COLS - tot) / 2);
  for (const ch of word) {
    const glyph = BIGFONT[ch];
    for (let r = 0; r < 5; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < lw; c++)
        if (glyph[r][c] === "█") {
          put(18 + r, x + c * 2, "▒", 0x0c);
          put(18 + r, x + c * 2 + 1, "▒", 0x0c);
        }
    x += (lw + gap) * 2;
  }
  render();''',
'''  // the credits card: covers the RIGHT HALF of the house exactly
  // (rows 1-17 x cols 40-79), boxed
  const L = 40, R2 = 79, T = 1, B = 17;
  for (let r = T; r <= B; r++)
    for (let c = L; c <= R2; c++) put(r, c, " ", 0x07);
  for (let c = L + 1; c < R2; c++) { put(T, c, "─", 0x0f); put(B, c, "─", 0x0f); }
  for (let r = T + 1; r < B; r++) { put(r, L, "│", 0x0f); put(r, R2, "│", 0x0f); }
  put(T, L, "┌", 0x0f); put(T, R2, "┐", 0x0f);
  put(B, L, "└", 0x0f); put(B, R2, "┘", 0x0f);
  const card = (row, text, attr) =>
    print(row, L + 1 + Math.max(0, Math.floor((R2 - L - 1 - text.length) / 2)),
          text, attr);
  card(3,  "SLEUTH  ·  remastered", 0x0f);
  card(6,  "Original game (C) 1983", 0x07);
  card(7,  "Eric N. Miller · Norland Software", 0x0b);
  card(9,  "Remaster code: Claude (Anthropic)", 0x07);
  card(10, "Design & playtest archaeology: Jeromai", 0x07);
  card(12, "You are encouraged to copy and", 0x07);
  card(13, "share this program with others.", 0x07);
  card(15, " PRESS ANY KEY TO BEGIN THE GAME ", 0x70);
  // the giant letters, red, one blank row below the house
  const word = "SLEUTH", lw = 4, gap = 2, tot = word.length * (lw + gap) * 2 - gap * 2;
  let x = Math.floor((COLS - tot) / 2);
  for (const ch of word) {
    const glyph = BIGFONT[ch];
    for (let r = 0; r < 5; r++)
      for (let c = 0; c < lw; c++)
        if (glyph[r][c] === "█") {
          put(19 + r, x + c * 2, "▒", 0x0c);
          put(19 + r, x + c * 2 + 1, "▒", 0x0c);
        }
    x += (lw + gap) * 2;
  }
  render();''')

rep('''  T: ["████", " ██ ", " ██ ", " ██ ", " ██ "],''',
'''  T: ["████", " █  ", " █  ", " █  ", " █  "],''')

# ============ 4. MENU redesign ============
rep('''function drawMenu() {
  clearScreen(0x07);
  centre(3, "S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  centre(6, "Select the option of your choice:", 0x07);
  const left = 22;
  print(9,  left, "(A) Start the Game", 0x0f);
  print(11, left, `(H) House:    ${menuHouseLabel()}`, 0x07);
  print(12, left, `(C) Colours:  ${SCHEMES[state.schemeIdx].name}`, 0x07);
  print(13, left, `(S) Suspects: ${ui.customCast ? "personalized" : "original cast"}`, 0x07);
  print(14, left, `(R) Random Number Seed:  ${SEED}`, 0x07);
  print(16, left, "(I) Review Instructions", 0x07);
  centre(20, " PRESS A KEY TO CHOOSE ", 0x70);
  centre(23, "Copyright (C) 1983 by Eric N. Miller and Norland Software", 0x08);
  centre(24, "remastered 2026 · Claude & Jeromai", 0x08);
  render();
}''',
'''function menuCast() {
  if (ui.customCast) return ui.customCast;
  const h = window.SLEUTH_HOUSES[ui.houseIdx < 0 ? 0 : ui.houseIdx];
  return h.characters.slice(0, 7).map((p) => ({ ...p, nick: p.first }));
}

function drawMenu() {
  clearScreen(0x07);
  print(3, 8, "S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  print(6, 4, "Select the option of your choice:", 0x07);
  const left = 4, valCol = 30;
  print(9,  left, "(A) Start the Game", 0x0f);
  print(11, left, "(H) House:", 0x07);
  print(11, valCol, menuHouseLabel(), 0x0f);
  print(12, left, "(C) Colours:", 0x07);
  print(12, valCol, SCHEMES[state.schemeIdx].name, 0x0f);
  print(13, left, "(S) Suspects:", 0x07);
  print(13, valCol, ui.customCast ? "personalized" : "original cast", 0x0f);
  print(14, left, "(R) Random Number Seed:", 0x07);
  print(14, valCol, String(SEED), 0x0f);
  print(16, left, "(I) Review Instructions", 0x07);
  // the guest list panel: whoever will attend the party
  const GL = 48, GR = 79, GT = 8, GB = 18;
  for (let c = GL + 1; c < GR; c++) { put(GT, c, "─", 0x0f); put(GB, c, "─", 0x0f); }
  for (let r = GT + 1; r < GB; r++) { put(r, GL, "│", 0x0f); put(r, GR, "│", 0x0f); }
  put(GT, GL, "┌", 0x0f); put(GT, GR, "┐", 0x0f);
  put(GB, GL, "└", 0x0f); put(GB, GR, "┘", 0x0f);
  print(GT + 1, GL + 2, "Guest List", 0x0b);
  const cast = menuCast();
  for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(7, cast.length); i++) {
    const p = cast[i];
    const full = (p.first + " " + (p.last || "")).trim();
    const nick = p.nick || p.first;
    const line = `${i + 1}. ${full}` + (nick !== p.first ? ` (${nick})` : "");
    print(GT + 3 + i, GL + 2, line.slice(0, GR - GL - 3), 0x07);
  }
  if (ui.houseIdx < 0 && !ui.customCast)
    print(GB - 1, GL + 2, "(house 1 shown; cast follows house)", 0x08);
  centre(21, " PRESS A KEY TO CHOOSE ", 0x70);
  centre(23, "Copyright (C) 1983 by Eric N. Miller and Norland Software", 0x08);
  centre(24, "remastered 2026 · Claude & Jeromai", 0x08);
  render();
}''')

# ============ 5+6. NAME ENTRY: three fields ============
rep('''function drawNames() {
  const n = ui.names;
  clearScreen(0x07);
  centre(3, "P E R S O N A L I Z E D   S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  centre(6, `SUSPECT # ${n.idx + 1}`, 0x0f);
  print(9,  18, "Enter first name:", 0x07);
  print(9,  38, n.field === 0 ? n.buffer + "_" : n.first, n.field === 0 ? 0x70 : 0x07);
  print(11, 18, "Enter last name:", 0x07);
  if (n.field === 1) print(11, 38, n.buffer + "_", 0x70);
  centre(16, "(seven names: six suspects and one victim!)", 0x08);
  centre(18, "Enter accepts · blank keeps the original name · Esc abandons", 0x08);
  if (n.dup) centre(20, "(first names must be distinct — they are how the parser knows who's who)", 0x0c);
  render();
}''',
'''function drawNames() {
  const n = ui.names;
  clearScreen(0x07);
  centre(3, "P E R S O N A L I Z E D   S L E U T H", 0x0f);
  centre(6, `SUSPECT # ${n.idx + 1}`, 0x0f);
  print(9,  18, "Enter first name:", 0x07);
  print(9,  38, n.field === 0 ? n.buffer + "_" : n.first, n.field === 0 ? 0x70 : 0x07);
  print(11, 18, "Enter last name:", 0x07);
  print(11, 38, n.field === 1 ? n.buffer + "_"
              : (n.field > 1 ? (n.last || "—") : ""),
        n.field === 1 ? 0x70 : 0x07);
  print(13, 18, "Enter nickname:", 0x07);
  if (n.field === 2) print(13, 38, n.buffer + "_", 0x70);
  centre(16, "(seven names: six suspects and one victim!)", 0x08);
  centre(17, "Enter accepts · blank keeps the original · a lone space = no last name", 0x08);
  centre(18, "blank nickname = first name · Esc abandons", 0x08);
  if (n.dup) centre(20, "(names must be distinct — they are how the parser knows who's who)", 0x0c);
  render();
}''')

rep('''    if (k === "Enter") {
      const fallback = house().characters[n.idx];
      if (n.field === 0) {
        const cand = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.first;
        // first names are the parser's match keys: they must differ
        if (n.list.some((p) => p.key === cand.toUpperCase())) {
          n.dup = true; n.buffer = "";
          drawNames(); return;
        }
        n.dup = false;
        n.first = cand;
        n.field = 1; n.buffer = "";
      } else {
        const last = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.last;
        n.list.push({ first: n.first, last,
                      key: n.first.toUpperCase() });
        n.idx++; n.field = 0; n.buffer = ""; n.first = "";
        if (n.idx >= 7) {
          ui.customCast = n.list;
          uiEnter("menu");
          return;
        }
      }
      drawNames(); return;
    }''',
'''    if (k === "Enter") {
      const fallback = house().characters[n.idx];
      const taken = (name) => n.list.some((p) =>
        p.key === name.toUpperCase() || p.nickKey === name.toUpperCase());
      if (n.field === 0) {
        const cand = n.buffer.trim() || fallback.first;
        if (taken(cand)) { n.dup = true; n.buffer = ""; drawNames(); return; }
        n.dup = false;
        n.first = cand;
        n.field = 1; n.buffer = "";
      } else if (n.field === 1) {
        // a lone space means: this character has no last name
        n.last = n.buffer.length && !n.buffer.trim() ? ""
               : (n.buffer.trim() || fallback.last);
        n.field = 2; n.buffer = "";
      } else {
        const nick = n.buffer.trim() || n.first;
        if (nick !== n.first && taken(nick)) {
          n.dup = true; n.buffer = ""; drawNames(); return;
        }
        n.dup = false;
        n.list.push({ first: n.first, last: n.last, nick,
                      key: n.first.toUpperCase(),
                      nickKey: nick.toUpperCase() });
        n.idx++; n.field = 0; n.buffer = ""; n.first = ""; n.last = "";
        if (n.idx >= 7) {
          ui.customCast = n.list;
          uiEnter("menu");
          return;
        }
      }
      drawNames(); return;
    }''')

open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("PATCH_COMPLETE")
PATCH_EOF
cat /tmp/p.log
grep -q PATCH_COMPLETE /tmp/p.log && node --check web/engine.js && python3 - << 'EOF' && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth
h = open('web/index.html').read()
h = h.replace('''  #screen {''',
'''  #screen span { padding: 2px 0; }   /* overpaint fractional-scaling
                                        hairlines between text rows */
  #screen {''')
h = h.replace('''    <span>original SLEUTH by Eric N. Miller available at
      <a href="http://www.calcaprt.com/share.htm">calcaprt.com/share.htm</a></span>
  </div>''',
'''  </div>
  <div class="plate">
    <span>original SLEUTH by Eric N. Miller available at
      <a href="http://www.calcaprt.com/share.htm">calcaprt.com/share.htm</a></span>
  </div>''')
open('web/index.html','w').write(h)
print("index.html: hairline fix + footer line")
EOF
Output

data_shared.js: 20 verbs, 18 framings, 41 general elaborations, 7 confessions
data_v1.js: 16 rooms, 17x40 grid
data_v2.js: 17 rooms, 17x40 grid

Thinking · Thinking about context from a previous conversation that isn't available.
The seven-tweak patch landed successfully with the build at 167594 bytes, so now I'm verifying the layout with tests—checking that the title card occupies the right half of the screen with the map visible on the left, the letter rows positioned correctly with proper spacing, the menu options left-aligned with values at column 30 and the guest list showing seven entries with personalized nicknames, and confirming the names section is set up properly. I also need to verify the nickname plumbing works end-to-end: questions and accusations reference the nickname, dialogue uses it correctly, alibi partners are identified by nickname, and the solve logic still functions. The CSS fixes for the span padding should be in both index.html and playable, with the footer link on its own line, and I should run a full win regression test. Before packaging everything up, I need to check if the container reset—if it did, I'll need to restore from the uploads and the m6 zip, though that zip is stale from before this patch. The patched files should still be intact in /home/claude, so I'll verify that first.

Checking workspace survival first — the seven-tweak patch had just landed:

bash_toolVerify the patched state survived the session boundary
bash
cd /home/claude 2>/dev/null && ls web/engine.js >/dev/null 2>&1 && grep -c "nickKey\|Guest List\|lone space" web/engine.js && grep -c "padding: 2px" web/index.html && ls -la web/sleuth_playable.html

Thinking · Verified system components and prepared final packaging.
The engine's intact with the nickname and guest-list code working, the HTML padding is fixed, and the playable version is built—now I'm running through the full verification suite before packaging everything up.

Intact with all patches. Full verification of the seven tweaks:

bash_toolVerify title geometry, menu layout, nicknames, one-word names, win by nick
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 150 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_FAST: 1, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "?seed=4242" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const rows = () => plain().split("\n");
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(0,1000)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

// === 1) title card covers the right half exactly ===
const r3 = rows()[3];
assert(r3.slice(0, 40).includes("█"), "map visible left of the card");
assert(r3[40] === "│" && r3[79] === "│", "card borders at cols 40 and 79");
assert(rows()[1][40] === "┌" && rows()[17][79] === "┘", "card box spans rows 1-17");
assert(r3.includes("SLEUTH  ·  remastered"), "title line inside card");
// === 2) letters shifted down, thin T ===
assert(rows()[18].trim() === "", "blank row 18 between house and letters");
assert(rows()[19].includes("▒"), "letters begin at row 19");
assert(rows()[23].includes("▒"), "letters end at row 23");
// T stem: row 21 (letter middle) — find the T region: letters S L E U T H,
// the T is 5th: check its stem is 2 chars wide
const midRow = rows()[21];
const stems = midRow.match(/▒+/g) || [];
assert(stems.some(s => s.length === 2), "thin T stem (2 chars)");

// === 4) menu: left options, aligned values, guest list ===
key(" ");
const mrows = rows();
const hLine = mrows.findIndex(l => l.includes("(H) House:"));
assert(mrows[hLine].indexOf("(H)") === 4, "options at left margin");
assert(mrows[hLine].indexOf("surprise me") === 30 &&
       mrows[hLine + 1].indexOf("0x07") === 30, "values aligned at col 30");
assert(flat().includes("Guest List"), "guest list panel present");
assert(flat().includes("1. Edward Brisbane") || /1\. \w+ \w+/.test(flat()), "original cast listed");
assert(flat().includes("cast follows house"), "surprise-me footnote");

// === 5+6) three-field personalization: nick, lone-space last ===
key("h");                                     // pin Brisbane
key("s");
assert(flat().includes("Enter nickname"), "nickname field present");
// suspect 1: full three fields
type("Astral"); type("Winterstar"); type("Az");
// suspect 2: one-word name (lone space last), nick defaults to first
type("Iguana"); key(" "); key("Enter"); key("Enter");
// suspect 3: duplicate NICKNAME rejected
type("Vitril"); type("Chrome"); type("Az");
assert(flat().includes("must be distinct"), "duplicate nickname rejected");
key("Enter");                                  // blank nick -> Vitril
for (let i = 4; i <= 7; i++) { type("Guest" + i); type("Tavern"); key("Enter"); }
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu" && flat().includes("personalized"), "cast stored");
assert(flat().includes("Astral Winterstar (Az)"), "guest list shows full name + nick");
assert(/\d\. Iguana\b/.test(flat()) && !flat().includes("Iguana Brisbane"), "one-word name listed cleanly");

// === game: nick used everywhere, parser accepts nick ===
key("a");
assert(D.ui.mode === "game", "game starts");
const az = world.suspects.find(s => s.nick === "Az") ||
           (world.victim.nick === "Az" ? null : null);
if (world.victim.nick !== "Az") {
  const s = world.suspects.find(x => x.nick === "Az");
  assert(s && s.first === "Astral", "Az registered");
  s.room = 1; state.roomId = 1;
  const a1 = D.house().anchors["1"]; state.row = a1[0]; state.col = a1[1];
  let told = false;
  for (let i = 0; i < 12 && !told; i++) { s.room = 1; type("q az"); told = world.alibis[s.key].told; }
  assert(told, "QUESTION by nickname works");
  assert(flat().includes("Az ") && !/Astral (replies|turns|whispers|sneers|says)/.test(flat()), "dialogue uses the nickname");
}
// blank-last victim: intro must not say 'the  estate'
D.execute("restart");
let guard = 0;
while (world.victim.first !== "Iguana" && guard++ < 40) D.execute("restart");
if (world.victim.first === "Iguana") {
  assert(!flat().includes("the  estate") && flat().includes("Iguana estate"),
         "blank-last victim: estate falls back to nickname");
  assert(flat().includes("Iguana was brutally murdered"), "one-word intro clean");
} else console.log("(skip: Iguana never drew victim in 40 restarts)");

// full win regression with custom cast
world.hasGlass = true;
const g = D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.nickKey.toLowerCase());
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "ACCUSE by nickname wins");
console.log("\nALL SEVEN TWEAKS VERIFIED");
EOF
Output
ok: map visible left of the card
ok: card borders at cols 40 and 79
ok: card box spans rows 1-17
ok: title line inside card
ok: blank row 18 between house and letters
ok: letters begin at row 19
ok: letters end at row 23
ok: thin T stem (2 chars)
ok: options at left margin
ok: values aligned at col 30
ok: guest list panel present
ok: original cast listed
                                                                                
                                                                                
                                                                                
        S L E U T H                                                             
                                                                                
                                                                                
    Select the option of your choice:                                           
                                                                                
                                                ┌──────────────────────────────┐
    (A) Start the Game                          │ Guest List                   │
                                                │                              │
    (H) House:                surprise me (rando│)1. Edward Brisbane           │
    (C) Colours:
stderr
FAIL: surprise-me footnote
exit 1
Thinking · Diagnosing layout collisions and missing footnote elements.
I'm seeing the title and menu rendering correctly in green, but there's a critical overlap issue: the House value "surprise me (random)" is 20 characters wide and extends from columns 30-49, which collides with the guest list box that starts at column 48. The footnote for "cast follows house" is also missing—it should appear at row 17, column 50 but isn't showing up in the output. Looking at the footnote problem more closely: the text was written to print at column 50 with 35 characters total, which would extend to column 84—well past the 80-column limit. The print function likely clips at the edge, truncating the footnote to just "(house 1 shown; cast follows h", which means the assertion checking for "cast follows house" fails because the full phrase gets cut off. The fixes are straightforward: shorten the House value labels to eliminate the overlap (either trim "surprise me (random)" down to just "surprise me" or strip the "House N · " prefix entirely), and reposition or shorten the footnote text so it fits within the 80-column boundary.
Thinking · Diagnosed layout conflicts and devised condensing solutions.
I found layout issues with the menu—the "surprise me (random)" option overlaps the guest-list box, and the footnote gets clipped. I'll shorten both to fix the collisions.
bash_toolFix menu collision; rerun full verification
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - << 'EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
old = '''function menuHouseLabel() {
  return ui.houseIdx < 0 ? "surprise me (random)"
       : window.SLEUTH_HOUSES[ui.houseIdx].label;
}'''
new = '''function menuHouseLabel() {
  if (ui.houseIdx < 0) return "surprise me";
  // strip the "House N \u00b7 " prefix and clamp: the value column must
  // not collide with the guest-list panel at col 48
  return window.SLEUTH_HOUSES[ui.houseIdx].label
    .replace(/^House \\d+ \\u00b7 /, "").slice(0, 17);
}'''
assert old in src
src = src.replace(old, new)
old2 = '''  if (ui.houseIdx < 0 && !ui.customCast)
    print(GB - 1, GL + 2, "(house 1 shown; cast follows house)", 0x08);'''
new2 = '''  if (ui.houseIdx < 0 && !ui.customCast)
    print(GB - 1, GL + 2, "(cast follows the house)", 0x08);'''
assert old2 in src
src = src.replace(old2, new2)
open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("menu collision fixed")
EOF
node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null && cd web && timeout 150 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_FAST: 1, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "?seed=4242" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const rows = () => plain().split("\n");
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(0,1100)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };

key(" ");                                       // -> menu
const hLine = rows().findIndex(l => l.includes("(H) House:"));
assert(rows()[hLine].slice(30, 48).includes("surprise me") &&
       rows()[hLine][48] === "│", "house value clear of the guest-list box");
assert(flat().includes("(cast follows the house)"), "surprise-me footnote fits the box");
key("h");
assert(rows()[hLine].slice(30, 48).trim() === "Brisbane estate", "house label stripped of prefix");
assert(flat().includes("1. Edward Brisbane"), "Brisbane cast in the guest list");

// three-field personalization + one-word + nick collision
key("s");
type("Astral"); type("Winterstar"); type("Az");
type("Iguana"); key(" "); key("Enter"); key("Enter");
type("Vitril"); type("Chrome"); type("Az");
assert(flat().includes("must be distinct"), "duplicate nickname rejected");
key("Enter");
for (let i = 4; i <= 7; i++) { type("Guest" + i); type("Tavern"); key("Enter"); }
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu" && flat().includes("personalized"), "cast stored");
assert(flat().includes("Astral Winterstar (Az)"), "guest list shows nick");
assert(/\d\. Iguana │/.test(plain()) || /\d\. Iguana\s*$/m.test(plain()), "one-word name clean");

key("a");
assert(D.ui.mode === "game", "game starts");
if (world.victim.nick !== "Az") {
  const s = world.suspects.find(x => x.nick === "Az");
  s.room = 1; state.roomId = 1;
  const a1 = D.house().anchors["1"]; state.row = a1[0]; state.col = a1[1];
  let told = false;
  for (let i = 0; i < 12 && !told; i++) { s.room = 1; type("q az"); told = world.alibis[s.key].told; }
  assert(told, "QUESTION by nickname");
}
let guard = 0;
while (world.victim.first !== "Iguana" && guard++ < 40) D.execute("restart");
if (world.victim.first === "Iguana") {
  assert(!flat().includes("the  estate") && flat().includes("Iguana estate"),
         "blank-last victim: estate falls back to nickname");
}
world.hasGlass = true;
const g = D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(g[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g"); type("a " + world.murderer.nickKey.toLowerCase());
assert(world.over === "won" && flat().includes("Sleuth!"), "ACCUSE by nickname wins");
console.log("\nALL SEVEN TWEAKS VERIFIED");
EOF
