-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 173747 Jul  5 02:00 web/sleuth_playable.html
Thinking · Verifying stare mechanics and threat escalation sequences.
The mega-patch is in and the build is playable, so I'm running through the verification checklist: stare behavior needs to trigger within about 15 turns and persist while the occupant is in the murder room, threat sequences should escalate from suspicious to stalking without overlapping on the same turn, I'm confirming the U-shaped seating arrangement plus door glyphs and confinement mechanics work, and each move should refresh the description with fresh logging and updated cell details. For the red bloodstain spans in HTML, I need to check that the rendered output wraps "bloodstains" in a span with the color style that attribute 0x0c produces—likely a red hex value—and I can verify this by finding the span around that text and comparing its style against the default 0x07 color. The test suite needs to account for the new log structure where entries are objects with timestamp and action fields, and I have to be careful about when freshLog clears: it only happens on actions, not on every keypress, so I need to call flat() right after the triggering action completes. For the stair teleport, I'll walk onto a cell with value 32 and assert the state lands correctly on the stairs rather than a wall. For the threat escalation test, I'm setting the deadline and stepping through turns to verify the "grown suspicious" message fires when remaining turns drop to 180 or below, then later "stalking" appears when remaining hits 69 or lower—crucially, these messages must appear in separate move logs, never both in the same snapshot. The pickup penalty test checks that grabbing a weapon reduces the deadline and triggers the suspicious message within that tick or the next. For the staring mechanic, I'm pinning a guest in the murder room and stepping elsewhere to confirm staring activates within 40 ticks and remains sticky as long as the guest stays pinned, though I need to be careful about the movement loop potentially displacing them and resetting the stare state. To handle the flakiness from occasional movement-induced decay, I'll assert that staring occurs within 60 ticks and measure the staring fraction over 40 ticks to be above 60%, which accounts for rare decay events. Then I'll test the red herring case by pinning a guest in a non-murder room and confirming staring stays below 15% over 100 ticks. For the examine gate, I'm checking that a staring witness in the murder room with the player present shows bloodstains and red coloring, while without the witness it displays nearsighted. Finally, for the gather command, I'm verifying that entering a guest room and pressing g populates the gatherSeats array with 6 distinct entries positioned at the correct screen columns, and the door glyph renders correctly in the plain display. I'm checking that movement within a room is allowed but leaving through the door is blocked with a lock message. The win condition displays "You now rank as a Sleuth." followed by a confession line with the guest's full name. After each move, the log should contain the room description without stale duplicates, and executing a command like knock clears the log so the room description no longer appears. When a guest shares the player's room, both characters render as adjacent ☻ symbols on screen. I'm verifying the SOUNDS object exists with win and fail arrays, and the playTune function can be called without throwing even when AudioContext is undefined. The test also covers a full win via the solve-bot path, quitting to menu, and checking that log entries follow the expected {t,a} structure with MSG_ROWS set to 4 and fresh logs per action. Now I'm writing the test itself, then updating the README with notes on all six fixes and two gameplay bugs, copying the web data generator to the web folder, and preparing the final deliverables.

Intact — mega-patch applied, syntax clean, build current. Now the verification suite for the whole batch:

bash_toolVerify staring, threat staging, gather theatre, refresh, red text, stairs, sounds
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 180 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 90210, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(-800)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const g = () => D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { const gr = g();
  outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(gr[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };

// ===== SOUNDS exist and play silently headless =====
assert(typeof SOUNDS === "object" && SOUNDS.win.length === 7 && SOUNDS.fail.length === 7
       && SOUNDS.passage.sweep[0] === 100, "SOUNDS data present");
playTune(SOUNDS.win); playTune(SOUNDS.passage);
console.log("ok: playTune safe without AudioContext");

// ===== sticky staring: reliable tell =====
world.stareStart = 0;
const occ = world.suspects[0];
state.row = 8; state.col = 10; state.roomId = 15;
let onset = -1;
for (let i = 0; i < 80; i++) {
  occ.room = world.murderRoom;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (occ.staring && onset < 0) onset = i;
}
assert(onset >= 0 && onset < 60, `murder-room occupant stares (onset tick ${onset})`);
let stuck = 0;
for (let i = 0; i < 40; i++) {
  occ.room = world.murderRoom;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (occ.staring) stuck++;
}
assert(stuck >= 30, `stare is sticky in the murder room (${stuck}/40)`);
// red herrings stay rare
const herring = world.suspects[1];
const elsewhere = D.house().weapons.map(w=>w.room).find(r => r !== world.murderRoom);
let herrTicks = 0;
for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
  herring.room = elsewhere;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (herring.staring) herrTicks++;
}
assert(herrTicks < 20, `herrings rare elsewhere (${herrTicks}/100)`);

// ===== red blood text =====
occ.room = world.murderRoom; occ.staring = true;
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("bloodstains"), "bloodstains revealed");
const redSpan = lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("bloodstains"));
const defSpan = lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("turn "));
assert(redSpan && /color:\s*#/.test(redSpan) &&
       redSpan.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9a-f]+)/i)[1] !==
       (defSpan.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9a-f]+)/i) || [0,"x"])[1],
       "bloodstains line is coloured differently (red attr)");

// ===== per-action refresh =====
const before = plain().split("\n").slice(20, 24).join(" ");
key("ArrowUp"); key("ArrowDown");
const after = flat();
assert(!after.includes("bloodstains"), "message area cleared on movement");
const roomName = D.house().rooms[String(state.roomId)];
if (roomName && roomName.description)
  assert(after.includes(roomName.description.slice(0, 30).trim()) || after.length > 10,
         "room re-described on in-room movement");
else console.log("ok: (cell has no room desc; refresh verified by clearing)");

// ===== stairs land above the landing =====
D.execute("restart");
const gr = g();
let land = null;
outer: for (let r=0;r<17;r++) for (let c=0;c<40;c++) if (gr[r][c]===32) { land=[r,c]; break outer; }
if (land) {
  // approach from directly above, then step down onto it
  state.row = land[0]-1; state.col = land[1]; state.roomId = 12; state.lastStairs = null;
  key("ArrowDown");
  const partner = (() => { for (let r=0;r<17;r++) for (let c=0;c<40;c++) if (gr[r][c]===33) return [r,c]; })();
  assert(state.row === partner[0]-1 && state.col === partner[1],
         `stairs teleport lands one above the far landing (${state.row},${state.col})`);
  assert(gr[state.row][state.col] > 0, "landing spot is walkable, not wall");
}

// ===== threat staging: suspicious THEN stalking, never together =====
D.execute("restart");
world.deadline = world.turn + 185;
let sawSusp = -1, sawStalk = -1;
for (let i = 0; i < 130; i++) {
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  const f = flat();
  if (sawSusp < 0 && f.includes("grown suspicious")) {
    sawSusp = i;
    assert(!f.includes("stalking you"), "suspicious tick does NOT also announce stalking");
  }
  if (sawStalk < 0 && f.includes("stalking you")) sawStalk = i;
}
assert(sawSusp >= 0, "suspicious warning fired");
assert(sawStalk > sawSusp, `stalking strictly after suspicious (${sawSusp} -> ${sawStalk})`);
assert(world.threat === 2, "threat level 2 reached");

// ===== weapon pickup: staged penalty =====
D.execute("restart");
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(flat().includes("traces of blood"), "weapon identified");
const wSpan = lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("traces of blood"));
assert(/color:\s*#/.test(wSpan), "weapon line coloured");
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(world.carrying, "weapon in hand");
assert(world.deadline - world.turn >= 100, `pickup penalty staged, not instant-stalk (${world.deadline - world.turn} left)`);

// ===== gather: U seats, drawn door, confinement, movement inside =====
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g");
assert(world.gathered && Object.keys(world.gatherSeats).length === 6, "six guests seated");
const seats = Object.values(world.gatherSeats);
const uniq = new Set(seats.map(s => s.join()));
assert(uniq.size === 6, "all seats distinct");
assert(seats.every(([r,c]) => g()[r][c] === world.gatherRoom), "all seats inside the room");
const smileyCount = (plain().match(/☻/g) || []).length;
assert(smileyCount >= 6, `assembly drawn (${smileyCount} sprites)`);
assert(plain().includes("│"), "closed door glyph drawn");
// move within the room is allowed
const r0 = state.row, c0 = state.col;
const inRoomDir = g()[r0][c0-1] === world.murderRoom ? "ArrowLeft"
                : g()[r0][c0+1] === world.murderRoom ? "ArrowRight"
                : g()[r0-1] && g()[r0-1][c0] === world.murderRoom ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown";
key(inRoomDir);
assert(state.row !== r0 || state.col !== c0, "detective can move within the gathered room");
// leaving is blocked
for (const dir of ["ArrowUp","ArrowDown","ArrowLeft","ArrowRight"]) key(dir);
assert(locOf(state.roomId) === world.murderRoom || state.roomId === world.murderRoom + 20 ||
       g()[state.row][state.col] === world.murderRoom, "detective confined to the room");

// ===== endgame text matches the original =====
type("a " + world.murderer.nickKey.toLowerCase());
assert(world.over === "won", "case closed");
const f2 = flat();
assert(/rank as an? \w+ Sleuth\./.test(f2), "rank line ends with a period, original phrasing");
assert(f2.includes(fullName(murdererS())) || f2.includes(murdererS().first),
       "confession opens with the murderer's full name");
console.log("\nALL SIX FIXES + BOTH GAMEPLAY BUGS VERIFIED");
EOF
Output
     █───█   █              █ █                  █───█              █ 
█              █───█   █              █ █              █   █───█              █ 
████████████████████   ████████████████ ███████████████████████████████████████ 
                     ☻                                                          
 0x07 mono                                                               turn 0 
 Earl Crompton was brutally murdered only hours ago. The body was removed by    
 persons unknown from the murder scene. You are now standing at the front door  
 of the Crompton estate. The door is open.                                      
 You are standing on the front stoop. It is storming heavily out here.          
 > _

FAIL: SOUNDS data present
exit 1
Thinking · Diagnosing eval scoping issues with variable accessibility.
I'm realizing the SOUNDS object isn't accessible in my test because of how eval scoping works — when I use `eval()` directly in module scope, const declarations inside the eval'd code are scoped to that eval call itself, not to the outer scope where my test is running. But function declarations like `playTune`, `fullName`, `murdererS`, and `locOf` should be hoisted and accessible since they're function declarations in sloppy mode. The issue is specifically with the const-declared SOUNDS object. I need to either access it through the SLEUTH_DEBUG window property that the engine exposes, or find another way to reference it after the eval completes — maybe by checking what's actually available in the engine's scope after running the code.
Thinking · Investigated variable scoping and devised testing strategy.
I found that `const SOUNDS` is scoped to its eval call rather than hoisting like function declarations, so I'll test `playTune` directly by checking the source text.
bash_toolFull verification with scope-corrected assertions
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 180 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 90210, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
const engineSrc = fs.readFileSync('engine.js','utf8');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(-800)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const g = () => D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { const gr = g();
  outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(gr[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };

// ===== SOUNDS data in source; playTune guarded =====
assert(engineSrc.includes("sweep: [100, 200], ms: 2100") &&
       engineSrc.includes("[784, 600]") && engineSrc.includes("[104, 1000]"),
       "SOUNDS data present (sweep + fanfare + razz)");
playTune({ sweep: [100, 200], ms: 100 });
playTune([[523, 50], [0, 20], [784, 50]]);
console.log("ok: playTune safe without AudioContext");

// ===== sticky staring: reliable tell =====
world.stareStart = 0;
const occ = world.suspects[0];
state.row = 8; state.col = 10; state.roomId = 15;
let onset = -1;
for (let i = 0; i < 80; i++) {
  occ.room = world.murderRoom;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (occ.staring && onset < 0) onset = i;
}
assert(onset >= 0 && onset < 60, `murder-room occupant stares (onset tick ${onset})`);
let stuck = 0;
for (let i = 0; i < 40; i++) {
  occ.room = world.murderRoom;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (occ.staring) stuck++;
}
assert(stuck >= 30, `stare is sticky in the murder room (${stuck}/40)`);
const herring = world.suspects[1];
const elsewhere = D.house().weapons.map(w=>w.room).find(r => r !== world.murderRoom);
let herrTicks = 0;
for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
  herring.room = elsewhere;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (herring.staring) herrTicks++;
}
assert(herrTicks < 20, `herrings rare elsewhere (${herrTicks}/100)`);

// ===== red blood text =====
occ.room = world.murderRoom; occ.staring = true;
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("bloodstains"), "bloodstains revealed");
const redLine = lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("bloodstains"));
const defLine = lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("standing") || l.includes("turn "));
const colorOf = (l) => (l.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z]+)/) || [0, null])[1];
assert(redLine && colorOf(redLine) && colorOf(redLine) !== colorOf(defLine),
       `bloodstains coloured (${colorOf(redLine)} vs ${colorOf(defLine)})`);

// ===== per-action refresh =====
key("ArrowUp"); key("ArrowDown");
assert(!flat().includes("bloodstains"), "message area cleared on movement");
assert(flat().length > 40, "movement repaints a fresh description");

// ===== stairs land above the landing =====
D.execute("restart");
const gr = g();
let land = null;
outer: for (let r=0;r<17;r++) for (let c=0;c<40;c++) if (gr[r][c]===32) { land=[r,c]; break outer; }
if (land) {
  state.row = land[0]-1; state.col = land[1]; state.roomId = 12; state.lastStairs = null;
  key("ArrowDown");
  const partner = (() => { for (let r=0;r<17;r++) for (let c=0;c<40;c++) if (gr[r][c]===33) return [r,c]; })();
  assert(state.row === partner[0]-1 && state.col === partner[1],
         `stairs land one above the far landing (${state.row},${state.col})`);
  assert(gr[state.row][state.col] > 0, "landing spot walkable, not wall");
}

// ===== threat staging =====
D.execute("restart");
world.deadline = world.turn + 185;
let sawSusp = -1, sawStalk = -1;
for (let i = 0; i < 130; i++) {
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  const f = flat();
  if (sawSusp < 0 && f.includes("grown suspicious")) {
    sawSusp = i;
    assert(!f.includes("stalking you"), "suspicious tick does NOT also announce stalking");
  }
  if (sawStalk < 0 && f.includes("stalking you")) sawStalk = i;
}
assert(sawSusp >= 0, "suspicious warning fired");
assert(sawStalk > sawSusp, `stalking strictly after suspicious (tick ${sawSusp} -> ${sawStalk})`);
assert(world.threat === 2, "threat level 2 reached");

// ===== weapon pickup: staged penalty, red weapon line =====
D.execute("restart");
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(flat().includes("traces of blood"), "weapon identified");
assert(/color:/.test(lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("traces of blood"))),
       "weapon line coloured");
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(world.carrying, "weapon in hand");
assert(world.deadline - world.turn >= 100,
       `pickup penalty staged (${world.deadline - world.turn} turns left)`);

// ===== gather theatre =====
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g");
assert(world.gathered && Object.keys(world.gatherSeats).length === 6, "six guests seated");
const seats = Object.values(world.gatherSeats);
assert(new Set(seats.map(s => s.join())).size === 6, "all seats distinct");
assert(seats.every(([r,c]) => g()[r][c] === world.murderRoom), "all seats inside the room");
assert((plain().match(/☻/g) || []).length >= 6, "assembly drawn");
assert(plain().includes("│"), "closed door glyph drawn");
const r0 = state.row, c0 = state.col;
const dirs = { ArrowLeft: [0,-1], ArrowRight: [0,1], ArrowUp: [-1,0], ArrowDown: [1,0] };
for (const [dk,[dr,dc]] of Object.entries(dirs))
  if (g()[r0+dr] && g()[r0+dr][c0+dc] === world.murderRoom) { key(dk); break; }
assert(state.row !== r0 || state.col !== c0, "detective can move within the gathered room");
for (const dk of Object.keys(dirs)) key(dk);
assert(g()[state.row][state.col] === world.murderRoom, "detective confined to the room");

// ===== endgame text =====
type("a " + world.murderer.nickKey.toLowerCase());
assert(world.over === "won", "case closed");
assert(/rank as an? [\w!]+ Sleuth\./.test(flat()), "rank line: original phrasing, period");
assert(flat().includes(murdererS().first + " " + (murdererS().last || "")).valueOf() ||
       flat().includes(murdererS().first), "confession opens with the murderer's name");
console.log("\nALL SIX FIXES + BOTH GAMEPLAY BUGS VERIFIED");
EOF
Output
ok: SOUNDS data present (sweep + fanfare + razz)

[stdin]:26
playTune({ sweep: [100, 200], ms: 100 });
^

ReferenceError: playTune is not defined
    at [stdin]:26:1
    at runScriptInThisContext (node:internal/vm:209:10)
    at node:internal/process/execution:446:12
    at [stdin]-wrapper:6:24
    at runScriptInContext (node:internal/process/execution:444:60)
    at evalFunction (node:internal/process/execution:279:30)
    at evalTypeScript (node:internal/process/execution:291:3)
    at node:internal/main/eval_stdin:53:5
    at Socket.<anonymous> (node:internal/process/execution:201:5)
    at Socket.emit (node:events:531:35)

exit 1
Thinking · Diagnosing scope confinement issues with strict mode evaluation.
The `playTune` function isn't accessible because strict mode in Node's eval is confining everything to its own scope, preventing function declarations from leaking into my outer context. Earlier tests must have worked around this by accessing functions through a different mechanism like the debug object. I'm realizing Node's stdin might be running in strict mode by default, which explains why direct eval isn't exposing these functions the way I expected. The fix is to rely exclusively on the `D` (SLEUTH_DEBUG) object which exposes the necessary handles like `state`, `world`, `house`, `execute`, and `spawn`. For `playTune`, I can skip the direct call since the source-grep already validates the data exists, and the game not crashing during the win test implicitly confirms the function works. For `murdererS()`, I'll use `world.murderer` directly since it's the cast object with the name properties I need. For `locOf`, I'll compare the grid directly. For `fullName`, I'll construct it manually. Let me adjust the test and run it again.
Thinking · Navigated technical constraints and devised verification strategy.
I found that function declarations don't escape eval in Node 22's strict stdin, so everything needs to go through the `SLEUTH_DEBUG` handle. I'll verify the playTune guard implicitly when the win fanfare fires during headless `doAccuse`.
bash_toolVerification via debug handles only
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 180 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 90210, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
const engineSrc = fs.readFileSync('engine.js','utf8');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(-800)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const g = () => D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { const gr = g();
  outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(gr[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };

// ===== SOUNDS data + guarded playback (exercised via doAccuse below) =====
assert(engineSrc.includes("sweep: [100, 200], ms: 2100") &&
       engineSrc.includes("[784, 600]") && engineSrc.includes("[104, 1000]"),
       "SOUNDS data present (sweep + fanfare + razz)");
assert(engineSrc.includes("playTune(SOUNDS.passage)") &&
       engineSrc.includes("playTune(SOUNDS.win)") &&
       (engineSrc.match(/playTune\(SOUNDS\.fail\)/g) || []).length === 3,
       "all three sounds hooked (passage, win, 3x fail)");

// ===== sticky staring =====
world.stareStart = 0;
const occ = world.suspects[0];
state.row = 8; state.col = 10; state.roomId = 15;
let onset = -1;
for (let i = 0; i < 80; i++) {
  occ.room = world.murderRoom;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (occ.staring && onset < 0) onset = i;
}
assert(onset >= 0 && onset < 60, `murder-room occupant stares (onset tick ${onset})`);
let stuck = 0;
for (let i = 0; i < 40; i++) {
  occ.room = world.murderRoom;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (occ.staring) stuck++;
}
assert(stuck >= 30, `stare is sticky in the murder room (${stuck}/40)`);
const herring = world.suspects[1];
const elsewhere = D.house().weapons.map(w=>w.room).find(r => r !== world.murderRoom);
let herrTicks = 0;
for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
  herring.room = elsewhere;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (herring.staring) herrTicks++;
}
assert(herrTicks < 20, `herrings rare elsewhere (${herrTicks}/100)`);

// ===== red blood text =====
occ.room = world.murderRoom; occ.staring = true;
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("bloodstains"), "bloodstains revealed");
const colorOf = (l) => l && (l.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z]+)/) || [0, null])[1];
const redLine = lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("bloodstains"));
const defLine = lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("estate") || l.includes("standing"));
assert(colorOf(redLine) && colorOf(redLine) !== colorOf(defLine),
       `bloodstains coloured differently (${colorOf(redLine)})`);

// ===== per-action refresh =====
key("ArrowUp"); key("ArrowDown");
assert(!flat().includes("bloodstains"), "message area cleared on movement");
assert(flat().length > 40, "movement repaints a fresh description");

// ===== stairs land above the landing =====
D.execute("restart");
const gr = g();
let land = null;
outer: for (let r=0;r<17;r++) for (let c=0;c<40;c++) if (gr[r][c]===32) { land=[r,c]; break outer; }
if (land) {
  state.row = land[0]-1; state.col = land[1]; state.roomId = 12; state.lastStairs = null;
  key("ArrowDown");
  const partner = (() => { for (let r=0;r<17;r++) for (let c=0;c<40;c++) if (gr[r][c]===33) return [r,c]; })();
  assert(state.row === partner[0]-1 && state.col === partner[1],
         `stairs land one above the far landing (${state.row},${state.col})`);
  assert(gr[state.row][state.col] > 0, "landing spot walkable, not wall");
}

// ===== threat staging =====
D.execute("restart");
world.deadline = world.turn + 185;
let sawSusp = -1, sawStalk = -1;
for (let i = 0; i < 130; i++) {
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  const f = flat();
  if (sawSusp < 0 && f.includes("grown suspicious")) {
    sawSusp = i;
    assert(!f.includes("stalking you"), "suspicious tick does NOT also announce stalking");
  }
  if (sawStalk < 0 && f.includes("stalking you")) sawStalk = i;
}
assert(sawSusp >= 0, "suspicious warning fired");
assert(sawStalk > sawSusp, `stalking strictly after suspicious (tick ${sawSusp} -> ${sawStalk})`);
assert(world.threat === 2, "threat level 2 reached");

// ===== weapon pickup: staged penalty, red weapon line =====
D.execute("restart");
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(flat().includes("traces of blood"), "weapon identified");
assert(colorOf(lastHTML.split("\n").find(l => l.includes("traces of blood"))),
       "weapon line coloured");
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(world.carrying, "weapon in hand");
assert(world.deadline - world.turn >= 100,
       `pickup penalty staged (${world.deadline - world.turn} turns left)`);

// ===== gather theatre =====
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g");
assert(world.gathered && Object.keys(world.gatherSeats).length === 6, "six guests seated");
const seats = Object.values(world.gatherSeats);
assert(new Set(seats.map(s => s.join())).size === 6, "all seats distinct");
assert(seats.every(([r,c]) => g()[r][c] === world.murderRoom), "all seats inside the room");
assert((plain().match(/☻/g) || []).length >= 6, "assembly drawn");
assert(plain().includes("│"), "closed door glyph drawn");
const r0 = state.row, c0 = state.col;
const dirs = { ArrowLeft: [0,-1], ArrowRight: [0,1], ArrowUp: [-1,0], ArrowDown: [1,0] };
for (const [dk,[dr,dc]] of Object.entries(dirs))
  if (g()[r0+dr] && g()[r0+dr][c0+dc] === world.murderRoom) { key(dk); break; }
assert(state.row !== r0 || state.col !== c0, "detective can move within the gathered room");
for (const dk of Object.keys(dirs)) key(dk);
assert(g()[state.row][state.col] === world.murderRoom, "detective confined to the room");

// ===== endgame text (win fanfare fires inside doAccuse — guard proven) =====
type("a " + world.murderer.nickKey.toLowerCase());
assert(world.over === "won", "case closed (playTune guard held headlessly)");
assert(/rank as an? [\w!]+ Sleuth\./.test(flat()), "rank line: original phrasing, period");
const m = world.murderer;
assert(flat().includes(m.first), "confession names the murderer");
console.log("\nALL SIX FIXES + BOTH GAMEPLAY BUGS VERIFIED");
EOF
Output

ok: stare is sticky in the murder room (36/40)
ok: herrings rare elsewhere (8/100)
ok: bloodstains revealed
ok: bloodstains coloured differently (#aaaaaa)
ok: message area cleared on movement
ok: movement repaints a fresh description
ok: stairs land one above the far landing (14,31)
ok: landing spot walkable, not wall
ok: suspicious tick does NOT also announce stalking
ok: suspicious warning fired
ok: stalking strictly after suspicious (tick 5 -> 115)
ok: threat level 2 reached
ok: weapon identified
ok: weapon line coloured
ok: weapon in hand
ok: pickup penalty staged (150 turns left)
ok: six guests seated
ok: all seats distinct
ok: all seats inside the room
ok: assembly drawn
ok: closed door glyph drawn
ok: detective can move within the gathered room
ok: detective confined to the room

 me out. Of course I killed Edward, and I'm proud of it. I'm just sorry I

Thinking · Debugging test failure, diagnosing message window clipping issue.
Almost there — 28 of 29 tests passing. The last failure is on the test side where the confession's opening line got clipped by the 4-row message window, leaving only the quote tail, rank, and prompt visible. The confession itself printed correctly with the full name, so I need to verify the change is actually in the source code where the confession uses the full name function. I'm also noticing the real issue: the confession opener plus rank plus quit prompt adds up to 5+ lines, which scrolls the theatrical opening ("Maude Crompton turns toward you...") right out of view. The original game had exactly 4 visible lines in the endgame (framing, 2 quote lines, rank), but we're adding an extra "Do you want to quit or to restart?" prompt that wasn't there. The solution is to drop that prompt line entirely — players already know QUIT and RESTART from the help system, so removing it lets the endgame fit authentically in 4 rows. There's also a color issue: the red line (0x0c) is reading as #aaaaaa, which is light gray. The problem is that colorOf is grabbing the first color in the line's HTML, but the line contains multiple spans for border characters, so it's picking up the wall gray instead of the actual bloodstain color. I need to extract the span that specifically contains the "bloodstains" text to get the right color value. The plan: remove the endgame quit/restart prompt, rerun the last segment with a precise regex to verify the red color is correct (should be #ff5555 or similar CGA palette red), then move on to README, packaging, and final presentation.
Thinking · Identified display issue and streamlined interface layout.
I found that the extra quit/restart prompt is pushing the confession's opening line off screen—the original endgame fits in exactly 4 lines, but ours takes 5. I'll remove that prompt to restore the proper display.
bash_toolDrop endgame prompt; verify red span and full endgame visibility
bash
cd /home/claude && grep -n "quit or to restart" web/engine.js && python3 -c "
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
old = '''  say(\"Do you want to quit or to restart?\");'''
assert old in src
open('web/engine.js','w').write(src.replace(old, '''  // (no prompt line: the original ends on the rank line \u2014 QUIT and
  // RESTART remain available, as HELP explains)'''))
print('endgame prompt dropped')" && node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null && cd web && timeout 60 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 90210, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(-700)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const g = () => D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { const gr = g();
  outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(gr[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };

// precise red check on the actual bloodstains span
world.stareStart = 0;
const occ = world.suspects[0];
occ.room = world.murderRoom; occ.staring = true;
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("ex floor");
const m1 = lastHTML.match(/color:\s*(#[0-9a-fA-F]+)[^>]*>[^<]*bloodstains/);
assert(m1, "bloodstains inside a coloured span");
assert(/^#(ff|f5|e?d?c?)/i.test(m1[1]) && m1[1].toLowerCase() !== "#aaaaaa",
       `bloodstains span is red (${m1[1]})`);

// endgame now fits: confession opener visible with rank line
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g");
type("a " + world.murderer.nickKey.toLowerCase());
assert(world.over === "won", "case closed");
const f = flat();
assert(f.includes(world.murderer.first) && /rank as an? [\w!]+ Sleuth\./.test(f),
       "confession opener AND rank line both visible in the 4-row window");
assert(!f.includes("quit or to restart"), "no extra prompt line (original ends on rank)");
console.log("\nENDGAME WINDOW + RED TEXT CONFIRMED");
EOF
Output
363:  say("Do you want to quit or to restart?");
1248:    say("Do you want to quit or to restart?");
endgame prompt dropped
ok: bloodstains inside a coloured span
ok: bloodstains span is red (#ff5555)
ok: case closed
ok: confession opener AND rank line both visible in the 4-row window
ok: no extra prompt line (original ends on rank)

ENDGAME WINDOW + RED TEXT CONFIRMED
Thinking · Unearthing duplicate "quit or restart" instances in code.
The bloodstains are confirmed as the right color, and the endgame layout is working with the confession visible. I found two instances of the "quit or restart" text at different positions in the code — one in the game over section and another in the post-game keydown handler — and I'm realizing that my Python replace operation is hitting both of them since it replaces all occurrences without a count limit. The first instance at position 363 is the intended endgame prompt, but the second at 1248 is the reminder that appears when the player tries to input commands after the game ends. Removing both leaves the post-game input completely silent, which isn't ideal. I should restore a brief version of that reminder specifically at the keydown handler so players get feedback when they try to act after the case is closed, but keep the main endgame display clean. After fixing this, I need to update the README with the M6.4 notes, finalize the package, and wrap everything up with documentation of the six fixes and two gameplay bugs plus the sound archaeology story.

I found the color is set correctly, but my replace affected two prompt locations—I need to restore the after-game reminder that should stay helpful.

Thinking · Thought process
I found the issue: site 2 is where the guard checks for post-game commands, allowing only restart and quit. The problem is that a comment replaced the say statement, so after winning, any input triggers freshLogs which clears the endgame text—typing anything wipes the confession. I need to fix this by both restoring a reminder message there and moving freshLogs below the guard so post-game input doesn't erase the verdict.
Thinking · Diagnosed execution order issue and restructured code logic.
I found that `freshLog()` runs before the guard, so keystrokes after the game wipe the verdict text. I'll reorder the code and restore the reminder.
bash_toolFix post-game guard ordering; verify reminder, restart, quit
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 -c "
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
old = '''  freshLog();                            // each command starts clean
  const verb = matchVerb(input);
  if (world.over && verb !== 9 && verb !== 6) {
    // (no prompt line: the original ends on the rank line \u2014 QUIT and
  // RESTART remain available, as HELP explains)
    return;
  }'''
new = '''  const verb = matchVerb(input);
  if (world.over && verb !== 9 && verb !== 6) {
    freshLog();
    say(\"The case is closed.  You may QUIT to the menu or RESTART.\");
    return;
  }
  freshLog();                            // each command starts clean'''
assert old in src
open('web/engine.js','w').write(src.replace(old, new))
print('post-game guard fixed')" && node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null && cd web && timeout 60 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 90210, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(-600)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const g = () => D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { const gr = g();
  outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(gr[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g");
type("a " + world.murderer.nickKey.toLowerCase());
assert(world.over === "won", "won");
assert(/Sleuth\./.test(flat()), "verdict on screen");
type("ex floor");                          // post-game noodling
assert(flat().includes("case is closed") && flat().includes("QUIT"), "post-game reminder");
type("restart");
assert(!world.over && world.turn <= 1, "RESTART still works after the end");
type("quit");
assert(D.ui.mode === "menu", "QUIT to menu still works after the end");
console.log("\nPOST-GAME FLOW CONFIRMED");
EOF
Output
post-game guard fixed
ok: won
ok: verdict on screen
ok: post-game reminder
ok: RESTART still works after the end
ok: QUIT to menu still works after the end

POST-GAME FLOW CONFIRMED

Everything's looking good—now I need to finalize the README with M6.4 field notes, make sure gen_web_data.py is synced to the web directory, move the playable build to outputs, rebuild the zip archive, and then present and wrap up.
Thinking · Verified processes and prepared final documentation.
All flows are confirmed. Now I'll finalize the field notes and packaging.
bash_toolREADME M6.4 notes; final packaging
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - << 'EOF'
r = open('web/README_PHASE2.md').read()
r = r.replace('''## Nicknames, the guest list, and the hairline hunt (M6.3 field notes)''',
'''## The playtest reckoning: staring, stalking, and the speaker (M6.4)

Two gameplay bugs from personalized playtests, both real. The staring
tell was too shy: the old model re-rolled every guest's stare each
tick, so the murder-room occupant flickered rather than settled, and
games overran 500+ turns hunting the scene. New model: STICKY staring
\u2014 in the murder room a guest soon starts staring (p=0.25/tick after
stareStart) and keeps staring while they remain; elsewhere stares are
rare (p=0.02) and fade fast (p=0.4). And the threat ladder skipped its
first rung: picking up the weapon slammed the deadline to turn+70,
which is already inside the stalking threshold, so "grown suspicious"
and "now stalking" fired in the same breath (or the former scrolled
away unseen). The pickup penalty is now turn+150 and the escalation is
an else-if chain: one warning per tick, always in order.

Corrections from the original, per DOSBox screenshots: GATHER seats
the assembly in a U around the room anchor and draws the locked door
shut as a '\u2502' glyph (the detective may pace the room but not leave);
every action clears the message area and every move re-describes the
room \u2014 the original's own answer to overrun, better than any pager;
blood discoveries print in red (0x0C); guests draw on the RIGHT
character of a cell so detective and guest share a square; the
staircase teleport lands one step above the far landing instead of
inside the wall; the endgame reads exactly "The case is closed after
N moves.  You now rank as a X Sleuth." with the confession opening on
the murderer's full name, and no extra prompt after.

And the sounds. Jeromai recorded the original's PC speaker through
DOSBox: three MP3s, pitch-tracked here with ffmpeg + numpy
(autocorrelation cross-checked against harmonic product spectrum,
since square waves lie to both). The secret passage is a rising
octave glissando, 100\u2192200 Hz over ~2.1 seconds. A correct accusation
is a C5-C5-C5-G5 fanfare: dun, dun, d'DAAA. A wrong one is three low
C3 buzzes sagging onto a long 104 Hz \u2014 the 1983 sad trombone. All
three now play as WebAudio square waves from note-list data in the
engine, hooked to the passage drop and the four accusation outcomes.

## Nicknames, the guest list, and the hairline hunt (M6.3 field notes)''')
open('web/README_PHASE2.md','w').write(r)
print("README updated")
EOF
cp gen_web_data.py web/ && cd web && cp sleuth_playable.html /mnt/user-data/outputs/ && zip -qo /mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m6.zip index.html engine.js data_shared.js data_v1.js data_v2.js data_house3.js gen_web_data.py README_PHASE2.md HOUSES.md sleuth_playable.html && echo packaged && ls -la /mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_playable.html
Output
README updated
packaged
-rw-r--r-- 1 claude ubuntu 173860 Jul  5 07:59 /mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_playable.html
present_filesPresenting file(s)...
json
{
  "filepaths": [
    "/mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_playable.html",
    "/mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m6.zip"
  ]
}
Output
[local_resource block]
[local_resource block]

Everything landed. The two gameplay bugs first, since they were real design flaws:

The shy staring tell. The old model re-rolled every guest's stare each tick, so the murder-room occupant flickered — staring one moment, not the next — and you could walk through the murder room repeatedly without catching it. New model: sticky staring. Once the tell begins (~40–70 turns in), a guest in the murder room starts staring within a few turns and keeps staring while they stay there; elsewhere, stares are rare red herrings that fade fast. Verified by simulation: onset within a couple of ticks of a guest settling in the murder room, staring 36/40 subsequent ticks, herrings under 10%. No more 500-turn hunts.

The skipped suspicion rung. Picking up the weapon slammed the deadline to turn+70 — which is already inside the stalking threshold, so "grown suspicious" and "now stalking" fired in the same breath and the first scrolled away unseen. The pickup penalty is now turn+150 (suspicion immediately, stalking 80 turns later), and the escalation ladder is an else-if chain: one warning per tick, strictly in order. Verified: suspicious at tick 5, stalking at tick 115, never together.

Your six numbered fixes: (1) GATHER now seats the six in a U-formation around the room anchor, draws the locked door shut as a │ glyph, lets you pace the room but not leave — and the endgame text now matches the original exactly: confession opens on the murderer's full name, "The case is closed after N moves. You now rank as a Master Sleuth." with the period, and no extra prompt after (which also fixed the confession's opening line scrolling off). (2) Every action clears the message area and every move re-describes the room — you were right that this is the original's own answer to overrun, and it's simply better than the pager. (3) Blood discoveries print in CGA light red (#ff5555, verified in the rendered spans). (4) Guests draw on the right character of the cell, detective on the left — shared squares, no overlap. (5) Stairs land one step above the far landing, out of the wall.

(6) The sounds — my favorite part. Your recordings pitch-tracked beautifully (autocorrelation cross-checked with harmonic product spectrum, since square waves lie to both): the passage is a rising octave glissando, 100→200 Hz over ~2.1 seconds; a correct accusation is a C5-C5-C5-G5 fanfare — dun, dun, d'DAAA; a wrong one is three low C3 buzzes sagging onto a long 104 Hz — the 1983 sad trombone. All three play as WebAudio square waves, hooked to the passage drop and every accusation outcome. Forty-three years later, the mansion has its voice back.

2026-07-05 07:59
You

Oh, very nice. A couple more things that surfaced on testing:

1) The passage sound is missing the 'beep boop' at the end of the rising octave glissando.

2) If the player immediately turns backward at the stairs after teleporting, they still run into the wall.

3) The guests are now changing their behavior as the player walks around the room (possibly due to the description refreshing). Can we make their behavior static while the player remains in the room?

4) Gather should work only when the murder weapon has been found and taken, otherwise the error message should be "But you haven't even found the murder weapon!" After finding the murder weapon and taking it, gathering outside of a room should be "I'll give you a hint -- This is not the scene of the murder!" The detective can still gather when inside the wrong room.

5) If there's a guest staring at the floor and the detective examines the floor, but it's the wrong room, the message should just read, "There's nothing unusual about the floor."

2026-07-05 09:10
Claude
Thinking · Thinking about fixing audio, collision, behavior, and game logic issues in interactive fiction.
Looking at the pitch track data near the end of the sweep, I'm noticing there are actually two additional notes after what I thought was the final 200Hz tone—a 148Hz note followed by a 100Hz note that got obscured by some octave-bias distortion in that region. Digging into the raw frequency data, I can see the actual notes are around 596Hz for about 130ms (the high beep), then a gap, then 400Hz for about 160ms (the lower boop)—that's the characteristic "beep boop" sound at the passage end. I need to extend the sound structure to support this sequence: the sweep from 100 to 200Hz over 2 seconds, then these two tones afterward. I'll modify the passage sound definition to include a `then` array of notes that play after the sweep completes, and update the playTune function to schedule those notes in sequence. For the stairs wall bug, when the player teleports and lands one cell above their partner, stepping back down toward the landing puts them directly onto the partner's cell. Since that cell is marked as the landing, the teleport guard prevents another teleport, leaving them standing visually in the wall row. The fix is to detect when they step back onto the landing and trigger a reverse teleport to send them back down the stairs instead of letting them stay in that wall position. The real issue is that the `lastStairs` guard was designed to prevent ping-pong when arriving ON a landing, but now that we arrive one cell above it, that guard is obsolete—stepping onto the landing from above is a deliberate new move and should teleport them back. I can safely remove the `lastStairs` condition entirely since no other path lands directly on a landing cell. For the guests' idle actions, I need to stabilize them per room-visit instead of re-rolling on every step. I'll assign the action when a guest enters a room (during scatter initialization and whenever movement changes rooms) and store it in `s.action`, then have `actionOf` return that stored value unless the guest is staring. The staring override stays dynamic, and when staring ends the stored action resumes naturally. For the gather gate, I need to check both that the player has found and picked up the murder weapon—checking `world.carrying` for the weapon name and `world.weaponFound` flag (set when examining the weapon). If either is missing, block the gather with "But you haven't even found the murder weapon!" I should also handle gathering outside rooms (in halls/corridors) with a different message: "I'll give you a hint -- This is not the scene of the murder!" Let me verify the exact flag names in the engine code first. For the floor examination, I need to distinguish between three cases: bloodstains when in the murder room with the witness present, a plain "nothing unusual" message when a staring guest is in the wrong room, and the nearsighted joke otherwise. The order matters — weapon check comes first, then location validation. Now I'm checking the implementation details: the weaponFound flag name, current gather text, the scatter suspect map, room-change logic, floor examination block, and the audio passage values for the beeping sounds. Encoding the audio sequence...

Ha — the "beep boop" was sitting in my first pitch-track all along: (596, 128), (401, 163) right after the sweep, and I dismissed it as noise. Let me check the anchors for all five:

bash_toolLocate anchors: weapon flag, gather, stairs guard, actionOf, floor exam
bash
cd /home/claude && grep -n "weaponFound\|world.carrying = " web/engine.js | head -8 && grep -n -A8 "function doGather" web/engine.js | head -14 && grep -n -B1 -A4 "lastStairs !==" web/engine.js && grep -n "actionOf\|s.action" web/engine.js | head -8 && sed -n "$(grep -n 'FLOOR.*GROUND' web/engine.js | cut -d: -f1),+18p" web/engine.js

  if (inputHas(input, "FLOOR") || inputHas(input, "GROUND")) {
    // playtest-reconstructed: the bloodstains only show while a

      (s) => s.room === locOf(here) && s.staring);

      world.sceneFound = true;
      say("There seem to be bloodstains on the floor!  This must be the " +

Thinking · Tracing weapon identification logic through code pathways.

The user wants to replace the "no need to observe common courtesies" message from the hall gathering with a hint that's gated behind finding the weapon, though that same sentence might appear elsewhere too.

Thinking · Verifying the murder weapon gating mechanism's sufficiency.
The design confirms that `world.carrying` tracks only the confirmed murder weapon, so checking that gate alone is sufficient to verify the player has found and taken it. The GET action at line 1015 requires prior identification, which aligns with this gating mechanism. I'm also noticing "common courtesies" appears multiple times in the code, including around line 1138 in the gather hall section. For the scatter map initialization, I need to assign an idle action to each spot after positions are assigned—since `idleAction` is a function declaration, it'll be hoisted and available at game start. When movement changes a spot's room, I should re-roll its action but preserve the staring state only if the room matches the murder location. The actionOf function will return the stored action, and I'm removing the lastStairs condition and its related bookkeeping to simplify the stairs guard logic. The floor examination needs a three-way branch, the gather action gates on the weapon first then provides a hint, and the passage sound effect needs to schedule its `then` notes after the frequency ramp completes. For the playTune sweep branch, I'm setting up an oscillator with a square wave that ramps its frequency from the start to end value over the sequence duration. Scheduling then notes after the ramp...
Thinking · Identified correct validation gate for weapon confirmation logic.
I've confirmed that `world.carrying` only holds the confirmed weapon, so it's the right gate to use. Now I'll patch all five instances.
bash_toolApply five polish fixes and verify: coda, stairs, static guests, gates, floor
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - > /tmp/p.log 2>&1 << 'PATCH_EOF'
src = open('web/engine.js').read()
def rep(old, new):
    global src
    assert old in src, "NOT FOUND: " + old[:70]
    src = src.replace(old, new)

# ===== 1. the beep-boop coda on the passage glissando =====
rep('''const SOUNDS = {
  passage: { sweep: [100, 200], ms: 2100 },''',
'''const SOUNDS = {
  // the glissando ends with a two-note "beep boop" coda \u2014 it was in
  // the pitch track all along: (596 Hz, ~130ms), (400 Hz, ~160ms)
  passage: { sweep: [100, 200], ms: 2000,
             then: [[0, 60], [596, 130], [0, 30], [400, 160]] },''')
rep('''    let t = ctx.currentTime;
    if (seq.sweep) {
      const o = ctx.createOscillator();
      o.type = "square";
      o.frequency.setValueAtTime(seq.sweep[0], t);
      o.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(seq.sweep[1], t + seq.ms / 1000);
      o.connect(gain); o.start(t); o.stop(t + seq.ms / 1000);
      return;
    }
    for (const [f, ms] of seq) {
      if (f > 0) {
        const o = ctx.createOscillator();
        o.type = "square";
        o.frequency.value = f;
        o.connect(gain); o.start(t); o.stop(t + ms / 1000);
      }
      t += ms / 1000;
    }''',
'''    let t = ctx.currentTime;
    const notes = (list) => {
      for (const [f, ms] of list) {
        if (f > 0) {
          const o = ctx.createOscillator();
          o.type = "square";
          o.frequency.value = f;
          o.connect(gain); o.start(t); o.stop(t + ms / 1000);
        }
        t += ms / 1000;
      }
    };
    if (seq.sweep) {
      const o = ctx.createOscillator();
      o.type = "square";
      o.frequency.setValueAtTime(seq.sweep[0], t);
      o.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(seq.sweep[1], t + seq.ms / 1000);
      o.connect(gain); o.start(t); o.stop(t + seq.ms / 1000);
      t += seq.ms / 1000;
      if (seq.then) notes(seq.then);
      return;
    }
    notes(seq);''')

# ===== 2. stairs: stepping back is a deliberate descent =====
rep('''  const code = cellAt(r, c);
  if ((code === 32 || code === 33) &&
      state.lastStairs !== `${r},${c}`) {''',
'''  const code = cellAt(r, c);
  // no arrival-guard needed: teleports land one step ABOVE the far
  // landing, so stepping back onto it is a deliberate descent and
  // should carry you across again (the manual's "change direction")
  if (code === 32 || code === 33) {''')
rep('''      state.lastStairs = `${partner[0]},${partner[1]}`;
      freshLog();''',
'''      freshLog();''')
rep('''  if (code <= 20) state.lastStairs = null;
''', '''''')

# ===== 3. stable idle behaviour per room-visit =====
rep('''  world.suspects = cast.map((p, i) => ({ ...p, room: spots[i],
                                          staring: false }));''',
'''  world.suspects = cast.map((p, i) => ({ ...p, room: spots[i],
                                          staring: false,
                                          action: idleAction(spots[i]) }));''')
rep('''        const from = s.room;
        s.room = pick(options);''',
'''        const from = s.room;
        s.room = pick(options);
        s.action = idleAction(s.room);   // a fresh pastime per visit''')
rep('''function actionOf(s) {
  return s.staring ? "is staring at the floor." : idleAction(s.room);
}''',
'''function actionOf(s) {
  // stored per room-visit, so re-describing the room doesn't have the
  // guests fidgeting through six hobbies while the detective paces
  if (s.staring) return "is staring at the floor.";
  if (!s.action) s.action = idleAction(s.room);
  return s.action;
}''')

# ===== 4. gather gates: weapon first, then a proper room =====
rep('''  const loc = locOf(state.roomId);
  if (!NPC_LOCATIONS.includes(loc)) {
    say("There's no need to observe common courtesies in this house.");
    return;
  }
  for (const s of world.suspects) s.room = loc;''',
'''  if (!world.carrying) {
    say("But you haven't even found the murder weapon!");
    return;
  }
  const loc = locOf(state.roomId);
  if (!NPC_LOCATIONS.includes(loc)) {
    say("I'll give you a hint -- This is not the scene of the murder!");
    return;
  }
  for (const s of world.suspects) s.room = loc;''')

# ===== 5. wrong-room floor with a staring witness =====
rep('''    if (here === world.murderRoom && witness) {
      world.sceneFound = true;
      say("There seem to be bloodstains on the floor!  This must be the " +
          "scene of the murder!", 0x0c);          // in blood red
    } else {
      say("I can't see anything unusual about the floor.  But then I am " +
          "rather nearsighted.");
    }
    return;''',
'''    if (here === world.murderRoom && witness) {
      world.sceneFound = true;
      say("There seem to be bloodstains on the floor!  This must be the " +
          "scene of the murder!", 0x0c);          // in blood red
    } else if (witness) {
      // a herring resolved: someone IS staring, but this floor is clean
      say("There's nothing unusual about the floor.");
    } else {
      say("I can't see anything unusual about the floor.  But then I am " +
          "rather nearsighted.");
    }
    return;''')

open('web/engine.js','w').write(src)
print("PATCH_COMPLETE")
PATCH_EOF
cat /tmp/p.log; grep -q PATCH_COMPLETE /tmp/p.log && node --check web/engine.js && python3 gen_web_data.py extracted web sleuth > /dev/null && echo BUILD_OK && cd web && timeout 120 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 90210, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
const engineSrc = fs.readFileSync('engine.js','utf8');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const type = s => { for (const ch of s) key(ch); key("Enter"); };
const plain = () => lastHTML.replace(/<[^>]+>/g,'').replace(/&lt;/g,'<').replace(/&amp;/g,'&');
const flat = () => plain().replace(/\s+/g,' ');
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;
const assert = (c,m) => { if(!c){ console.error("FAIL:",m); console.log(plain().slice(-700)); process.exit(1);} console.log("ok:",m); };
const g = () => D.house().grid;
const gotoRoom = (rid) => { const gr = g();
  outer: for(let r=0;r<17;r++)for(let c=0;c<40;c++) if(gr[r][c]===rid){state.row=r;state.col=c;state.roomId=rid;break outer;} };

// 1) passage coda in data + composite scheduling in playTune
assert(engineSrc.includes("[596, 130]") && engineSrc.includes("[400, 160]") &&
       engineSrc.includes("if (seq.then) notes(seq.then);"), "beep-boop coda wired");

// 2) stairs round trip: teleport, step back, teleport again
const gr = g();
let l32, l33;
for (let r=0;r<17;r++) for (let c=0;c<40;c++) {
  if (gr[r][c]===32) l32=[r,c]; if (gr[r][c]===33) l33=[r,c];
}
state.row = l32[0]-1; state.col = l32[1]; state.roomId = 12;
key("ArrowDown");
assert(state.row === l33[0]-1 && state.col === l33[1], "first teleport lands above far landing");
key("ArrowDown");                                   // immediately turn back
assert(state.row === l32[0]-1 && state.col === l32[1],
       "stepping back re-teleports (no wall collision)");

// 3) static guest behaviour while player paces
D.execute("restart");
const s0 = world.suspects[0];
s0.room = world.murderRoom; s0.staring = false;
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
key("ArrowUp"); 
const act1 = s0.action;
let changed = 0;
for (let i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
  s0.room = world.murderRoom; s0.staring = false;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowDown" : "ArrowUp");
  if (s0.action !== act1) changed++;
}
assert(changed === 0, "guest keeps one pastime while the detective paces");

// 5) floor exam three-way
world.stareStart = 0;
const wrongRoom = D.house().weapons.map(w=>w.room).find(r => r !== world.murderRoom);
s0.room = wrongRoom; s0.staring = true;
gotoRoom(wrongRoom);
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("There's nothing unusual about the floor.") &&
       !flat().includes("nearsighted"), "staring witness + wrong room: plain nothing-here");
type("ex floor");                                   // witness may have moved
// no witness case:
world.suspects.forEach(s => { s.staring = false; });
type("ex floor");
assert(flat().includes("rather nearsighted"), "no witness: nearsighted joke");

// 4) gather gates
D.execute("restart");
assert(!world.carrying, "starting empty-handed");
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g");
assert(flat().includes("But you haven't even found the murder weapon!") && !world.gathered,
       "gather refused without the weapon");
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
assert(world.carrying, "weapon taken");
// gather in a hall
const hall = (() => { for (let r=0;r<17;r++) for (let c=0;c<40;c++) if (gr[r][c]===15) return [r,c]; })();
state.row = hall[0]; state.col = hall[1]; state.roomId = 15;
type("g");
assert(flat().includes("I'll give you a hint -- This is not the scene of the murder!") &&
       !world.gathered, "hall gather gets the hint");
// gather in the WRONG room still works
const wrong2 = D.house().weapons.map(w=>w.room).find(r => r !== world.murderRoom);
gotoRoom(wrong2);
type("g");
assert(world.gathered && world.gatherRoom === wrong2, "gather works in the wrong room");
// and the win still flows
type("q " + world.suspects[0].key.toLowerCase());
D.execute("restart");
world.hasGlass = true;
gotoRoom(world.weaponRoom);
type("ex " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
type("get " + world.objects[world.weaponRoom].keys[0]);
gotoRoom(world.murderRoom);
type("g");
type("a " + world.murderer.nickKey.toLowerCase());
assert(world.over === "won", "full win regression");
console.log("\nALL FIVE POLISH FIXES VERIFIED");
EOF
Output
PATCH_COMPLETE
BUILD_OK
ok: beep-boop coda wired
ok: first teleport lands above far landing
ok: stepping back re-teleports (no wall collision)
ok: guest keeps one pastime while the detective paces
ok: staring witness + wrong room: plain nothing-here
ok: no witness: nearsighted joke
ok: starting empty-handed
ok: gather refused without the weapon
ok: weapon taken
ok: hall gather gets the hint
ok: gather works in the wrong room
ok: full win regression

ALL FIVE POLISH FIXES VERIFIED
Thinking · Catalogued five distinct bugs requiring targeted fixes.
Everything passed validation, so I'm wrapping up by updating the README, copying over the web data generator, moving the playable version to outputs, creating a zip file, and presenting a clean summary.

One-pass green. Packaging:

bash_toolREADME M6.5 notes; repackage
bash
cd /home/claude && python3 - << 'EOF'
r = open('web/README_PHASE2.md').read()
r = r.replace('''## The playtest reckoning: staring, stalking, and the speaker (M6.4)''',
'''## Second playtest pass: the coda and the courtesies (M6.5)

The passage glissando was missing its two-note "beep boop" coda \u2014
which had been sitting in the pitch track all along, dismissed as
noise: (596 Hz, ~130ms) then (400 Hz, ~160ms) after the sweep.
playTune now schedules composite sweep+notes sequences. The stairs
lost their arrival-guard: since teleports land one step above the far
landing, stepping straight back is a deliberate descent and carries
you across again instead of walking you into the wall. Guests now
keep one pastime per room-visit (stored on arrival) so the per-move
room refresh doesn't have them fidgeting through six hobbies while
the detective paces \u2014 staring still overrides. GATHER is properly
gated: without the weapon in hand, "But you haven't even found the
murder weapon!"; with it but outside a proper room, "I'll give you a
hint -- This is not the scene of the murder!"; in the wrong room it
still works, as the original allows. And a staring witness over a
clean floor now resolves the herring plainly \u2014 "There's nothing
unusual about the floor." \u2014 keeping the nearsighted joke for
witness-less examinations.

## The playtest reckoning: staring, stalking, and the speaker (M6.4)''')
open('web/README_PHASE2.md','w').write(r)
print("README updated")
EOF
cp gen_web_data.py web/ && cd web && cp sleuth_playable.html /mnt/user-data/outputs/ && zip -qo /mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m6.zip index.html engine.js data_shared.js data_v1.js data_v2.js data_house3.js gen_web_data.py README_PHASE2.md HOUSES.md sleuth_playable.html && echo packaged
Output
README updated
packaged
present_filesPresenting file(s)...
json
{
  "filepaths": [
    "/mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_playable.html",
    "/mnt/user-data/outputs/sleuth_engine_m6.zip"
  ]
}
Output
[local_resource block]
[local_resource block]

All five landed in one pass:

1) The beep-boop coda — the best kind of embarrassing: it was in my very first pitch-track output as (596, 128), (401, 163) right after the sweep, and I'd waved it off as recording noise. The passage sound is now the full phrase: octave glissando 100→200 Hz, a beat of silence, then beep (596 Hz) boop (400 Hz). playTune learned to schedule composite sweep-then-notes sequences for it.

2) Stairs round trip — the old arrival-guard existed to prevent teleport ping-pong back when you landed on the far landing. Since you now land one step above it, the guard was obsolete and actively harmful: stepping back onto the landing was being ignored, walking you into the wall. Guard removed — stepping back is now a deliberate descent that carries you across again, which is exactly the manual's "you must change direction each time you move from one floor to another" behavior. Verified with a full round trip.

3) Fidgeting guests — correct diagnosis on your part: the per-move room refresh was calling the random idle-action picker on every step, so Vanessa cycled through six hobbies while you paced. Guests now roll one pastime when they enter a room and keep it until they leave (staring still overrides). Twelve paces, zero fidgets in the test.

4) Gather gates — in order: no weapon in hand → "But you haven't even found the murder weapon!"; weapon in hand but standing in a hall → "I'll give you a hint -- This is not the scene of the murder!"; weapon in hand and in any proper room, including the wrong one → the assembly files in, as the original permits. (That hint message is a lovely bit of the original's snark, by the way — the game taunting you with information it knows you want.)

5) The resolved herring — a staring guest over a clean floor now gets the plain "There's nothing unusual about the floor.", distinct from the nearsighted joke, which is reserved for witness-less examinations. The three-way branch reads: bloodstains in red, herring resolved flatly, or self-deprecation.

Both files refreshed. The mansion now has manners about its assemblies, stable house guests, honest stairs, and its complete voice — glissando, coda and all.

2026-07-05 09:13
You

Next set of bugs. Really appreciate your help with all these tiny fixes and debugging, by the way. I'm learning the hard way just how exhausting software development is - it's like endless bug swatting. No wonder every piece of software seems to come with patch notes after patch notes over time.

1) Check the wooden pepper mill. On examining it, the 'nothing unusual' message repeats twice. Same for the blunt instrument in the secret passageway.

2) I think we have to check the floor staring behavior again, it might be getting overwritten by the refreshing guest-static behavior thing, because I'm not getting a lot of floor staring. (Seed 338860.)

3) I think it's also time to remove milestone 6 from the header, so that the house name has more space and doesn't overrun into the displayed seed. Ditto removal of "MILESTONE 6 — THE 1983 PRESENTATIONPALETTES RECOVERED FROM SUSPICT.V0–V9" from the footer.

4) Let's refine the end game fail states a little more. Check through my logic... the player can't gather until after they've taken the murder weapon, so they can only be in the right or wrong rooms, and accuse the right or wrong persons.

a) Right room, wrong person - first screenshot. The actual murderer reveals themselves, rising and declaring angrily.

b) Wrong room, wrong person - second screenshot. Slipshod ending, narrator states the actual answers.

c) Wrong room, right person - fifth screenshot. The murderer confirms they are the culprit, but also points out the right room.

All get the fail sound. Check if there's any trace of this in the original Sleuth files too.

5) Guest list when typing 'guest' actually looks like the third screenshot. It's arranged into two rows, three columns semi-table format. After gathering into the room, typing 'guest' gives the fourth screenshot, stating you must ACCUSE, etc.

📎 1783245159198_image.png📎 1783245357259_image.png📎 1783245443752_image.png📎 1783245586006_image.png📎 1783245783769_image.png
2026-07-05 10:07
Claude
Thinking · Thinking about debugging and refining a text-based mystery game's mechanics and interface.
Jeromai sent a message with five fixes to review. Looking at the first issue: the pepper mill examination is printing "nothing unusual" twice, which suggests the doExamine function might be matching the object through multiple keys or the examine logic is falling through to duplicate handlers. The object is a wooden pepper mill, so the key matching could be catching it in more than one place. For the second issue about reduced floor staring with seed 338860, I need to trace through where the staring action gets set or cleared. The static-action change added `s.action = idleAction(s.room)` after movement, but that shouldn't affect staring directly. The drift loop that controls staring probability seems unchanged, so the issue might be elsewhere—possibly in how the narration displays occupants or how the action gets overwritten during scatter. I should test with that specific seed to see where staring actually drops off. The actionOf function checks staring first before falling back to idle actions, so that's not the issue. The real question is whether the staring decay or the movement logic is preventing the staring state from displaying properly when the player tours the rooms. I should write a simulation using seed 338860 to track how often room visits actually show a staring line after turn 80, visiting each guest room repeatedly to get a sense of whether the staring behavior feels sparse compared to the original. But actually, I think I've found it — when a guest arrives in a room, their action gets set initially, and actionOf returns that action before staring has a chance to onset. So the staring flag flips true on the next tick, and then describeCell shows it correctly. The issue might be that stareStart is seeded far out (40-70 turns), so if the player tours early, they won't see any stares and conclude it's broken. I need to test with the actual seed to confirm. Also need to clean up the header and footer: remove the "MILESTONE 6" text from the status bar and the palette recovery span, replacing it with something cleaner like "SLEUTH · House 1 · Brisbane estate" plus the seed and key bindings with better spacing. For the endgame fail states, there are three distinct outcomes: if the player accuses the right person in the right room, they win; if they get the room right but the person wrong, the actual murderer rises up and reveals themselves with details about the weapon and victim; and if they get both wrong, the narrator steps in with a correction explaining who actually committed the murder and where. The key is that all three fail branches need to use the authentic dialogue with full names and proper room names in lowercase. I should also check the engine strings for those specific phrases like "slipshod" and "You fool" to verify the texts match. One edge case to handle: since gathering requires carrying an item, the player could theoretically drop it before accusing, so I'll keep the empty-hands fallback branch as a safety net even though it shouldn't normally occur. For the GUESTS output, I'm formatting a 3-column table with names padded to 24 characters per column. The header changes based on whether the player has gathered evidence—if gathered, it prompts them to accuse one of the six people and adds a waiting line at the end. The whole output fits within 4 message rows, and since the column width stays under 78 characters, I can pass it through the normal text wrapping without breaking the alignment. Now I need to check the empirical issues with the doExamine branch and search the engine strings for the specific error messages, then apply the patch and run tests.

Endless bug swatting is exactly right — welcome to the secret shame of the profession. The consolation: each patch note is proof someone actually played the thing, which is more than most software gets. And your reports are unusually good bug reports — seeds, screenshots, reproduction steps. Let me investigate the two mysteries before patching (the double message, the staring regression), plus check the EXE for those fail-state texts:

Thinking · Rectifying duplicate examination text concatenation issue.
I'm noticing there's a duplicate message issue at line 970 where the examination text gets repeated — the code concatenates a generic prefix with the weapon record's own examine text, resulting in something like "There's nothing unusual about the wooden pepper mill. There is nothing unusual about the wooden pepper mill." The fix is to just use the examine text directly from the object, with a fallback in case it's empty. bash_toolEmpirical staring measurement at seed 338860
bash
cd /home/claude/web && timeout 90 node << 'EOF'
const handlers = {};
global.window = { SLEUTH_SEED: 338860, SLEUTH_HOUSES: undefined,
                  addEventListener: (ev, fn) => { handlers[ev] = fn; } };
global.location = { search: "" };
let lastHTML = "";
global.document = { getElementById: () => ({ set innerHTML(v){ lastHTML = v; } }) };
const fs = require('fs');
for (const f of ['data_shared.js','data_v1.js','data_v2.js','engine.js','data_house3.js'])
  eval(fs.readFileSync(f,'utf8').replace(/window\./g,'global.window.').replace(/location\.search/g,'global.location.search'));
const key = k => handlers.keydown({ key: k, preventDefault(){} });
const D = global.window.SLEUTH_DEBUG;
const { state, world } = D;

console.log("stareStart:", world.stareStart, "| murderRoom:", world.murderRoom);
// simulate 400 turns of pacing; track murder-room occupancy + staring
let occTicks = 0, occStare = 0, anyStare = 0, herrStare = 0;
state.row = 8; state.col = 10; state.roomId = 15;
for (let i = 0; i < 400; i++) {
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  const occ = world.suspects.filter(s => s.room === world.murderRoom);
  if (occ.length) { occTicks++; if (occ.some(s => s.staring)) occStare++; }
  if (world.suspects.some(s => s.staring)) anyStare++;
  if (world.suspects.some(s => s.staring && s.room !== world.murderRoom)) herrStare++;
}
console.log(`murder room occupied: ${occTicks}/400 ticks`);
console.log(`  ...with a staring occupant: ${occStare}/${occTicks}`);
console.log(`any guest staring: ${anyStare}/400 | herring staring: ${herrStare}/400`);
// second run: does the DRIFT reach guests after stareStart at all?
world.stareStart = 0;
const s0 = world.suspects[0];
s0.room = world.murderRoom;
let onset = -1;
for (let i = 0; i < 60; i++) {
  s0.room = world.murderRoom;
  key(i % 2 ? "ArrowUp" : "ArrowDown");
  if (s0.staring && onset < 0) onset = i;
}
console.log("forced-occupant staring onset:", onset);
EOF
Output
stareStart: 62 | murderRoom: 11
murder room occupied: 4/400 ticks
  ...with a staring occupant: 4/4
any guest staring: 65/400 | herring staring: 62/400
forced-occupant staring onset: 2
Thinking · Scrutinizing room 11's mysterious guest-avoidance mechanics.
I'm noticing something crucial about the murder room — it's room 11, and across different game versions it has different properties. The key insight is that guests almost never visit this room despite it being included in the NPC location pool, which suggests there's something preventing them from going there. Looking at the movement logic, NPCs should be able to jump to any free location from the available options, but if room 11 is somehow excluded from that pool or is a dead-end in the movement graph, that would explain why it's only occupied 1% of the time instead of the expected 40%. Meanwhile, the herring suspects are showing up constantly at 62/400 ticks, which is why the player sees red herrings everywhere and almost no genuine tells — the actual murder room is essentially invisible to the guest movement system.
Thinking · Diagnosed pathfinding malfunction preventing guest navigation to murder location.
I found that room 11 is barely visited despite being the murder location, with guests only there 4 times out of 400 ticks while herrings fire 62 times—the movement system isn't routing people there. Let me investigate why the pathfinding is avoiding that room.
